digo: Achili ni nyere, chila mmwenga ana zakpwe
literal_en: Intelligence is like hair, everyone has some
idiomatic_en: Everyone has their own kind of intelligence
swahili: Akili ni kama nywele, kila mmoja ana zake
commentary_en: Used to remind people that everyone possesses some form of intelligence, even if it manifests differently. Said when someone is being dismissed as foolish or when a person's unique skills are being overlooked.
commentary_dg: Inaambwa kpwa kukumbusa atu kpwamba chila mutu ana aina ya achili zakpwe, hata ichikala zinaonekana kpwa njira tofauti. Inaambwa mutu achiberwa ni mzuzu au achili zakpwe zichipuuzwa.
swahili_equivalent: Akili ni kama nywele, kila mmoja ana zake
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu ana aina yake ya akili na uwezo.
commentary_sw: Husemwa ili kukumbusha watu kwamba kila mtu ana aina fulani ya akili, hata kama inajidhihirisha kwa njia tofauti. Husemwa mtu anapodharauliwa kuwa mjinga au ujuzi wake unapopuuzwa.

literal_en: He who is full does not know him who is hungry
idiomatic_en: The satisfied cannot understand the hungry
commentary_en: A rebuke directed at those who lack empathy for the suffering of others. Used when a well-off person fails to understand or acknowledge the hardships of the poor or hungry.
commentary_dg: Ni makaripio kpwa atu ambao taana huruma kpwa mateso ga atu anjine. Inaambwa mutu ariyeneemeka achishindwa kumanya au kutambua tabu za achiya au enye ndzala.
english_equivalent: A full belly does not understand an empty one
idiomatic_sw: Mtu aliyeshiba hawezi kuelewa hali ya mwenye njaa.
commentary_sw: Ni karipio kwa wale ambao hawana huruma kwa mateso ya wengine. Husemwa mtu aliyeneemeka anaposhindwa kuelewa au kutambua shida za maskini au wenye njaa.

literal_en: He who overtakes you is not your companion/friend
idiomatic_en: Those who outpace you are no longer your companions
commentary_en: can be applied to many situations, eg. financial, education
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inaweza kuhenderwa kpwa hali nyinji, dza vira ga mapesa, ga masomo, na ganye. Mutu achikutsupa kpwa uwezo, si myao kare.
idiomatic_sw: Wale wanaokupita hawako tena upande wako.
commentary_sw: Inaweza kutumika katika hali nyingi, k.m. kifedha, kielimu.

literal_en: He who is bereaved is the one who is eaten
idiomatic_en: The one who mourns is the one who must feed others
commentary_en: The bereaved person is expected to feed his guests
commentary_dg: Mutu achiyefwererwa ndiye anayetarajiwa kurisha ajeni. Hata ye mwenye ana utsungu, ni lazima ahendere atu kurya.
idiomatic_sw: Aliyefiwa ndiye anayebeba mzigo wa kulisha wageni.
commentary_sw: Mtu aliyefiwa anatarajiwa kulisha wageni wake.

literal_en: He has become (like) a crocodile pulling (prey) into deep water
idiomatic_en: He lures people into deep waters like a crocodile
swahili: Amekuwa mamba anayevuta watu kina kirefu
commentary_en: Said of one who tempts others by deceitful means
commentary_dg: Husemwa kpwa mutu anayeshawishi atu anjina kpwa udanganyifu, dza mamba achiyevwehera nyama chivwani.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayewadanganya wengine kwa hila ni kama mamba anayevuta mawindo majini.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kwa mtu anayewashawishi wengine kwa udanganyifu.

literal_en: He has wiped himself on a banana tree and is going with his excrement
idiomatic_en: He tried to clean himself but the mess still shows
swahili: Amejisafisha kwa mgomba lakini bado amechafuliwa
commentary_en: i.e. He hasn’t done a good job and is still soiled. Said of one who tries to trick others but is seen through
commentary_dg: Mutu anayejeza kudanganya atu anjina ela anaonekana wazi. Kajihenda vizuri na bado ana mavige gakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Aliyejaribu kujificha lakini bado uchafu wake unaonekana.
commentary_sw: Yaani hajafanya kazi nzuri na bado amechafuliwa. Husemwa kwa mtu anayejaribu kudanganya wengine lakini anaonekana wazi.

literal_en: Savings anre not bad
idiomatic_en: Saving is never a bad idea
commentary_en: Simple but powerful encouragement to save. Used to advise someone who hesitates to put aside even small amounts of money or resources, reminding them that any savings are better than none.
commentary_dg: Ni kuhimiza kpwa urahisi ela kpwa nguvu ya kuika akiba. Inaambwa kpwa kumuhimiza mutu anayesita kuika hata chidide cha pesa au mali, kumukumbusa kpwamba akiba yoyosi ni bora kuriko kukala kutsona.
english_equivalent: A penny saved is a penny earned
idiomatic_sw: Kuweka akiba si jambo baya kamwe.
commentary_sw: Himizo rahisi lakini lenye nguvu la kuweka akiba. Husemwa kumhimiza mtu anayesita kuweka hata kiasi kidogo cha pesa au mali, kumkumbusha kwamba akiba yoyote ni bora kuliko kutokuwa nayo.

literal_en: Savings do not rot
idiomatic_en: Savings never go to waste
commentary_en: Reinforces the virtue of saving by teaching that what is set aside retains its value over time. Often paired with 'Akiba si mbaya' to emphasize the lasting benefit of thrift and foresight.
commentary_dg: Inaimарisa fadhila ya kuika akiba kpwa kufundza kpwamba chitu chirichoikwa kanda chinadumu thamani yakpwe kpwa muda. Mara nyinji inaambwa phamwenga na 'Akiba si mbaya' kpwa kusisitiza faida ya kudumu ya kuika na busara.
idiomatic_sw: Kile ulichoweka kando hakipotei kamwe.
commentary_sw: Inaimarisha fadhila ya kuweka akiba kwa kufundisha kwamba kile kilichowekwa kando kinadumu thamani yake kwa muda. Mara nyingi husemwa pamoja na 'Akiba si mbaya' kusisitiza faida ya kudumu ya uwekaji na busara.

digo: Akosaye rero ni mlume wa muhondo
literal_en: The one who does wrong today is the man of tomorrow
idiomatic_en: Today's wrongdoer is tomorrow's better man
swahili: Aliyekosa leo ni mtu wa kesho
commentary_en: A consoling proverb that teaches forgiveness and the possibility of change. Used to remind people that someone who makes mistakes today may learn from them and become a better person in the future.
commentary_dg: Ndarira ya kuphoza moyo inayofundza msamaha na uwezekano wa kubadilika. Inaambwa kpwa kukumbusa atu kpwamba mutu anayekosa rero anaweza kudzifundza na kukala mutu mnono siku za mbere.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu aliyekosa leo anaweza kuwa mtu bora kesho.
commentary_sw: Methali ya kufariji inayofundisha msamaha na uwezekano wa mabadiliko. Husemwa kukumbusha watu kwamba mtu anayekosa leo anaweza kujifunza na kuwa mtu bora siku za usoni.

literal_en: The one who slaps you teaches you how to fight
idiomatic_en: Your enemy teaches you how to fight
commentary_en: Used also in relation to insults, cunning, deceit etc. You learn by bad example
commentary_dg: Hutumika kpwa mambo ga matusi, ujanja, na udanganyifu. Mutu anadzifundza kpwa mfano mubaya — achiyekubandanya anakufundza kupigana.
english_equivalent: What doesn't kill you makes you stronger
idiomatic_sw: Adui yako ndiye anayekufundisha kupigana.
commentary_sw: Hutumika pia kuhusu matusi, ujanja, udanganyifu n.k. Unajifunza kwa mfano mbaya.

digo: Akuhombaye na mala, kombe zindaoga uhoko
literal_en: He who has sex using his fingers, his nails will wash in vaginal fluids
idiomatic_en: Foolish actions in intimate matters bring shameful consequences upon the doer.
swahili: Aliyekuhomba kwa vidole, kucha zitaoga uchafu
commentary_en: A crude but pointed warning against foolish or improper behaviour in intimate matters. The graphic imagery drives home the message that reckless actions bring shameful consequences upon the doer.
commentary_dg: Onyo kali kpwa tabiya za kizuzu au zisizomudu kpwa mambo ga siri. Picha kali inasisitiza ujumbe kpwamba mahendo ga ovyo ganarehe matokeo ga haya kpwa mutu mwenye kuhenda.
idiomatic_sw: Matendo ya kipuuzi huleta matokeo ya aibu.
commentary_sw: Onyo kali dhidi ya tabia za kipuuzi au zisizofaa katika mambo ya karibu. Picha kali inasisitiza ujumbe kwamba matendo ya ovyo huleta matokeo ya aibu kwa mtendaji mwenyewe.

literal_en: Men infront, men behind
idiomatic_en: True men step up whether the leader is present or not
commentary_en: When a leader is away other men must step in to solve the problem. When the leader returns he will say this proverb because he approves of what they have done in his absence.
commentary_dg: Chilongozi achiphiya kure, alume anjina ni lazima asimame kutatuwa tatizo. Chilongozi achiyouya ndaamba methali ihi kpwa kukubali garigohendwa wakati kayuko.
idiomatic_sw: Wanaume wa kweli husimama wakati kiongozi yupo au hayupo.
commentary_sw: Kiongozi akiondoka, wanaume wengine lazima wasimame kutatua tatizo. Kiongozi akirudi atasema methali hii kwa sababu anakubali walichofanya wakati hayupo.

literal_en: Children don’t carry other children on their backs
idiomatic_en: Children cannot carry each other
commentary_en: cf. The blind can’t lead the blind
commentary_dg: Mwana kamuhimira mwana myawe — ni dza chipofu kuphirikira chipofu. Mutu asiye na uwezo kamuhimira mutu asiye nawo.
idiomatic_sw: Wasio na uzoefu hawawezi kuongozana.
commentary_sw: Linganisha na: Kipofu haongozi kipofu.

literal_en: Piglets suckle each others lips
idiomatic_en: Even piglets help each other
commentary_en: People help one another
commentary_dg: Atu husaidiana, dza vira ana a nguluwe anaoamwana madomo. Hata nyama adzo anadzisaidiya.
idiomatic_sw: Watu husaidiana kama watoto wa nguruwe wanavyonyonyeshana.
commentary_sw: Watu husaidiana.

literal_en: A person who goes to hell/underworld does not return
idiomatic_en: There is no return from the grave
commentary_en: A solemn reminder of the finality of death. Used to counsel people to value life and treat one another well while there is still time, for death is a journey with no return.
commentary_dg: Ukumbusho mzito wa uhakika wa chifo. Inaambwa kpwa kushauri atu kuthamini maisha na kuhendana vinono wakati achere moyo, kpwa sababu chifo ni charo chisichokuwa na kuuya.
idiomatic_sw: Hakuna kurudi toka kaburini.
commentary_sw: Ukumbusho mzito wa uhakika wa kifo. Husemwa kuwashauri watu kuthamini maisha na kutendana vizuri wakati bado wako hai, kwa sababu kifo ni safari isiyo na kurudi.

literal_en: A person who is high up, wait for him below
idiomatic_en: Those who are high up will eventually come down
commentary_en: Pride comes before a fall
commentary_dg: Kiburi hutangulia kugbwa. Mutu ariye dzulu mgodze photsi, mana siku yakpwe ndiyo indakpwedza.
idiomatic_sw: Aliye juu ataanguka siku moja.
commentary_sw: Kiburi hutangulia anguko.

literal_en: A person who is not shy hides his shame
idiomatic_en: A shameless person talks freely about what others would hide
swahili: Asiye na haya hujificha aibu yake
commentary_en: This person talks a lot without caring what other people think, they say things other people would be ashamed to say
commentary_dg: Mutu hiyu husema maneno manji bila kujali atu anjina. Husema mambo ambago atu anjina angeona haya kugasema.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na haya husema mambo ambayo wengine wangeaibika kuyasema.
commentary_sw: Mtu huyu husema mengi bila kujali watu wengine wanafikiri nini, husema mambo ambayo wengine wangeona aibu kuyasema.

digo: Aryaye jashoye kana kondo na mutu
literal_en: He who eats his own sweat has no conflict with anyone
idiomatic_en: He who earns his own living has no quarrel with anyone
swahili: Alaye jasho lake hana ugomvi na mtu
commentary_en: Praises self-reliance and honest labour. A person who earns their livelihood through their own sweat owes nothing to anyone and therefore lives free of conflict and dependency.
commentary_dg: Inasifiwa kudzitegemea na kazi ya halali. Mutu anayedziphatira riziki kpwa jasho rakpwe mwenye kana deni kpwa mutu yeyosi na kpwa hivyo anaishi bila kondo wala kutegemea atu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayejitegemea hana ugomvi na mtu yeyote.
commentary_sw: Inasifa kujitegemea na kazi halali. Mtu anayejipatia riziki kwa jasho lake mwenyewe hadaiwi na mtu yeyote na kwa hivyo anaishi bila ugomvi wala utegemezi.

literal_en: A person who has no child does not know its bitterness
idiomatic_en: Only a parent knows the pain of raising a child
swahili: Asiye na mwana hajui uchungu wake
commentary_en: labour pains
commentary_dg: Methali ihi ni kuhusu utsunguwe wa kuvyala. Asiye na mwana kamanya utsungu wa kumrera na kumvyala.
swahili_equivalent: Asiye na mwana hajui uchungu wake
idiomatic_sw: Ni mzazi peke yake anayejua uchungu wa kulea mtoto.
commentary_sw: Uchungu wa kuzaa.

literal_en: He who has no-one of his own let him cry to God
idiomatic_en: He who has no one should cry to God
swahili: Asiye na wa kwake na amwombe Mungu
commentary_en: A lament for the person who has no family or community to turn to. In Digo society, kinship networks are the primary source of support; without them, one's only recourse is prayer to God.
commentary_dg: Ni maombolezo ga mutu asiye na jamaa au atu a kumtegemea. Kpwa Adigo, ukoo ndiyo chandzo chikulu cha msada; bila hivyo, njira pekee iriyosala ni kumuarira Mlungu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na mtu wa kumsaidia na amtegemee Mungu.
commentary_sw: Ni maombolezo ya mtu asiye na familia au jamii ya kumtegemea. Katika jamii ya Wadigo, ukoo ndio chanzo kikuu cha msaada; bila huo, njia pekee iliyobaki ni kumwomba Mungu.

literal_en: He who doesn’t know how to groan has not yet been in pain
idiomatic_en: You don't know what hardship is until you've experienced it
commentary_en: Applied to situations such as the difficulties of being a breadwinner
commentary_dg: Hutumika kpwa hali dza vira ugumu wa kuwa mutu wa kulisha kaya. Usiyemanya kuhula, kadzangbwe kuumwa kpwa kpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Hujui shida hadi uipitie mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Hutumika katika hali kama ugumu wa kuwa mlezi wa familia.

literal_en: One who doesn’t know meat, don’t take him to the market
idiomatic_en: Don't take a novice where expertise is needed
commentary_en: Advises against placing an inexperienced person in a situation that requires expertise. Just as someone who cannot judge the quality of meat will be cheated at the market, an unprepared person will fail when put in a demanding situation.
commentary_dg: Inashauri kpwa kukahaza kumuika mutu asiye na uzoefu kpwa hali inayolonda utaalamu. Dza viratu mutu asiyemanya nyama andadanganywa sokoni, mutu asiyetengezwa andashindwa kpwa hali ngumu.
idiomatic_sw: Usimpeleke mjinga mahali ambapo utaalamu unahitajika.
commentary_sw: Inashauri dhidi ya kumweka mtu asiye na uzoefu katika hali inayohitaji utaalamu. Kama vile mtu asiyejua nyama atadanganywa sokoni, mtu asiyeandaliwa atashindwa katika hali ngumu.

literal_en: Instead of Hassan it is Hussein
idiomatic_en: One replaces another — nothing really changes
commentary_en: An observation about superficial change. When one person is replaced by another of the same kind, nothing truly changes. The names Hassan and Hussein, being similar, underscore the point that the substitution is meaningless.
commentary_dg: Ndarira inaonesa kpwamba kubadilika kpwa dzulu-dzulu tana maana. Mutu mmwenga achirichibadilishwa na mutu wa aina iyo-iyo, takuna chinachobadilika kpweli. Madzina Hassani na Husseini, kpwa kufanana kwao, ganasisitiza kpwamba kubadilishana ni kpwa bure.
idiomatic_sw: Mmoja anaondoka, mwingine anachukua nafasi yake — hakuna kinachobadilika kweli.
commentary_sw: Uchunguzi kuhusu mabadiliko ya juu juu. Mtu mmoja anapobadilishwa na mwingine wa aina hiyo hiyo, hakuna kinachobadilika kweli. Majina Hassan na Hussein, kwa kufanana kwake, yanasisitiza kwamba ubadilishaji ni wa bure.

literal_en: The sea has no friend
idiomatic_en: The sea is no one's friend
commentary_en: A warning from the coastal Digo community about the dangers of the sea. The ocean is powerful and indifferent; it does not spare anyone. Fishermen and seafarers are reminded never to take the sea for granted.
commentary_dg: Onyo kula kpwa Adigo a pwani kuhusu hatari za bahari. Bahari ina nguvu na taina huruma; taihurumii mutu yeyosi. Avuvi na asafiri a baharini ganakumbuswa kutsokuhala bahari kpwa urahisi.
idiomatic_sw: Bahari ni hatari na haichagui rafiki.
commentary_sw: Onyo kutoka jamii ya Wadigo wa pwani kuhusu hatari za bahari. Bahari ni yenye nguvu na haina huruma; haimhurumii mtu yeyote. Wavuvi na wasafiri wa baharini wanakumbushwa kutokuichukulia bahari kirahisi.

literal_en: Get, eat a little, put aside a big part
idiomatic_en: Earn a little, save a lot
commentary_en: eg. when you get your salary, don’t spend it all at once, save some and make it last
commentary_dg: Ukiphaha mushahara wako, usiumale kpwa siku mwenga. Tika rya chidide, chinji chiiche — ugalavye polo polo.
idiomatic_sw: Pata kidogo, tumia kidogo, weka mengi kando.
commentary_sw: K.m. unapopata mshahara, usitumie wote mara moja, weka akiba na uidumishe.

literal_en: Bit-by-bit finishes chopping the log
idiomatic_en: Little by little, even a log gets chopped
commentary_en: Encourages perseverance and steady effort. Even a large task can be completed through small, consistent steps. Used to motivate someone who feels overwhelmed by the size of a task or challenge.
commentary_dg: Inahimiza uvumilivu na juhudi za pole-pole. Hata kazi kulu inaweza kumala kpwa hatua ndide za kudumu. Inaambwa kpwa kumuphoza moyo mutu anayeshindwa ni ukulu wa kazi au changamoto.
english_equivalent: Slow and steady wins the race
idiomatic_sw: Kidogo kidogo hujaza kibaba na kazi yoyote inawezekana kwa uvumilivu.
commentary_sw: Inahimiza uvumilivu na juhudi za polepole. Hata kazi kubwa inaweza kukamilishwa kwa hatua ndogo za kudumu. Husemwa kumtia moyo mtu anayeshindwa na ukubwa wa kazi au changamoto.

literal_en: A free (gift) is not eaten, yesterday is not today
idiomatic_en: Yesterday's free gift doesn't guarantee today's
swahili: Bure ya jana si ya leo
commentary_en: Even if you got something for nothing yesterday, don’t expect the same today
commentary_dg: Hata ukiphaha chitu bure dzana, usitarajie hivyo hivyo rero. Dzana kala ni dzana, rero ni rero.
idiomatic_sw: Usitegemee kupewa bure leo kwa sababu ulipewa bure jana.
commentary_sw: Hata kama ulipata kitu bure jana, usitegemee hivyo hivyo leo.

literal_en: An empty house cannot be asked for fire
idiomatic_en: You cannot ask for fire from an empty house
commentary_en: An empty house will have nothing, no cooking will be done there, therefore no fire.
commentary_dg: Nyumba bweni taina chitu. Hakuna mutu ndayeika moho hamo, kpwa hivyo moho tauwezekana kuvoywa.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kupata kitu kutoka kwa mtu asiye na chochote.
commentary_sw: Nyumba tupu haitakuwa na kitu, hakuna kupika kutafanywa hapo, kwa hivyo hakuna moto.

literal_en: The (food) given does not have left- overs/scrapings
idiomatic_en: When you are given food, don't ask for the scrapings too
commentary_en: If you are given what you need, you should not expect more. The cook is the one who controls the pot and decides who gets what, the leftovers belong to her.
commentary_dg: Ukihewa unachohitaji, usitarajie zaidi. Mpishi ndiye anayetawala chungu — mabaki ni ga mpishi mwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Ukipewa kitu, usitarajie kupata zaidi.
commentary_sw: Ukipewa unachohitaji, usitarajie zaidi. Mpishi ndiye anayetawala chungu na kuamua nani anapata nini; mabaki ni yake.

literal_en: The poor person’s thing does not rot
idiomatic_en: The poor use everything they have immediately
commentary_en: The poor person uses what he has and doesn’t store it
commentary_dg: Mchiya hutumia chochosi arichonacho mara mwenga, kahendere akiba. Chachikpwe tachivunda mana chinarya kare.
idiomatic_sw: Maskini hutumia alichonacho mara moja kwa sababu hana uwezo wa kuweka akiba.
commentary_sw: Maskini hutumia alichonacho na haweki akiba.

literal_en: The poor person’s thing does not give birth to a calf.
idiomatic_en: A poor person's possessions never multiply
commentary_en: Laments the disadvantage of poverty. A poor person's possessions do not grow or multiply the way a rich person's investments do. The cow imagery reflects an agrarian society where livestock was a measure of wealth.
commentary_dg: Inaomboleza hasara ya uchiya. Mali ya mchiya taikula wala kuzvyala faida dza uwekezaji wa tajiri. Picha ya ng'ombe inaonesa jamii ya ulimizi ambapho mifugo kala ni chipimo cha utajiri.
idiomatic_sw: Mali ya maskini haikui wala kuzaa faida.
commentary_sw: Inaomboleza hasara ya umaskini. Mali ya maskini haikui wala kuzaa faida kama uwekezaji wa tajiri. Picha ya ng'ombe inaonyesha jamii ya kilimo ambapo mifugo ilikuwa kipimo cha utajiri.

literal_en: The short person’s thing is eaten by the long person’s
idiomatic_en: The short-sighted person's gains are taken by the far-sighted one
commentary_en: A person who doesn’t plan ahead and lacks intelligence will be outwitted by a cleverer person, who can think ahead.
commentary_dg: Mutu asiye na achili ya mbere ndashindwa ni mutu mwerevu. Ariye mure anaona kure, mfupi kaona mbere.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na mpango atashindwa na mwenye akili zaidi.
commentary_sw: Mtu asiye na mpango wa mbele na asiye na akili atashindwa na mtu mwerevu zaidi anayeweza kufikiri mbele.

literal_en: The drunkard’s thing is eaten by the palm wine tapper
idiomatic_en: The drunkard's wealth goes to the one who serves him drink
swahili: Cha mlevi huliwa na mwenye pombe
commentary_en: Observes that the drunkard's earnings inevitably end up in the hands of the palm wine tapper. Among the coastal Digo, palm wine (tembo) is a central social drink, and this proverb warns that addiction enriches the supplier at the drinker's expense.
commentary_dg: Inaonesa kpwamba mapato ga mlevi mwishowe ganamfikira mjema. Kpwa Adigo a pwani, tembo ni chinywadzi chikulu cha jamii, na ndarira ihi inaonya kpwamba ulevi unamunufaisha muuzaji kpwa hasara ya munywadzi.
idiomatic_sw: Mali ya mlevi humilikiwa na muuzaji wa pombe.
commentary_sw: Inaona kwamba mapato ya mlevi hatimaye huishia mikononi mwa mtema mnazi. Miongoni mwa Wadigo wa pwani, tembo ni kinywaji kikuu cha jamii, na methali hii inaonya kwamba uraibu unamnufaisha muuzaji kwa hasara ya mnywaji.

literal_en: The weak person’s is destroyed by the wind
idiomatic_en: The weak person's efforts are easily destroyed
commentary_en: The wind represents a powerful person or force. A weak person's efforts or possessions can easily be swept away by those with more power. It speaks to the vulnerability of the powerless in the face of the strong.
commentary_dg: Kusi inawakilisha mutu mwenye nguvu au uwezo mkpwulu. Juhudi au mali ya mutu mnyonje inaweza kuphotezwa kpwa urahisi ni enye nguvu zaidi. Inaonesa unyonje wa asiye na uwezo mbere za enye nguvu.
idiomatic_sw: Kazi ya mtu dhaifu huharibiwa kwa urahisi.
commentary_sw: Upepo unawakilisha mtu mwenye nguvu au nguvu kubwa. Juhudi au mali ya mtu dhaifu inaweza kupotezwa kwa urahisi na wenye nguvu zaidi. Inazungumzia udhaifu wa asiye na uwezo mbele ya wenye nguvu.

idiomatic_en: A fool's possessions are consumed for free
commentary_en: A fool's resources are consumed without return because the fool lacks the discernment to protect them. Others take advantage of a foolish person's generosity or carelessness, eating freely from what the fool has without offering anything back.
commentary_dg: Mali ya mzuzu inatumika bila faida kpwa sababu mzuzu kana busara ya kuilinda. Atu anjine ganahala fursa ya ukarimu au uzembe wa mzuzu, achirya bure kula kpwa aricho nacho bila kumuuyizira chochosi.
idiomatic_sw: Mali ya mpumbavu hutumika bila faida yoyote.
commentary_sw: Mali ya mzuzu hutumika bila faida kwa sababu mjinga hana busara ya kuilinda. Wengine huchukua fursa ya ukarimu au uzembe wa mjinga, wakila bure kutoka kwa alichonacho bila kumrudishia chochote.

literal_en: An empty finger is not licked
idiomatic_en: No one licks a finger that has nothing on it
commentary_en: Nobody pays attention to a person who has nothing to offer. Just as no one licks a finger that has no food on it, people will not seek out someone who cannot provide benefit or value.
commentary_dg: Takuna mutu anayemjali mutu ambaye kana chochosi cha kulavya. Dza viratu takuna mutu anayeramba chala ambacho tachina chakurya, atu taandamulonda mutu ambaye kaweza kurehe faida au thamani.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na chochote cha kutoa hatafuatwa na mtu yeyote.
commentary_sw: Hakuna mtu anayemjali mtu ambaye hana chochote cha kutoa. Kama vile hakuna mtu anayeramba kidole ambacho hakina chakula, watu hawatamtafuta mtu ambaye hawezi kuleta faida au thamani.

literal_en: One finger does not crush a louse
idiomatic_en: You cannot accomplish anything alone
commentary_en: Stresses the necessity of cooperation. Just as a single finger cannot crush a louse — you need at least two — no person can succeed entirely alone. Used to encourage communal effort and mutual support.
commentary_dg: Inasisitiza umuhimu wa ushirikiano. Dza viratu chala chimwenga tachiweza kubanda tsaha — unalonda angalau mbiri — takuna mutu anayeweza kufanikiwa macheye kabisa. Inaambwa kpwa kuhimiza kazi ya phamwenga na msada wa pande zosi.
idiomatic_sw: Hakuna anayeweza kufanya kazi kubwa peke yake.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza umuhimu wa ushirikiano. Kama vile kidole kimoja hakiwezi kuvunja chawa — unahitaji angalau viwili — hakuna mtu anayeweza kufanikiwa peke yake kabisa. Husemwa kuhimiza kazi ya pamoja na msaada wa pande zote.

literal_en: The good finger is trapped in a ring
idiomatic_en: Good qualities can become a trap
commentary_en: Warns that one's good qualities can become a burden. The best finger is chosen for the ring, but it is then trapped by it. Similarly, a capable or generous person may find themselves bound by the expectations others place upon them.
commentary_dg: Inaonya kpwamba sifa nono za mutu zinaweza kukala mzigo. Chala chinono chinatsagulwa kpwa pete, ela chinafungwa nayo. Vivyo hivyo, mutu mwenye uwezo au ukarimu anaweza kudzikuta akafungwa ni matarajio ga atu anjine.
idiomatic_sw: Ubora wako unaweza kukufunga katika majukumu.
commentary_sw: Inaonya kwamba sifa nzuri za mtu zinaweza kuwa mzigo. Kidole kizuri huchaguliwa kwa pete, lakini kinafungwa nayo. Vivyo hivyo, mtu mwenye uwezo au ukarimu anaweza kujikuta amefungwa na matarajio ya wengine.

literal_en: A finger does not point at a forest
idiomatic_en: Don't boast about what you haven't yet accomplished
commentary_en: You point at a forest when you want to farm there. Don't boast about the field you have there until you have actually cleared the field.
commentary_dg: Unaonyesha tsaka unaphotaka kurima huko. Usdzisifu kuhusu munda wako hadi ukale ukamaliza kusafisha.
idiomatic_sw: Usijisifu kwa kitu ambacho bado hujafanya.
commentary_sw: Unaonyesha msitu unapotaka kulima huko. Usijisifu kuhusu shamba lako huko hadi kweli umesafisha shamba.

literal_en: Tomorrow's journey is prepared for today.
idiomatic_en: Prepare today for tomorrow's journey
commentary_en: Emphasizes the importance of preparation and foresight. Whatever you plan to do tomorrow must be prepared for today. Used to counsel against procrastination and to encourage planning ahead.
commentary_dg: Inasisitiza umuhimu wa matengenezo na busara ya mbere. Chochosi unachopanga kuhenda muhondo ni lazima chitengezwe rero. Inaambwa kpwa kuonya kpwa kukahaza kusukumiza mambo na kuhimiza kupanga kare.
idiomatic_sw: Maandalizi ya mapema ndiyo msingi wa mafanikio ya kesho.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza umuhimu wa maandalizi na busara ya mbele. Chochote unachopanga kufanya kesho lazima kiandaliwe leo. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya kusogeza mambo na kuhimiza kupanga mapema.

literal_en: If a tackle defeats you while you are standing up you won’t be able (to win) while you are sitting either
idiomatic_en: If you cannot handle challenges while strong, you will fare worse when weak
commentary_en: If you are defeated by things while you have strength (health, money etc.) you will be even more defeated by them when your strength is gone
commentary_dg: Ukishindwa ni mambo wakati una nguvu — afya, mapesa — ndashindwa zaidi wakati nguvu zako zisiphokala ziko.
idiomatic_sw: Ushindwao na mambo ukiwa na nguvu, utashindwa zaidi nguvu zikiisha.
commentary_sw: Ukishindwa na mambo wakati una nguvu (afya, pesa n.k.) utashindwa zaidi wakati nguvu zako zitakapoisha.

literal_en: Delayed you shall arrive
idiomatic_en: Better late than never
swahili: Chelewa chelewa, utapata mwana si wako
commentary_en: Encourages perseverance even when one is slow or delayed. It is better to arrive late than not to arrive at all. Said to comfort someone who feels they are falling behind or to motivate someone who has paused midway.
commentary_dg: Inahimiza uvumilivu hata mutu achichelewa. Ni bora kufika kpwa kuchelewa kuriko kutsokufika kabisa. Inaambwa kpwa kumuphoza moyo mutu anayeona akachelewa au kumuphoza moyo mutu ariyeima kahi-kahi ya njira.
idiomatic_sw: Heri kuchelewa kuliko kutofika kabisa.
commentary_sw: Inahimiza uvumilivu hata mtu akichelewa. Ni bora kufika kwa kuchelewa kuliko kutofika kabisa. Husemwa kumfariji mtu anayehisi amechelewa au kumtia moyo mtu aliyesimama katikati ya njia.

literal_en: The market has fallen on the snake
idiomatic_en: When trouble strikes, everyone scatters
commentary_en: Describes the chaos and panic that erupts when danger suddenly appears in a public place. The market is a place of normal activity; when a snake appears there, order collapses and everyone scatters.
commentary_dg: Inaeleza fujo na woga unaokpwedza hatari ichidzitokeza gafula phatu pha atu. Chete ni phatu pha shughuli za kawaida; nyoka ichidzitokeza hipho, utaratibu unavundzika na chila mutu anachimbira.
idiomatic_sw: Matatizo yakitokea, watu hukimbia kila upande.
commentary_sw: Inaelezea fujo na hofu inayotokea hatari inapojitokeza ghafla mahali pa umma. Soko ni mahali pa shughuli za kawaida; nyoka anapojitokeza hapo, utaratibu unavunjika na kila mtu anakimbia.

digo: Chibaya ni chako, chizuri ni cha mayo
literal_en: Bad things are yours, good things are your mother’s
idiomatic_en: People take credit for the good but blame you for the bad
swahili: Kibaya ni chako, kizuri ni cha mama
commentary_en: Observes the injustice of how credit and blame are distributed. When things go well, others — especially family — claim the success. When things go wrong, the individual bears the blame alone.
commentary_dg: Inaonesa udhalimu wa jinsi sifa na malaumo ganavyoganywa. Mambo gachihenda vinono, atu anjine — haswa jamaa — ganadai mafanikio. Mambo gachihenda vibaya, mutu anatsukula malaumo macheye.
idiomatic_sw: Watu huchukua sifa kwa mazuri na kukuachia lawama kwa mabaya.
commentary_sw: Inaona udhalimu wa jinsi sifa na lawama zinavyogawanywa. Mambo yakienda vizuri, wengine — hasa familia — wanadai mafanikio. Mambo yakienda vibaya, mtu anabeba lawama peke yake.

literal_en: The goat out in front breaks his horn
idiomatic_en: The one who rushes ahead often gets hurt first
swahili: Mbuzi wa mbele mbele huvunjwa pembe
commentary_en: Someone who rushes into things or is always the first to speak up will end up in trouble
commentary_dg: Mutu anayeharakisha mambo au kuwa wa kpwandza kusema ndadziora kpwa matatizo. Chibuzi cha mbere-mbere ndiye wa kpwandza kuvundzwa liphembe.
idiomatic_sw: Anayeharakisha kupita mbele hupatwa na matatizo kwanza.
commentary_sw: Mtu anayeharakisha mambo au daima kuwa wa kwanza kusema atajipata katika matatizo.

digo: Chichigbwirwa china uriziwe na chichirichirwa china uriziwe
literal_en: The thing that you hold onto has its own cry and the thing that you leave has its own cry
idiomatic_en: Whatever you choose has its own complaints — you cannot win either way
swahili: Ukishika kina kilio, ukiacha kina kilio
commentary_en: A child who is punished will tell you it’s not fair, but a child who is not punished will not respect you. ie. you can’t win!
commentary_dg: Mwana uchiyemuadhibu ndaamba si haki, ela mwana usiyemuadhibu hatakuheshimu. Kpwa hivyo huwezi kushinda — chila chitu china uriziwe chakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kushinda kwa njia yoyote — kila uamuzi una matokeo yake.
commentary_sw: Mtoto anayeadhibiwa atasema si haki, lakini mtoto asiyeadhibiwa hatakuheshimu. Yaani huwezi kushinda!

digo: Chichimendzwa ni Mwanasiti, Chamkpwaya kana rakpwe
literal_en: Whatever is loved by Mwanasiti, Chamkpwaya doesn’t have a choice
idiomatic_en: A loving husband cannot refuse what his wife desires
swahili: Kilichopendwa na Mwanasiti, Chamkpwaya hana la kusema
commentary_en: Whatever the wife (Mwanasiti) wants, the husband (Chamkpwaya) has to buy, because he loves her.
commentary_dg: Mlume (Chamkpwaya) achimendza mkpwaziwe (Mwanasiti), chochosi achichimendzwa ndachimgulira. Mapendzi ganauhimira mutu kuhenda kpwa moyo.
idiomatic_sw: Mume anayempenda mkewe hawezi kukataa kile anachokitaka.
commentary_sw: Chochote mke (Mwanasiti) anachotaka, mume (Chamkpwaya) lazima anunue, kwa sababu anampenda.

idiomatic_en: The coastal Digo world and the islands are bound by ties that run deeper than the sea between them.
commentary_en: An obscure proverb referencing the connection between the Digo coast and the island of Pemba. It likely speaks to trade, migration, or cultural ties between mainland coastal communities and the islands of the Swahili world.
commentary_dg: Ndarira ambayo taimanyikana vinono inayoeleza uhusiano wa kahi-kahi ya pwani ya Adigo na chisiwa cha Pemba. Inaelekea kuonesa biashara, uhamisho, au mahusiano ga kitamaduni kahi-kahi ya atu a pwani na visiwa vya dunia ya Chiswahili.
idiomatic_sw: Methali hii inahusu uhusiano kati ya sehemu mbalimbali za pwani.
commentary_sw: Methali isiyoeleweka vizuri inayorejelea uhusiano kati ya pwani ya Wadigo na kisiwa cha Pemba. Inaelekea kuzungumzia biashara, uhamaji, au mahusiano ya kitamaduni kati ya jamii za pwani na visiwa vya ulimwengu wa Kiswahili.

digo: Chigumba kpwa nguluwe kpwa Mdigo chi utsungu
idiomatic_en: What a pig finds sweet, a Digo person finds bitter — what suits one may not suit another.
commentary_en: What is palatable or tolerable for one creature may be bitter for another. Among the Digo, this proverb draws a distinction between the pig — which eats anything — and the Digo person, for whom the same thing may be unacceptable or painful.
commentary_dg: Chinachofaa au kuvumilika kpwa chiumbe chimwenga chinaweza kukala chi utsungu kpwa chinjine. Kpwa Adigo, ndarira ihi inaonesa tofauti kahi-kahi ya nguluwe — ariyerya chila chitu — na Mdigo, ambaye chitu hicho-hicho chinaweza kukala chisichokubalika au chenye utsungu.
idiomatic_sw: Kinachofaa kwa mnyama hakifai kwa binadamu.
commentary_sw: Kinachofaa au kuvumilika kwa kiumbe kimoja kinaweza kuwa kichungu kwa kingine. Miongoni mwa Wadigo, methali hii inabainisha tofauti kati ya nguruwe — anayekula kila kitu — na Mdigo, ambaye kitu hicho hicho kinaweza kuwa kisichokubalika au chenye uchungu.

digo: Chigongo cha mgayi chaangulwa ni kusi
literal_en: A poor person’s stick is destroyed by the wind
idiomatic_en: The poor person's possessions are destroyed by the powerful
swahili: Fimbo ya maskini huangushwa na upepo
commentary_en: The wind represents the ‘big man’. For a poor person to succeed they need the patronage of the big man, otherwise they will fail.
commentary_dg: Kusi inawakilisha mutu mkpwulu. Mgayi ili afanikiwe anahitaji ulinzi wa mutu mkpwulu, au chigongo chakpwe ndachivundzwa.
idiomatic_sw: Maskini anahitaji ulinzi wa wenye nguvu, la sivyo ataanguka.
commentary_sw: Upepo unawakilisha 'mtu mkubwa'. Ili maskini afanikiwe anahitaji ulinzi wa mtu mkubwa, vinginevyo atashindwa.

literal_en: The new stick destroys the old one ?The old stick destroys the new one?
idiomatic_en: The new generation suffers for the sins of the old
commentary_en: Children are negatively affected by the bad deeds of their parents. People will judge them by what their parents have done.
commentary_dg: Ana a mutu huathiriwa vibaya ni mahendo mabaya ga avyazi ao. Atu andahukumu ana kpwa garigohendiwa ni avyazi ao.
idiomatic_sw: Watoto huathiriwa vibaya na matendo mabaya ya wazazi wao.
commentary_sw: Watoto huathiriwa vibaya na matendo mabaya ya wazazi wao. Watu watawahukumu kwa yale ambayo wazazi wao wamefanya.

literal_en: The leg moves, the buttocks do not move
idiomatic_en: You must get up and move if you want to achieve anything
commentary_en: ‘dzanga’- meaning uncertain. When you sit down you don’t achieve or get anything, you have to get up and walk and make an effort.
commentary_dg: Ukisagala tu hutaphaha chitu — ni lazima usimame, utembee, na uhendere juhudi. Chigulu chinadzanga, ela hako tarigadzanga.
idiomatic_sw: Lazima usimame na kutembea ili upate chochote.
commentary_sw: Ukikaa tu hutafanikiwa wala kupata chochote, lazima usimame na kutembea na kufanya juhudi.

literal_en: A pipe suits an honourable person
idiomatic_en: Fine things belong to those who deserve them
commentary_en: People believe that certain items should only belong to the rich and good. If a person who is known to be poor or bad suddenly has something such as a car, or a beautiful wife people will say that it is not his, or that he doesn’t deserve it
commentary_dg: Atu anaamini kuwa vitu fulani vinadzifwaha kumilikiwa ni matajiri na atu ema. Mchiya akionekana na chitu kizuri, atu andaamba si chakpwe au hastahili.
idiomatic_sw: Vitu fulani vinastahili kumilikiwa na watu wanaostahili.
commentary_sw: Watu wanaamini kuwa vitu fulani vinastahili kumilikiwa na matajiri na watu wema peke yao. Maskini au mtu mbaya akiwa na kitu kama gari au mke mzuri, watu watasema si chake au hastahili.

idiomatic_en: What eats at you lives inside your own belly — the greatest threats come from within.
commentary_en: A warning that the thing which harms you is often already inside you — in your own stomach, household, or heart. External enemies are less dangerous than the threats that come from within.
commentary_dg: Onyo kpwamba chitu chinachokurya mara nyinji tayari chi mwako ndani — bindoni mwako, nyumbani kwako, au moyoni mwako. Maadui a kondze ni hatari chidide kuriko vitisho vinavyokpwedza ndani.
idiomatic_sw: Kile kinachokudhuru kiko ndani yako mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Onyo kwamba kile kinachokudhuru mara nyingi tayari kiko ndani yako — tumboni mwako, nyumbani kwako, au moyoni mwako. Maadui wa nje ni hatari kidogo kuliko vitisho vinavyotoka ndani.

digo: Chila mmwenga ana fwerereye, gula waolagbwa ni jembe
literal_en: Everyone has their own bereavement, the frog is killed by a hoe
idiomatic_en: Everyone has their own fate — even the frog is killed by a hoe
swahili: Kila mmoja ana msiba wake, chura huuawa na jembe
commentary_en: We all have a different way to die
commentary_dg: Chila mutu ana njira yakpwe ya kufwa. Hata gula anaolaga ni jembe — hakuna mutu ndayeikimbia fungo rakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu ana hatima yake tofauti na mwenzake.
commentary_sw: Sisi sote tuna njia tofauti ya kufa.

literal_en: Every house has its sigh
idiomatic_en: Every household has its own rules and troubles
commentary_en: Every house has it’s own rules and principles, ways of doing things
commentary_dg: Chila nyumba ina sheria zakpwe na njira zakpwe za kuhenda mambo. Usitarajie nyumba ya mutu dza yakpwo.
idiomatic_sw: Kila nyumba ina kanuni na matatizo yake.
commentary_sw: Kila nyumba ina sheria na kanuni zake, njia zake za kufanya mambo.

digo: Chilume ni kani Chilume ni kani
literal_en: Manhood is (bold and) stubborn
idiomatic_en: True manhood requires boldness and toughness
commentary_en: kani – very broad meaning: bold, tough, cunning, stubborn, brazen
commentary_dg: Kani — neno renye maana pana: ujasiri, ugumu, ujanja, ukaidi. Kulume ni kuhenda mambo kpwa kani na nguvu.
idiomatic_sw: Uanaume wa kweli unahitaji ujasiri na uthabiti.
commentary_sw: Kani — maana pana sana: ujasiri, ugumu, ujanja, ukaidi, usahaulifu.

digo: Chimanyi wa madzi wauka na madzi
idiomatic_en: Those who know water are carried away by it — deep familiarity with something binds you to its fate.
commentary_en: Those who understand water eventually depart with the water — meaning expertise comes from deep immersion, and the knowledgeable are shaped by and ultimately bound to their domain. One can also read it as a warning: those who play with water will be carried away by it.
commentary_dg: Atu amanyao madzi mwishowe ganauka na madzi — maana ujuzi unarehe kpwa kuzama ndani, na mwenye maarifa anaumba ni na mwishowe anafungamana na uwanja wakpwe. Inaweza piya kukala onyo: achezaye na madzi andatsukurwa nago.
idiomatic_sw: Ujuzi wa maji huja kwa kupitia maji.
commentary_sw: Wale wanaojua maji hatimaye huondoka na maji — maana ujuzi hutoka kwa kuzama ndani, na mwenye maarifa huundwa na hatimaye hufungamana na uwanja wake. Inaweza pia kuwa onyo: anayecheza na maji atabebwa nayo.

idiomatic_en: What is spilled cannot be fully gathered again — some losses can never be undone.
commentary_en: Once something is spilled, it cannot be fully recovered. This proverb counsels care and caution — whether with words, relationships, or resources — because some losses are irreversible.
commentary_dg: Chitu chimwagikecho tachiweza kukusanywa tele kaheri. Ndarira ihi inashauri uangalifu — ikale ni maneno, mahusiano, au mali — kpwa sababu hasara zinjine tazikuuya.
idiomatic_sw: Kilichomwagika hakikusanywi kamili tena.
commentary_sw: Kitu kilichomwagika hakiwezi kukusanywa kamili tena. Methali hii inashauri uangalifu — iwe ni maneno, mahusiano, au mali — kwa sababu hasara zingine hazirudi.

literal_en: Goodness does not lack a defect
idiomatic_en: Nothing good is without a flaw
commentary_en: A reminder that nothing in this world is perfect. Even the best person, plan, or thing will have some flaw. Used to temper unrealistic expectations and to console someone whose efforts have been criticised.
commentary_dg: Ukumbusho kpwamba takuna chitu kamili duniani. Hata mutu mnono, mpango, au chitu chinono chindakala na ila fulani. Inaambwa kpwa kupunguza matarajio gasigokala ga kpweli na kumuphoza moyo mutu ambaye juhudi zakpwe zikakosolewa.
idiomatic_sw: Hakuna kitu kizuri ambacho hakina kasoro.
commentary_sw: Ukumbusho kwamba hakuna kitu kamili duniani. Hata mtu bora, mpango, au kitu bora kitakuwa na kasoro fulani. Husemwa kupunguza matarajio yasiyo halisi na kumfariji mtu ambaye juhudi zake zimekosolewa.

digo: Chiphala kudosa lugbwe siko kumala makonje
literal_en: If an antelope cuts a rope it does not mean the sisal is finished
idiomatic_en: One setback does not mean all is lost
swahili: Paa akikata kamba, si maana katani imeisha
commentary_en: There will be enough sisal to make a new rope. Don’t lose hope, even if things are not going well.
commentary_dg: Chiphala achidosa lugbwe, bado kuna makonje ga kuhendera lugbwe nyiphya. Usikate tamaa, hata mambo gachikuphiya vibaya.
idiomatic_sw: Kushindwa mara moja haimaanishi umeshindwa kabisa.
commentary_sw: Kutakuwa na katani ya kutosha kutengeneza kamba mpya. Usikate tamaa, hata mambo yakienda vibaya.

literal_en: A person who goes to a medicine-man does not return
idiomatic_en: Once you go to a healer, you lose everything you have
swahili: Aendaye kwa mganga harudi na kitu
commentary_en: he will have spent everything he has on him
commentary_dg: Mutu achiphiya kpwa mganga ndautumia kpwosi arichonacho. Mapesa gakpwe gosi gandamala haho kpwa mganga.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu aendaye kwa mganga atatumia kila alichonacho.
commentary_sw: Atatumia kila alichonacho kwake.

digo: Chirima cha Beng’anya sicho cha Meng’anya Beng’anya’s hill is not like Meng’anya’s
literal_en: Beng’anya = the father of somebody,
idiomatic_en: Everyone's struggles are unique — don't compare your problems to another's
swahili: Kilima cha Beng'anya si kile cha Meng'anya
commentary_en: Meng’anya = the mother of somebody.Your problems are not the same as my problems. A hill is steep, like problems. Me/Be are from Giryama
commentary_dg: Matatizo ga mutu mmwenga si sawa na matatizo ga mutu munji. Chila mutu ana chirima chakpwe — usirilinganishe na cha mutu myawe.
idiomatic_sw: Matatizo yako si sawa na matatizo yangu.
commentary_sw: Meng'anya = mama wa mtu fulani. Matatizo yako si sawa na matatizo yangu. Kilima ni kigumu, kama matatizo. Me/Be zinatoka kwa Kigiryama.

digo: Chiriro chiandza mwenye jirani naye akasikira
literal_en: Mourning starts at the neighbours house and he has heard it
idiomatic_en: When trouble starts nearby, the neighbour takes heed
swahili: Kilio huanza kwa mwenye, jirani naye akasikia
commentary_en: When mourning begins at a neighbour's house, the whole community takes notice. This proverb warns people to be alert to misfortune nearby, because trouble that starts elsewhere may soon reach your own door.
commentary_dg: Chiriro chichiandza nyumbani kpwa jirani, jamii yosi inasikira. Ndarira ihi inaonya atu kukala makini na msiba wa jirani, kpwa sababu matatizo ganayoandza phatu phanjine ganaweza kufika mryangoni kwako vi karibuni.
idiomatic_sw: Matatizo yakianzia jirani yako, jiandae kwani yanaweza kukujia pia.
commentary_sw: Kilio kinapoanza nyumbani kwa jirani, jamii yote inasikia. Methali hii inaonya watu kuwa macho kwa msiba wa jirani, kwa sababu matatizo yanayoanza mahali pengine yanaweza kufika mlangoni kwako hivi karibuni.

literal_en: Do not sin against a thing which has no sin
idiomatic_en: Do not wrong someone who has done you no wrong
commentary_en: If you have a good relationship with someone, don’t abuse it.
commentary_dg: Ukikala na uhusiano mzuri na mutu, usidze kuuhendera dambi. Chisicho dambi usichitiye dambi.
idiomatic_sw: Usiharibu uhusiano mzuri kwa matendo mabaya.
commentary_sw: Ukiwa na uhusiano mzuri na mtu, usiutumie vibaya.

literal_en: A bed is a brother
idiomatic_en: Your bed is your closest companion in times of sorrow
commentary_en: You are very close to your bed when you lie on it. It’s the place where you take your sorrows and illness.
commentary_dg: Chitanda ni mahali pha kpwa karibu sana na uwe. Ndipha unapholaza huzuni zako na ukongo wako — ndugu wa kpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Kitanda ni mahali pa karibu zaidi unapopeleka huzuni na maumivu yako.
commentary_sw: Uko karibu sana na kitanda chako unapojilaza. Ni mahali unapopeleka huzuni na ugonjwa wako.

literal_en: The sheep’s head listens in the little clay cooking pot
idiomatic_en: If the sheep had listened to advice, its head would not be in the cooking pot
swahili: Kichwa cha kondoo husikia ndani ya chungu
commentary_en: If the sheep had listened top advice earlier she wouldn’t have ended up in the pot.
commentary_dg: Ng'ondzi kala achiisikira ushauri kare kabila, kachiize kpwa chidzunguni. Usikire ushauri kabila mambo tagadzangbwekuphiya vibaya.
idiomatic_sw: Anayekataa ushauri atapatwa na matatizo.
commentary_sw: Kondoo kama angesikiliza ushauri mapema asingeishia ndani ya chungu.

literal_en: The thing which comes from inside you is hot gre
idiomatic_en: What comes from within you burns the deepest
swahili: Kitokacho ndani ya tumbo ni moto
commentary_en: e.g. If someone wants to hurt your child (which caused you such pains to deliver) you will feel at pain
commentary_dg: Mutu achitaka kumdhuru mwanao — ambaye kala ukagbwira utsungu kumvyala — undaona maumivu makulu. Kpwa mana chilacho ndanini chako ni cha moho.
idiomatic_sw: Maumivu yanayohusu wapendwa wako yanakuchoma zaidi.
commentary_sw: K.m. Mtu akitaka kumdhuru mtoto wako (ambaye ulikuwa na uchungu kumzaa) utahisi maumivu makubwa.

literal_en: The sign of rain is clouds
idiomatic_en: Coming events cast their shadows before them
commentary_en: Teaches the value of reading signs and being perceptive. Just as clouds reliably signal coming rain, events and behaviours give early warnings of what is to follow. Used to advise attentiveness and foresight.
commentary_dg: Inafundza thamani ya kusoma ishara na kukala mwangalifu. Dza viratu maingu ganatabiri mvula, matukio na tabiya ganarehe ishara za kare za chitu chinachokpwedza. Inaambwa kpwa kushauri makini na busara ya mbere.
idiomatic_sw: Ishara za mapema hukuonya kuhusu kile kinachokuja.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha thamani ya kusoma ishara na kuwa mwangalifu. Kama vile mawingu yanatabiri mvua, matukio na tabia hutoa ishara za mapema za kile kinachokuja. Husemwa kushauri makini na busara ya mbele.

literal_en: Medicine for a cricket is a cricket
idiomatic_en: Fight fire with fire — the punishment should fit the crime
commentary_en: The punishment should fit the rime. An eye for an eye etc.
commentary_dg: Adhabu inafwaha kulingana na kosa. Dawa ya chenene ni chenene — dzitso kpwa dzitso, dzino kpwa dzino.
idiomatic_sw: Adhabu inafaa kulingana na kosa.
commentary_sw: Adhabu inafaa kulingana na kosa. Jicho kwa jicho n.k.

literal_en: Medicine for a debt is to be paid
idiomatic_en: The only cure for a debt is repayment
commentary_en: (creditor’s perspective)
commentary_dg: Methali ihi ni kpwa upande wa mdai. Njira mwenga bahi ya kuusa deni ni kuriphiwa — hakuna njira nyinji.
idiomatic_sw: Njia pekee ya kumaliza deni ni kulipa.
commentary_sw: Kutoka upande wa mdai.

literal_en: Bit by bit finishes the basket
idiomatic_en: Little by little, even a full basket gets emptied
commentary_en: If you keep on taking a little your store will soon be empty
commentary_dg: Ukiendelea kuhola chidide chidide, ghala rako rindamala. Doko-doko yamala chiphinda — tunza vitu vyako vizuri.
idiomatic_sw: Ukiendelea kuchukua kidogo kidogo, hifadhi yako itamalizika.
commentary_sw: Ukiendelea kuchukua kidogo kidogo, ghala lako litaisha haraka.

digo: Donje kugbwa ni mlungu wa diya
literal_en: A fallen piece of dough is the God of a dog
idiomatic_en: One person's misfortune is another's blessing
swahili: Donje lililoanguka ni mungu wa mbwa
commentary_en: The one who causes the dough to fall is the dog’s God (not the person who was eating) and the dog will thank him as he eats.
commentary_dg: Yule anayesababisha donje kugbwa ndiye mlungu wa diya — si mutu ariyekala anarya. Bahati ya mutu mmwenga ni musada wa mutu munji.
idiomatic_sw: Hasara ya mtu mmoja ni faida ya mwingine.
commentary_sw: Yule anayesababisha unga kuanguka ndiye mungu wa mbwa (si yule aliyekuwa anakula) na mbwa atamshukuru akila.

digo: Dunia ni mlomo wa diya tautsungurirwa
literal_en: The earth is the mouth of a dog, you cannot peep inside (either)
idiomatic_en: The world is unfathomable, just like a dog's mouth — you cannot see inside
swahili: Dunia ni mdomo wa mbwa, hauchunguliwi
commentary_en: The world, like a dog's mouth, cannot be peered into — its workings are hidden and unpredictable. Used to express resignation before the mysteries of life, or to caution against assuming one understands everything.
commentary_dg: Dunia, dza mlomo wa diya, tautsungurirwa — mambo gakpwe gakafitswa na tagatabiriiki. Inaambwa kpwa kuonyesa kukubaliana na siri za maisha, au kuonya kpwa kukahaza kudhani unamanya chila chitu.
idiomatic_sw: Dunia ni siri na huwezi kujua kila kitu kinachoendelea ndani yake.
commentary_sw: Dunia, kama mdomo wa mbwa, haichunguliwi — mambo yake yamefichwa na hayatabiriiki. Husemwa kuonyesha kukubaliana na siri za maisha, au kuonya dhidi ya kudhani unaelewa kila kitu.

literal_en: A thing is said but should not be done
idiomatic_en: Some things are said but should never be done
commentary_en: Distinguishes between speech and action. Some things may be spoken of — as warnings, lessons, or hypotheticals — but should never be carried out. Used to restrain someone who is about to act rashly on something merely discussed.
commentary_dg: Inabainisha tofauti kahi-kahi ya maneno na mahendo. Mambo ganjine ganaweza kuambwa — dza maonyo, mafundzo, au mawazo — ela tagafai kuhendwa katu. Inaambwa kpwa kumuzuia mutu anayeelonda kuhenda kpwa haraka dzambo ririroambwa tu.
idiomatic_sw: Si kila kinachosemwa kinafaa kufanywa.
commentary_sw: Inabainisha tofauti kati ya maneno na matendo. Mambo mengine yanaweza kusemwa — kama maonyo, mafunzo, au mawazo — lakini hayafai kufanywa kamwe. Husemwa kumzuia mtu anayetaka kutenda kwa haraka jambo lililosemwa tu.

literal_en: An eye is to see with not to eat with
idiomatic_en: Looking is not the same as having
commentary_en: A reminder that seeing something does not entitle you to it. The eye can admire but the hand may not possess. Used to check envy, covetousness, or the assumption that witnessing abundance means one can partake in it.
commentary_dg: Ukumbusho kpwamba kuona chitu takukuphire haki ya kukimiliki. Dzitso rinaweza kulola ela mkpwono tauwezi kumiliki. Inaambwa kpwa kudhibiti wivu, tamaa, au kuona kpwamba kuona winji maana unaweza kushiriki.
idiomatic_sw: Kuona kitu si sawa na kukimiliki au kukipata.
commentary_sw: Ukumbusho kwamba kuona kitu hakukupi haki ya kukimiliki. Jicho linaweza kutazama lakini mkono hauwezi kumiliki. Husemwa kudhibiti wivu, tamaa, au dhana kwamba kuona wingi maana unaweza kushiriki.

literal_en: The eye of a one-eyed person
idiomatic_en: Guard carefully what is precious and irreplaceable to you
swahili: Jicho la chongo likitobolewa ndilo hilo
commentary_en: If a one-eyed person's remaining eye is destroyed, that is the end — there is no replacement. Used to warn someone to protect what is precious and irreplaceable, whether a relationship, a resource, or an opportunity.
commentary_dg: Dzitso ra tsongo richitsotswa, riro — taribadilishwa. Inaambwa kpwa kumuonya mutu arinde chitu chakpwe cha thamani ambacho tachibadilishwa, ikale ni uhusiano, rasilimali, au fursa.
idiomatic_sw: Linda kwa makini kitu chako cha thamani ambacho hakibadilishwi.
commentary_sw: Jicho la chongo likitobolewa, ndilo hilo — halibadilishwi. Husemwa kumwonya mtu alinde kitu chake cha thamani ambacho hakibadilishwi, iwe ni uhusiano, rasilimali, au fursa.

literal_en: An eye does not have a curtain
idiomatic_en: You cannot hide what is plainly visible
commentary_en: The eye has no curtain — what is seen cannot be unseen. Used when a truth has been revealed or witnessed and cannot be denied or hidden any longer. A statement about the power of evidence and direct witness.
commentary_dg: Dzitso tarina paziya — chirichoonekana tachiweza kufutwa. Inaambwa ukpweli uchifitshulwa au kushuhudiwa na tauwezi kukanushwa wala kufitswa tsona. Ni kauli kuhusu nguvu ya ushahidi na ushuhuda wa mutu mwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kuficha kitu kinachoonekana wazi wazi.
commentary_sw: Jicho halina pazia — kilichoonekana hakiwezi kufutwa. Husemwa ukweli unapofichuliwa au kushuhudiwa na hauwezi kukanushwa wala kufichwa tena. Ni kauli kuhusu nguvu ya ushahidi na ushuhuda wa moja kwa moja.

digo: Dzogolo kuika sio dawa ya kucha
literal_en: The cock crowing is not medicine for the dawn
idiomatic_en: What must happen will happen regardless
swahili: Jogoo kuika si dawa ya kucha
commentary_en: The day will always come, whether the cock crows or not
commentary_dg: Siku ndaikpwedza hata dzogolo richike au risike. Mambo ga kuphaha gandaphaha, haidzetegemee na chitu chanjina.
swahili_equivalent: Jogoo kuika si dawa ya kucha
idiomatic_sw: Kinachopaswa kutokea kitatokea hata bila ishara.
commentary_sw: Siku itaendelea kuja hata jogoo akiwika au la.

idiomatic_en: Even an elephant can rot in a single day — no matter how great, ruin can come swiftly.
commentary_en: In a single day, even an elephant can begin to rot. Great things can be destroyed in a very short time. Used to warn against complacency and to remind people that ruin can come swiftly, no matter how strong or established something seems.
commentary_dg: Kpwa dzuwa mwenga tu, hata ndzovu inaweza kuandza kuola. Vitu vikulu vinaweza kuharibiwa kpwa muda mfupi sana. Inaambwa kpwa kuonya kpwa kukahaza kudziridhisha na kukumbusa atu kpwamba uharibifu unaweza kukpwedza haraka, kutsojali chitu chinaonekana imara viyanivi.
idiomatic_sw: Muda mfupi unatosha kuharibu kitu kikubwa.
commentary_sw: Kwa siku moja tu, hata tembo anaweza kuanza kuoza. Vitu vikubwa vinaweza kuharibiwa kwa muda mfupi sana. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya kujiridhisha na kukumbusha watu kwamba uharibifu unaweza kuja haraka, haijalishi kitu kinavyoonekana imara.

digo: Fisi richirya mkpwongo, mzima funga mryango
literal_en: If a hyena eats a sick person, the well person (should) shut the door
idiomatic_en: When danger strikes the weak, the strong should take precaution
swahili: Fisi akila mgonjwa, mzima funga mlango
commentary_en: You must take steps to ensure someone else’s problem doesn’t affect you too
commentary_dg: Ni lazima uhende mambo ili tatizo ra mutu munji risikugbwire uwe. Fisi richirya mkpwongo, mzima ni lazima adzilinde mwenye.
swahili_equivalent: Fisi akila mgonjwa, mzima funga mlango
idiomatic_sw: Hatari ikimpata dhaifu, mwenye nguvu ajilinde.
commentary_sw: Lazima uchukue hatua ili tatizo la mtu mwingine lisikuathiri pia.

literal_en: The craftsman doesn’t sleep in a good place
idiomatic_en: The craftsman who serves others often neglects himself
commentary_en: The skilled craftsman who builds fine things for others often lives in poor conditions himself. A commentary on the irony that those who serve others with their expertise frequently neglect their own wellbeing.
commentary_dg: Fundi stadi anayedzenga vitu vinono kpwa atu anjine mara nyinji iye mwenye anaishi kpwa hali mbaya. Ni maoni kuhusu kinaya kpwamba atu anaohudumia atu anjine kpwa utaalamu wao mara nyinji ganadzisahau enye.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayewahudumia wengine mara nyingi hujisahau mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Fundi stadi anayejenga vitu vizuri kwa wengine mara nyingi yeye mwenyewe anaishi katika hali mbaya. Ni maoni kuhusu kinaya kwamba wale wanaowahudumia wengine kwa utaalamu wao mara nyingi hujisahau wenyewe.

literal_en: Tying something well doesn’t hurt the hand
idiomatic_en: Taking good care of your possessions never hurts you
commentary_en: i.e. take good care of things (e.g. the money you are carrying tied in your handkerchief), it can’t hurt you.
commentary_dg: Tunza vitu vyako vizuri — dza vira mapesa ga kufungwa kpwa leso yako. Fungato nono tailumiza mkpwono, ela inakutunza.
idiomatic_sw: Kutunza mali yako vizuri hakukudhuru kamwe.
commentary_sw: Yaani tunza vitu vizuri (k.m. pesa unazobeba zimefungwa katika leso yako), haviwezi kukudhuru.

literal_en: the ‘fungo’ that you eat is what will come out of you
idiomatic_en: You reap what you sow
commentary_en: fungo=meat or an insect. You reap what you sow, you are what you eat
commentary_dg: Unavyohenda ndicho undachophaha. Fungo uryacho ndicho chinachokuya — unavuna urivyopanda.
idiomatic_sw: Unavyovuna ndicho ulichopanda.
commentary_sw: Fungo = nyama au mdudu. Unavyovuna ndiyo ulivyopanda, wewe ni ulicho kula.

idiomatic_en: The knot of the tree is in its sap — every living thing holds its truth within.
commentary_en: An obscure proverb whose exact meaning is uncertain. 'Fungo' may refer to a knot or bundle, 'muche' to a tree or wife, and 'tsui' to juice or sap. It likely speaks to a traditional domestic or agricultural truth known to Digo elders.
commentary_dg: Ndarira ambayo maana yakpwe kamili taimanyikana vinono. 'Fungo' inaweza kumanisha fundo au furushi, 'muche' muhi au mche, na 'tsui' utomvu au madzi ga muhi. Inaelekea kuonesa ukpweli wa chijadi wa nyumbani au ulimizi unaomanyikana ni azehe a Chidigo.
idiomatic_sw: Methali hii inahusu hekima ya jadi ya Wadigo.
commentary_sw: Methali isiyoeleweka vizuri ambayo maana yake kamili haijulikani. 'Fungo' inaweza kumaanisha fundo au furushi, 'muche' mti au mke, na 'tsui' utomvu au juisi. Inaelekea kuzungumzia ukweli wa jadi wa nyumbani au kilimo unaojulikana na wazee wa Kidigo.

literal_en: Die alone
idiomatic_en: May you die alone — a curse of complete abandonment
commentary_en: A curse, said e.g. by an angry parent casting off a child, implying no further links
commentary_dg: Ni laana inayosemwa ni mzazi ariyekasirika kpwa mwanawe — ikimaanisha hakuna uhusiano kare. Maneno ga utsungu sana.
idiomatic_sw: Laana ya kutengwa kabisa na familia.
commentary_sw: Laana inayosemwa k.m. na mzazi aliyekasirika akimfukuza mtoto, ikimaanisha hakuna uhusiano tena.

literal_en: A distant stick does not kill a snake
idiomatic_en: Help that is far away is useless when danger is near
commentary_en: Used to warn that distant resources or allies are of no help in an urgent crisis. The proverb stresses the importance of having reliable help close at hand rather than depending on promises from far away.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kuonya kukala msaada wa kure tauhendere wakati wa hatari ya phapho. Methali ihi inasisitiza umuhimu wa kukala na msaada wa phephi badala ya kutegemea ahadi za kure.
idiomatic_sw: Msaada wa mbali haufai wakati hatari iko karibu.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kuonya kuwa msaada wa mbali haufai wakati wa hatari ya haraka. Methali hii inasisitiza umuhimu wa kuwa na msaada wa karibu badala ya kutegemea ahadi za mbali.

digo: Fwimbo ya mganga ni nyire na fupi
literal_en: The medicine-man’s stick is long and short
idiomatic_en: A deceiver adjusts his tools to suit his schemes
swahili: Fimbo ya mganga ni ndefu na fupi
commentary_en: Medicine men often deceive in order to get money, so they will give a treatment even when they know it won’t work.
commentary_dg: Aganga mara nyinji hudanganya ili kuphaha mapesa. Andamupha matibabu hata achimanya kuwa tagandafwaha kazi. Fwimbo yao ni nyire wakati wanaopatsa na fupi wakati tawaoptsa [?].
idiomatic_sw: Mdanganyifu hubadilisha mbinu zake kulingana na hali.
commentary_sw: Waganga mara nyingi hudanganya ili kupata pesa, kwa hivyo watatoa matibabu hata wakijua hayatafanya kazi.

literal_en: Old things do not stink
idiomatic_en: What has stood the test of time retains its value
commentary_en: Said to defend the value of old customs, traditions, or long-standing relationships. What has endured over time has proven its worth and should not be dismissed simply because it is not new.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kutetea thamani ya mila za kare, desturi, au uhusiano wa muda mure. Chitu chirichokala kpwa muda mure chikathibitisha thamani yakpwe, wala tachifwa kupuuzwa kpwa kukala si chiphya.
idiomatic_sw: Vitu vya zamani vina thamani yake ambayo haiishi.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kutetea thamani ya mila za zamani, desturi, au mahusiano ya muda mrefu. Kitu kilichodumu kwa muda kirefu kimethibitisha thamani yake wala hakistahili kupuuzwa kwa sababu si kipya.

literal_en: Things to do with traditional dances are not all spoken about
idiomatic_en: Not everything about a tradition can be told — some things must remain secret
commentary_en: because they are too many and some are secret. i.e. you cannot say everything there is to say about yourself
commentary_dg: Mambo ga moroni ni manji sana na ganjina ni siri. Huwezi kugasema gosi — dza vira huwezi kusema kpwosi kuhusu uwe mwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Si kila siri inaweza kusemwa wazi wazi.
commentary_sw: Kwa sababu ni nyingi sana na baadhi ni siri. Yaani huwezi kusema kila kitu kuhusu wewe mwenyewe.

digo: Gadza-gadza ya mganga yalaza mkpwongo na tamaa
literal_en: The medicine man’s treatment brings the sick person hope
idiomatic_en: Even false hope from a healer gives the sick person reason to endure
swahili: Dawa ya mganga humlaza mgonjwa na tamaa
commentary_en: hope of healing
commentary_dg: Gadza-gadza ya mganga inamupha mkpwongo tamaa ya kupholwa, hata kama dawa taifwahi kazi. Matumaini ndiyo ganayomuika mkpwongo.
idiomatic_sw: Hata matumaini madogo kutoka kwa mganga yanamsaidia mgonjwa kuvumilia.
commentary_sw: Matumaini ya kupona.

digo: Gafula ni mbii, dzogolo rachimbira ndifu
literal_en: Suddenly is ..., the cockrel ran ...
idiomatic_en: Danger strikes without warning, and everyone scrambles to save themselves.
commentary_en: Warns that danger strikes without warning. Just as a cockerel bolts at a sudden noise, people must always be prepared for the unexpected. Said when misfortune arrives with no prior sign.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala hatari indakpwedza bila tahadhari. Dza vira dzogolo rinachimbira kpwa sauti ya gafula, atu ni lazima akale tayari kpwa mambo gasigotarajiwa. Husemwa msiba uchikpwedza bila ishara.
idiomatic_sw: Hatari huja ghafla, na kila mmoja hukimbia kujilinda.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kuwa hatari huja bila tahadhari. Kama vile jogoo hukimbia kwa sauti ya ghafla, watu lazima wawe tayari kwa mambo yasiyotarajiwa. Husemwa msiba ukija bila ishara.

literal_en: Things (fruits) which won’t fall will be shaken down (from the tree)
idiomatic_en: What will not come willingly will be taken by force
commentary_en: If someone won’t do something voluntarily they will be forced
commentary_dg: Mutu asiyetaka kuhenda chitu kpwa hiyari yakpwe andalazimishwa. Matunda gasigogbwa kpwa muhi gandazuguzwa photsi.
idiomatic_sw: Kile kisichokuja kwa hiari kitachukuliwa kwa nguvu.
commentary_sw: Mtu asipofanya kitu kwa hiari atalazimishwa.

literal_en: Those who don’t know what glue is get caught two by two
idiomatic_en: The naive fall into the same trap together
commentary_en: Some animals (rats) stick together when being chased and all try to go into the same hole, which makes them much easier to catch. Stupid people do likewise.
commentary_dg: Atu asiyemanya hatari hundanaswa phamwenga, dza vira panya osi achidza kuinjira tundu mwenga. Uzuzu unauhendya mutu kutiywa kpwa urahisi.
idiomatic_sw: Wajinga hushirikiana katika makosa na kunaswa pamoja.
commentary_sw: Wanyama fulani (panya) hushikamana wakikimbiwa na wote hujaribu kuingia katika shimo moja, jambo linalowafanya wanaswe kwa urahisi. Wapumbavu hufanya vivyo hivyo.

literal_en: The homestead’s log is not urinated on
idiomatic_en: Show respect to elders and the traditions of your home
commentary_en: This log is where the elders sit. You should listen to them and respect them rather than ignoring or despising them.
commentary_dg: Gogo hiri ndipha azehe anaphosagala. Unapaswa kuasikira na kuaheshimu badala ya kuadharau. Heshima ya azehe ni muhimu kpwa kaya.
idiomatic_sw: Heshimu wazee na mila za nyumbani kwako.
commentary_sw: Gogo hili ndipo wazee wanapoketi. Unapaswa kuwasikiliza na kuwaheshimu badala ya kuwadharau.

literal_en: A millipede and its stick/piece of rafia
idiomatic_en: To solve a problem permanently, remove it completely — root and all
commentary_en: if you throw a millipede out together with a stick the millipede will not return. If you want to make sure a problem does not happen again, make sure you take care of it properly, e.g. a person you don’t want in you house.
commentary_dg: Ukitsupha gongolo phamwenga na chigongoche, gongolo tariuya kare. Ukitaka kuhakikisha tatizo tariuya, rishughulikiye kpwa kpweli na vizuri.
idiomatic_sw: Ili kumaliza tatizo kabisa, liondoe pamoja na chanzo chake.
commentary_sw: Ukitupa jongoo pamoja na kijiti, jongoo hatarejea. Ukitaka kuhakikisha tatizo halirudi tena, lishughulikie vizuri, k.m. mtu usiyemtaka nyumbani kwako.

literal_en: The weak person’s horn is a snail shell
idiomatic_en: The weak person's greatest weapon is still useless
commentary_en: The greatest weapon of a weak person is useless, ie. he has no rights
commentary_dg: Silaha kulu ya mutu mnyonje haina maana — ni dza kola bahi. Mnyonje kana haki kpwa mana kana nguvu.
idiomatic_sw: Silaha bora ya mtu dhaifu bado haina nguvu.
commentary_sw: Silaha kuu ya mtu dhaifu haina maana, yaani hana haki.

idiomatic_en: A person's right does not vanish
commentary_en: A statement of faith in justice: that which is rightfully someone's cannot be taken away permanently. Used to encourage those who have been wronged to be patient, as truth and fairness will eventually prevail.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kutiya moyo kukala haki taipotea. Mutu achiyedhulumiwa agodze, mana ukpweli na usawa ndivyo vindashinda mwishowe.
idiomatic_sw: Haki ya mtu haikomi wala kupotea.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kutia moyo kuwa haki haipotei. Mtu aliyedhulumiwa asubiri, kwa sababu ukweli na usawa ndavyo vitashinda mwishowe.

literal_en: A mother’s buttocks buy a field
idiomatic_en: A man in love will do anything for the woman he loves and her children
commentary_en: If a man really loves a woman there is no limit to what he will do for her and her children
commentary_dg: Mlume achimendza mchetu kpwa kpweli, hakuna kikomo cha achondamuhendere, ye na ana akpwe. Mapenzi ndiyo ganayohimira.
idiomatic_sw: Mwanaume anayempenda mwanamke hana kikomo cha kile atakachomfanyia.
commentary_sw: Mwanaume akimpenda mwanamke kweli kweli, hakuna kikomo cha kile atakachomfanyia yeye na watoto wake.

idiomatic_en: A person's true worth or fate is hidden within and cannot be judged from the outside.
commentary_en: Suggests that a person's true character or destiny is hidden within them, not visible on the surface. Used to caution against judging people by outward appearance, since what matters lies within.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala tabiya ya kpweli au hatima ya mutu imefichika ndani yakpwe, taionekana kunze. Husemwa kuonya kukala mutu asihukumiwe kpwa sura ya kunze, mana muhimu ni chiricho ndani.
idiomatic_sw: Methali hii inahusu sifa au hatima iliyofichika ndani ya mtu.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kuwa tabia halisi au hatima ya mtu imefichwa ndani yake, haionekani nje. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya kumhukumu mtu kwa sura ya nje, kwa kuwa muhimu ni kile kilichomo ndani.

literal_en: A lie travels fast
idiomatic_en: Lies spread faster than truth.
commentary_en: v. old Digo.
commentary_dg: Neno hiri ni ra Chidigo cha kare sana. Handzo — uongo — hunangundya haraka kuriko kpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Uongo husafiri haraka kuliko ukweli.
commentary_sw: Neno la Kidigo la kale.

literal_en: The 3 days of mourning don’t happen until the appointed time
idiomatic_en: Don't prepare for what hasn't happened yet.
swahili: Matanga ya siku tatu hayaji mpaka wakati wake
commentary_en: ie. you can’t plan a funeral until the person has died. Don’t expect things to happen without an appointment
commentary_dg: Mutu kaweza kupanga hanga kabila wakati wa kufwa ukafika. Vitu vinahedza wakativye, si kabila ya wakatiwe.
idiomatic_sw: Usitarajie mambo kabla ya wakati wake.
commentary_sw: Yaani, huwezi kupanga mazishi mpaka mtu amefariki. Usitarajie mambo yatokee bila mpangilio.

digo: Hewa hewa ya ajema, chila mmwenga ana chiburuche
literal_en: The palm wine tappers song, every one has his own gourd
idiomatic_en: Everyone has their own work to do, and they sing while doing it.
swahili: Wimbo wa wapanda mnazi, kila mmoja ana kibuyu chake
commentary_en: The song that the palm wine tappers sing means that they are doing their work. Applied to people working together and singing at the same time (ie. women pounding maize)
commentary_dg: Ajema achijema mnazi, anaimba hewa. Chila mutu ana kaziye na anaikato kpwa njira yakpwe. Dza achetu achisuna muhindi, anaimba wakati anasuna.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu ana kazi yake na njia yake ya kuifanya.
commentary_sw: Wimbo wa wapanda mnazi unamaanisha wanafanya kazi yao. Inatumika kwa watu wanaofanya kazi pamoja na kuimba wakati huo huo (kama vile wanawake wanapoponda mahindi).

literal_en: Noise noise is growing
idiomatic_en: Growth comes through the noise and bustle of life.
commentary_en: Said to encourage those who are struggling through the noise and demands of daily life. The bustle and commotion of effort is itself the process of growth and progress — there is no quiet path to maturity.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kumtiya moyo mutu anayepitiya kelele na mahitaji ga maisha ga chila siku. Bidii na hoyo-hoyo za jitihada ndizo njia ya kukula na kuendelea — taukuna njia tulivu ya kukomaa.
idiomatic_sw: Maendeleo yanatokana na bidii na shughuli nyingi.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kumtia moyo mtu anayepitia kelele na mahitaji ya maisha ya kila siku. Bidii na kelele za jitihada ndizo njia ya kukua na kuendelea — hakuna njia tulivu ya kukomaa.

digo: Hoyo hoyo ndiyo ukala 106.Inapigbwa ela ina chitswa It
literal_en: Noise noise is the hunt is beaten but it has a head Whe be a
idiomatic_en: Every discussion must have a point of focus.
swahili: Kelele kelele ndio msako; inapigwa lakini ina kichwa
commentary_en: The hunters have their own signals and sounds while they are hunting n you have a discussion (piga) there must point of focus (chitswa)
commentary_dg: Akala achikpwenda kpwa ukala, ana ishara zao na sauti zao. Kpwa hivyo mazungumzo gosi ni lazima gakale na chitswa — lengo ra kuelezwa.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mazungumzo yanapaswa kuwa na lengo.
commentary_sw: Wawindaji wana ishara zao na sauti zao wakati wa kuwinda. Unapokuwa na majadiliano lazima kuwe na lengo (kichwa).

literal_en: (From the) Ashes where you don’t sleep you beat away the dogs
idiomatic_en: If you don't want something, don't begrudge others who do.
commentary_en: You don’t sleep in the ashes but you don’t want the dogs to either. If you have something you aren’t using, why don’t you want me to use it either.
commentary_dg: Uwe kulala kpwa ivu ela unadzihusisha kpwa kuphirikiza diya. Ukiwa na chitu usichochitumiya, kpwa utu wani hutaki wengine wachitumiye?
idiomatic_sw: Usimzuie mtu kutumia kitu ambacho wewe hutaki.
commentary_sw: Hulali kwenye majivu lakini hutaki mbwa walale humo pia. Ukiwa na kitu usichokitumia, kwa nini hutaki mimi nikitumie?

digo: Jasho ra mutu taririka wala taririwa
literal_en: A person’s sweat is not edible and is not eaten
idiomatic_en: Never cheat a person out of what they have earned through hard work.
swahili: Jasho la mtu haliliwi wala haliliki
commentary_en: When a person works he sweats and uses the money earned to buy things. He should not be cheated of those things. God will revenge.
commentary_dg: Mutu achifanya kazi anahoka jasho na anahitumiya pesa achizopata kununua vitu. Asichenganywe vitu hivyo, mana Mlungu ndaumuonera.
idiomatic_sw: Usimdhulumu mtu matunda ya kazi yake.
commentary_sw: Mtu anapofanya kazi hutoka jasho na kutumia pesa alizopata kununua vitu. Asidanganywe vitu hivyo. Mungu atalipiza.

literal_en: A person’s sweat is not eaten
idiomatic_en: No one should exploit another's hard work.
commentary_en: A warning against exploiting another person's labour or earnings. Used to rebuke those who take advantage of someone else's hard work without contributing their own effort.
commentary_dg: Ni onyo dhidi ya kutumia jasho ra mutu munyawe. Husemwa kumkemea mutu anayetumia kazi ngumu ya munyawe bila kuchangiya jitihada yakpwe mwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Usile jasho la mtu mwingine.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha onyo dhidi ya kutumia jasho la mtu mwingine. Husemwa kumkemea mtu anayetumia kazi ngumu ya mwingine bila kuchangia jitihada yake mwenyewe.

idiomatic_en: Trying is the foundation of all knowledge and success.
commentary_en: Emphasises that trying is the foundation of all knowledge and achievement. Without attempting, one can never learn or succeed. Said to encourage those who hesitate to begin.
commentary_dg: Inasisitiza kukala kujeza ndiyo mudzi wa maarifa na mafanikio gosi. Bila kujeza, mutu kandadzifundza wala kufanikiwa. Husemwa kumtiya moyo mutu anayesita kuandza.
idiomatic_sw: Kujaribu ndio mwanzo wa kujua.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza kuwa kujaribu ndio msingi wa maarifa na mafanikio yote. Bila kujaribu, mtu hawezi kujifunza wala kufanikiwa. Husemwa kumtia moyo mtu anayesita kuanza.

literal_en: Be an “insect” so that you walk with your house
idiomatic_en: Be self-reliant and carry everything you need with you.
swahili: Kuwa kama konokono utembee na nyumba yako
commentary_en: chimehe-mehe is a type of grub which builds a cocoon and moves along with the cocoon around it. Rely entirely on yourself, don’t ask others for help.
commentary_dg: Chimehe-mehe ni mdudu ambaye anadzijengera ganda na kunyendeka nago. Methali ihi inafundza mutu adzitegemee mwenye, asitarajiye msada kpwa atu anjina.
idiomatic_sw: Jitegemee mwenyewe, usitegemee watu wengine.
commentary_sw: Chimehe-mehe ni aina ya mdudu anayejenga ganda na kutembea nalo. Jitegemee kabisa, usiombe msaada kwa wengine.

literal_en: Be with (him) and you will know his character
idiomatic_en: You only truly know someone by spending time with them.
commentary_en: Advises that true knowledge of a person's character comes only through close and sustained interaction. Surface impressions are unreliable — you must live with someone or spend real time with them to understand who they truly are.
commentary_dg: Inashauri kukala kumumanya mutu kpwa kpweli kunahitaji muda mure wa kukala naye. Maoni ga dzulu-dzulu tagategemeki — ni lazima ukale na mutu ili umumanye kpwa kpweli tabiyaze.
idiomatic_sw: Kumjua mtu ni kuishi naye.
commentary_sw: Inashauri kuwa kumjua mtu kwa kweli kunahitaji muda mrefu wa kuishi naye. Maoni ya juu juu hayategemeki — ni lazima ukae na mtu ili kumuelewa kwa kweli.

literal_en: A one-eyed guinea fowl scratches for a whole one
idiomatic_en: An incompetent person should not try to lead the competent.
swahili: Kanga wa jicho moja humfukurira mzima
commentary_en: USed when an incompetent person tries to lead a competent one.
commentary_dg: Husemwa mutu asiye na uwezo achijeza kutsongoza mutu ariye na uwezo. Kanga wa tsongo mmwenga kaweza kufukurira mzima.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na uwezo asijaribu kuongoza wenye uwezo.
commentary_sw: Hutumika mtu asiye na uwezo anapojaribu kuongoza mtu mwenye uwezo.

idiomatic_en: Reserve your tears for what truly matters, not for trivial losses.
commentary_en: Teaches that one should weep for one's mother — the person who truly matters — and not for a mere chick. Used to emphasise prioritising what is truly important over what is trivial.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala mutu amkanire mayaye — mutu anayemhusika kpwa kpweli — wala asimukanire mwana kuku. Husemwa kusisitiza kutanguliza mambo muhimu kuriko mambo madide.
idiomatic_sw: Heshimu mama yako zaidi ya kitu kingine chochote.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kuwa mtu amlie mama yake — mtu anayemhusika kweli — wala asimlie mwana kuku. Husemwa kusisitiza kutanguliza mambo muhimu kuliko mambo madogo.

literal_en: Cut your nose so that you make it beautiful
idiomatic_en: Sometimes you must sacrifice a little to preserve something greater.
commentary_en: A plea for reconciliation. If you owe a friend money, pay it back so that your friendship does not suffer. If you have argued with your parents, apologise, even if it hurts you a little to do this.
commentary_dg: Ni kuamba usalimishane. Ukiwa na deni kpwa msena, rihipha ili usenamwo usiharirike. Ukiwa umekosana na avyazio, kuomba msamaha ni bora, hata ikikuumiza.
idiomatic_sw: Wakati mwingine ni lazima ujitoe kidogo ili kulinda uhusiano.
commentary_sw: Ombi la maridhiano. Ukiwa na deni kwa rafiki, lipe ili urafiki wenu usiumie. Ukiwa umegombana na wazazi wako, omba msamaha, hata kama kunaumiza kidogo.

literal_en: A homestead does not return a corpse
idiomatic_en: Death is irreversible.
commentary_en: When a person dies they cannot come back
commentary_dg: Mutu achifwa, kaweza kuyuya tsena. Kufwa ni chitu ambacho tachiweza kugaluzwa. Kaya yayo ni ya kwake milele.
idiomatic_sw: Kifo hakina kurudi.
commentary_sw: Mtu akifa hawezi kurudi tena.

literal_en: Work is not eaten by termites
idiomatic_en: Hard work always pays off in the end.
commentary_en: You will benefit from your work
commentary_dg: Kazi yako tairiwa ni lutswa — kpwa maana undafaidika na kazi yako. Undapata matunda ga kazi yako siku zosi.
idiomatic_sw: Bidii yako haitapotea bure.
commentary_sw: Utafaidika na kazi yako.

literal_en: One slap, a goat and a cup of alcohol
idiomatic_en: A single slap calls for a goat and a drink in compensation.
swahili: Kofi moja, mbuzi na kikombe cha pombe
commentary_en: Something to do with celebrations
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inahusiana na mila za sherehe kpwa Adigo. Kofi mwenga unariphiwa kpwa mbuzi na kadzama ya uchi — ichikala ni fidia au sherehe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila tendo lina matokeo yake ambayo ni lazima yakabiliwe.
commentary_sw: Inahusiana na sherehe.

literal_en: Your brother’s war, neighbours should not enter it
idiomatic_en: Outsiders should not meddle in family disputes.
commentary_en: Warns outsiders against involving themselves in quarrels between relatives. Family disputes are private and have deep roots; an outsider who intervenes risks making things worse or being turned upon by both sides.
commentary_dg: Ni onyo kpwa ajeni asidziinjize kpwa kondo ya ndugu. Kondo ya anduguye ni ya siri na ina mizizi mire; mutu wa kunze anayeinjira anaweza kuhenda hali kukala mbii zaidi au kushambuliwa ni pande zosi mbiri.
idiomatic_sw: Jirani asiingilie ugomvi wa ndugu.
