commentary_sw: Inaonyesha onyo kwa wageni wasijiingize katika ugomvi wa ndugu. Ugomvi wa familia ni wa siri na una mizizi mirefu; mgeni anayeingilia anaweza kufanya hali kuwa mbaya zaidi au kushambuliwa na pande zote mbili.

digo: Kondo ya mlomo igololwa ni mlomo
literal_en: The war of the mouth is straightened by the mouth
idiomatic_en: Disputes caused by words should be resolved through dialogue, not violence.
swahili: Vita vya mdomo husuluhishwa kwa mdomo
commentary_en: A war of words can be solved by words. Disputes should not be solved through violence but through calm discussion
commentary_dg: Kondo ikiandzwa ni maneno, ni lazima iishe kpwa maneno. Migogoro taifwaha kutatuliwa kpwa kupigana, ela kpwa mazungumzo ga utulivu.
idiomatic_sw: Ugomvi wa maneno husuluhishwa kwa maneno, si kwa nguvu.
commentary_sw: Ugomvi wa maneno unaweza kutatuliwa kwa maneno. Migogoro isitatatuliwe kwa vurugu bali kwa mazungumzo ya utulivu.

literal_en: Your friend’s war is killing you
idiomatic_en: Getting involved in someone else's fight can get you killed.
commentary_en: The one who intervenes may be the one who gets killed. Don’t intervene.
commentary_dg: Uchiingira kondo ya myao, uwe ndiwe ndie ndaolagwa. Ni bora usiingire kondo zisizokuhusu.
idiomatic_sw: Usijingize katika ugomvi wa watu wengine.
commentary_sw: Anayeingilia anaweza kuwa ndiye anayeuawa. Usiingilie.

digo: Koo usumbirwa wima 123.Kpwala kaoya achioya anaocha
literal_en: You take action to make your clan upright
idiomatic_en: Take action to uplift your family when they are in need.
commentary_en: If a relative is sick you go around to make sure they get the best treatment so that they will get better (upright)
commentary_dg: Ndugu akiwa mkpwongo, ni lazima uzunguke kuhakikisha anapata matibabu mazuri ili apone. Koo yako usumbirwe kpwa kuterywa.
idiomatic_sw: Jitahidi kusaidia familia yako wakati wa shida.
commentary_sw: Ndugu akiwa mgonjwa unazunguka kuhakikisha anapata matibabu bora ili apone.

literal_en: There is talking, but not listening to one another
idiomatic_en: People hear advice but refuse to follow it.
commentary_en: People are given advice but do not act upon it
commentary_dg: Atu anapewa ushauri ela taasikira. Kuambirana kukuko, ela kuphokera na kuhenda kuhadikpwe takuna.
idiomatic_sw: Watu wanashauriwa lakini hawatekelezi.
commentary_sw: Watu wanapewa ushauri lakini hawaufuati.

literal_en: To get lost in the forest is to know the forest
idiomatic_en: You learn by making mistakes.
commentary_en: Encourages people not to fear mistakes, because errors are the truest teachers. One who has lost their way in the wilderness gains knowledge that the cautious traveller who stayed on the path never will.
commentary_dg: Inatiya moyo atu asiogophe makosa, mana makosa ndiyo alimu a kpweli. Mutu achiyeangamika nyika anaphaha maarifa ambago msafiri mwangalifu ariyesala njiani kandagaphaha katu.
idiomatic_sw: Kukosea ndiko kujifunza.
commentary_sw: Inatia moyo watu wasiogope makosa, kwa sababu makosa ndiyo walimu wa kweli. Mtu aliyepotea nyikani anapata maarifa ambayo msafiri mwangalifu aliyebaki njiani hatapata kamwe.

literal_en: Dying is helping each other
idiomatic_en: Life is about helping each other through hardship.
commentary_en: When you have a problem I help you and when I have a problem you help me
commentary_dg: Ukiwa na shida mimi ninakuterya, na mimi nikiwa na shida uwe unaniterya. Maisha ni kuteryahana kpwa utu wosi.
idiomatic_sw: Maisha ni kusaidiana wakati wa shida.
commentary_sw: Unapokuwa na shida mimi ninakusaidia na mimi ninapokuwa na shida wewe unamisaidia.

digo: Kuinjira ni mara mwenga, lakini kutuluka ni pore pore
literal_en: To enter happens immediately but to exit happens slowly
idiomatic_en: Getting into trouble is easy; getting out takes time.
swahili: Kuingia ni mara moja, lakini kutoka ni pole pole
commentary_en: Warns that it is easy to get into trouble, debt, or bad habits, but extracting oneself is slow and painful. Said to caution the impulsive or reckless before they commit to something difficult to undo.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala ni rahisi kudziinjiza matatoni, madeni, au tabiya mbii, ela kutuluka ni pore-pore na kpwa taabu. Husemwa kumuonya mutu asiyeaza kabila ya kudziinjiza kpwa dzambo gumu kurituluka.
idiomatic_sw: Ni rahisi kujitia matatani kuliko kujitoa.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kuwa ni rahisi kujitia matatani, madeni, au tabia mbaya, lakini kutoka ni polepole na kwa taabu. Husemwa kumwonya mtu asiyefikiri kabla ya kujitia katika jambo gumu kuondoka.

literal_en: To grow up is to wait for one another
idiomatic_en: Growing up means learning patience with one another.
commentary_en: Teaches that maturity means learning to wait for one another and to show patience. Growing up is not about speed but about understanding that life requires mutual waiting and accommodation.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala kukomaa ni kudzifundza kugodzana na kuvumiliana. Kukula si dzambo ra haraka ela ni kuelewa kukala maisha ganahitaji kugodzana na kuheshimiana.
idiomatic_sw: Kukomaa ni kujifunza kuvumiliana.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kuwa kukomaa ni kujifunza kusubiri na kuvumiliana. Kukua si jambo la haraka bali ni kuelewa kuwa maisha yanahitaji kusubiri na kuheshimiana.

literal_en: Grow up then come and see
idiomatic_en: Youth cannot fathom the burdens of adulthood.
commentary_en: said to young people concerning the responsibilites and burdens of adult life
commentary_dg: Husemwa kpwa adide kuhusu majukumu na mizigo ya maisha ga atu avyere. Kulani kpwandza, mundze mone mambo ga utu-uzima.
idiomatic_sw: Vijana hawawezi kuelewa mizigo ya utu uzima.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kwa vijana kuhusu majukumu na mizigo ya maisha ya watu wazima.

literal_en: Hurting a lot isn’t dying
idiomatic_en: Suffering greatly does not mean you will die from it.
commentary_en: Encourages endurance through suffering. Pain and hardship, however severe, are not the end. Said to comfort the sick, the grieving, or those undergoing trials, reminding them that survival is still within reach.
commentary_dg: Inatiya moyo kustahimili kulumwa. Kulumwa na shida, hata vikale vikali, si mwisho. Husemwa kumfariji mkpwongo, ariye na utsungu, au anayepitiya mitihani, kumkumbukiza kukala bado ana nafasi ya kuphola.
idiomatic_sw: Kuteseka sana haimaanishi kufa.
commentary_sw: Inatia moyo kustahimili maumivu. Maumivu na shida, hata yakiwa makali, si mwisho. Husemwa kumfariji mgonjwa, aliye na huzuni, au anayepitia mitihani, kumkumbusha kuwa bado ana nafasi ya kupona.

literal_en: A cowardly antelope has taken away his horns
idiomatic_en: Sometimes cowardice saves your life.
swahili: Kulungu mwoga amebeba pembe zake salama
commentary_en: The cowardly antelope runs away and hides when there is a fight, so he saves himself. Therefore it can be good to be a coward.
commentary_dg: Kulungu muoga anachimbira na kujificha wakati wa kondo, hivyo anadziokola. Wakati wanjina ni bora kukala muoga kuriko kudzihatarisha.
idiomatic_sw: Wakati mwingine woga unaweza kukuokoa.
commentary_sw: Kulungu mwoga anakimbia na kujificha wakati wa vita, hivyo anajiokoa. Kwa hivyo inaweza kuwa nzuri kuwa mwoga.

literal_en: To receive is to receive
idiomatic_en: What you receive is what you receive; accept it gratefully.
commentary_en: Teaches gratitude and acceptance. Whatever one receives, whether large or small, is a gift and should be accepted without complaint. Said to discourage greed and encourage contentment with what one has.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha shukrani na kukubali. Chochosi mutu anachophaha, chikale chikulu au chidide, ni zawadi na chinafwa kuphokerwa bila malalamiko. Husemwa kupinga tamaa na kutiya moyo kuridhika na urichonago.
idiomatic_sw: Kupokea chochote ni bora kuliko kutopokea.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha shukrani na kukubali. Chochote mtu anachopokea, kiwe kikubwa au kidogo, ni zawadi na kinastahili kupokelewa bila malalamiko. Husemwa kupinga tamaa na kutia moyo kuridhika na ulichonacho.

literal_en: Where there are cows there are no herders
idiomatic_en: Where there is wealth, there is no one willing to do the hard work.
commentary_en: Observes that where resources exist, there is often a shortage of people willing to do the work needed to tend them. Wealth without stewardship is wasted. Said to lament laziness or negligence in the face of opportunity.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kulaumu uzembe hata fursa iripo. Phatu phenye ng'ombe taphana mutu tayari kuarisa. Utajiri bila uangalifu hupotea — ni lazima kukale na mutu wa kuuhenda kazi.
idiomatic_sw: Mahali penye utajiri, hakuna aliye tayari kufanya kazi ngumu.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kulaumu uvivu au uzembe hata fursa ilipo. Mahali penye utajiri mara nyingi hakuna mtu tayari kufanya kazi ngumu ya kuutunza. Utajiri bila uangalifu hupotea.

digo: Kurima ni kushindana kuphaha ni kpwa Mlungu
literal_en: Farming is to have victory, to get is from God
idiomatic_en: We compete in effort, but the harvest comes from God.
swahili: Kulima ni kushindana, kupata ni kwa Mungu
commentary_en: Acknowledges that while human beings must compete and strive in their labours, the final outcome rests with God. Used to encourage effort while teaching humility — results are not entirely in our hands.
commentary_dg: Inatambua kukala binadamu ni lazima ashindane na kujitahidi kpwa kaziye, ela matokeo ga mwisho ni ga Mlungu. Husemwa kutiya moyo jitihada na kufundisha unyenyekevu — matokeo si mikononi mwehu heke.
idiomatic_sw: Binadamu hujitahidi lakini matokeo ni ya Mungu.
commentary_sw: Inatambua kuwa binadamu lazima ashindane na kujitahidi katika kazi yake, lakini matokeo ya mwisho ni ya Mungu. Husemwa kutia moyo jitihada huku ikifundisha unyenyekevu — matokeo si mikononi mwetu peke yetu.

literal_en: (to) grow upwards, it’s hot below
idiomatic_en: May you grow upward and prosper, for it is hard below.
commentary_en: Blessing, expression of thanks especially to a child
commentary_dg: Hino ni baraka husemwa hasa kpwa mwana. Ni maneno ga shukurani na heri: kurira dzulu, mana photsi ni kumoho na kuchizwa.
idiomatic_sw: Baraka kwa mtoto: ukue na kufanikiwa.
commentary_sw: Baraka na shukrani, hasa kwa mtoto.

literal_en: To look all around furtively is to keep yourself well equipped
idiomatic_en: Stay alert and resourceful to take care of yourself.
swahili: Kutazama huku na huku ni kujiandalia
commentary_en: Be aware. Use all means to look after yourself, e.g. work a shamba, run a business, make savings etc. lots of small things to ensure you don’t lack provision. Also, make sure you know what is going on around you.
commentary_dg: Kuwa macho na kutazama kpwa kpwila zosi. Tumia njira zosi kudzitunza — lima munda, henda biashara, weka akiba. Pia hakikisha unamanya gosi ganagohendeka phephi nawe.
idiomatic_sw: Kuwa makini na mazingira yako ni njia ya kujilinda.
commentary_sw: Kuwa macho. Tumia njia zote kujitunza, kama vile kulima shamba, kufanya biashara, kuweka akiba na kadhalika, ili usikose mahitaji. Pia, hakikisha unajua kinachoendelea karibu nawe.

digo: Kusagala bure si kama kunyendeka bure
literal_en: To sit for nothing is not like walking for nothing
idiomatic_en: It is better to wander than to sit idle.
swahili: Kukaa bure si kama kutembea bure
commentary_en: Encourages activity over idleness. Even aimless movement is better than sitting still doing nothing, because movement brings possibility and chance encounters. Said to motivate the idle to get moving.
commentary_dg: Inatiya moyo kuhenda badala ya kusagala bure. Hata kunyendeka bila lengo ni bora kuriko kusagala hivi, mana kunyendeka huleta fursa na mikutano ya bahati. Husemwa kumhimiza mutu asiyehenda chitu aandze kunyendeka.
swahili_equivalent: Kukaa bure si kama kutembea bure
idiomatic_sw: Ni bora kutembea bure kuliko kukaa bure.
commentary_sw: Inatia moyo kutenda badala ya kukaa bure. Hata kutembea bila lengo ni bora kuliko kukaa kimya, kwa sababu kutembea huleta fursa na mikutano ya bahati. Husemwa kumhimiza mtu asiyefanya kitu aanze kutembea.

literal_en: To be defeated by alcohol is shameful
idiomatic_en: It is disgraceful to be overcome by drink.
commentary_en: A rebuke directed at those who drink to excess and lose control of themselves. In Digo coastal society, self-restraint is valued; to be overcome by alcohol is considered deeply shameful.
commentary_dg: Ni kemeo kpwa mutu anayenwa kupita kiasi na kupoteza udhibiti. Kpwa Adigo a pwani, kudzidhibiti ni muhimu; kushindwa ni uchi ni chinemi — aibu kulu kpwa mutu yeyosi.
idiomatic_sw: Kulewa kupindukia ni aibu.
commentary_sw: Ni kemeo kwa mtu anayekunywa kupita kiasi na kupoteza udhibiti. Katika jamii ya Wadigo wa pwani, kujidhibiti ni muhimu; kulewa kupindukia kunaonekana kuwa aibu kubwa.

literal_en: Pretending cannot be healed
idiomatic_en: A pretender's illness cannot be cured.
commentary_en: If you are not really sick you cannot be treated. Those who fake illness to get out of working.
commentary_dg: Usipokala mkpwongo kpweli, huwezi kulagulwa. Husemwa kpwa atu adzihendzao akpwongo ili achimbirire kazi.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayejifanya mgonjwa hawezi kuponywa.
commentary_sw: Usipokuwa mgonjwa kweli huwezi kutibiwa. Husemwa kwa watu wanaojifanya wagonjwa ili wasifanye kazi.

digo: Kutana na indzi ni chironda kuphola
literal_en: Parting with flies means a sore is healing
idiomatic_en: When the flies leave, it means the wound is healing.
swahili: Kuachana na inzi ni kidonda kupona
commentary_en: Flies only come to an open wound
commentary_dg: Indzi zinahedza tu kpwa chironda chiricho wazi. Chironda chichiphola, indzi zinaphiya. Ni ishara ya kuphola.
idiomatic_sw: Ishara ya kupona ni pale matatizo yanapopungua.
commentary_sw: Inzi wanakuja tu kwenye kidonda kilicho wazi.

digo: Kutana na urindzi, ni kuolaga tumbiri mkare
literal_en: Parting with protection is to kill an old monkey
idiomatic_en: Removing your protector is like killing the goose that lays the golden eggs.
swahili: Kuachana na ulinzi ni kumuua tumbili mzee
commentary_en: cf. killing the goose which lays the golden eggs. The old monkey is the one who was providing the protection.
commentary_dg: Tumbiri mkare ndiye ariye anaterya — achiolagwa, urindzi unaisha. Ni dza kuolaga jogoo anayekupha mayai. Usidziphotsere urindzi wako.
idiomatic_sw: Usiondoe ulinzi wako, utajuta.
commentary_sw: Kama kumuua jogoo anayetaga mayai ya dhahabu. Tumbili mzee ndiye aliyekuwa akitoa ulinzi.

digo: Kuterya, takuangamika 143.Kuvweha sana siko kulatsa T
literal_en: Helping cannot be lost/destroyed o pull a lot is not to hit Whe rele Eg. has
idiomatic_en: Good deeds are never wasted.
commentary_en: Helpful deeds will be remembered n you go hunting you pull the string to ase the arrow, but you may miss the animal. if a man chooses a woman for his wife she to agree too, it’s not his choice alone.
commentary_dg: Matendo ga kuterya gandakumbukwa na tagaphotereka. Uchimuterya mutu, wema wako tauangamika.
idiomatic_sw: Matendo mema hayapotei kamwe.
commentary_sw: Matendo ya kusaidia yatakumbukwa.

literal_en: Commanding is not protection
idiomatic_en: Giving orders is not the same as actually protecting someone.
commentary_en: Distinguishes between authority and responsibility. Issuing commands from a distance is not the same as providing real protection. Said to criticise leaders who give orders but do not stand with their people in times of need.
commentary_dg: Inatofautisha kahi za uwezo na wajibu. Kulagiza kure si sawa na kurinda kpwa kpweli. Husemwa kukosoa vilongozi anaolagiza ela taasimama na atu ao wakati wa shida.
idiomatic_sw: Kuamrisha peke yake si sawa na kulinda.
commentary_sw: Inatofautisha kati ya mamlaka na wajibu. Kutoa amri kutoka mbali si sawa na kutoa ulinzi wa kweli. Husemwa kukosoa viongozi wanaotoa amri lakini hawasimami na watu wao wakati wa shida.

literal_en: (death) is in Longo and it’s with us Digos
idiomatic_en: Death spares no one, no matter where they are.
swahili: Kifo kiko Longo na kiko kwa Wadigo
commentary_en: Death is everywhere
commentary_dg: Kufwa kuko chila phatu — kpwa Longo na kpwa Adigo vivyo hivyo. Takuna mutu wala mudzi unaoweza kukwepa kufwa.
idiomatic_sw: Kifo kiko kila mahali, hakuna sehemu inayoepuka.
commentary_sw: Kifo kiko kila mahali.

literal_en: Legs are walkers
idiomatic_en: Your legs take you wherever you need to go.
commentary_en: If you meet someone in two places which are far apart it is because of their legs (otherwise they wouldn’t have got there)
commentary_dg: Ukimkutana mutu phatu phare, ni kpwa sababu ya maguluge. Magulu ndiyo ganagomtsukula mutu phatu phosi andaphokala anaphiya.
idiomatic_sw: Miguu ndiyo inayokupeleka popote.
commentary_sw: Ukimkutana mtu mahali mbali, ni kwa sababu ya miguu yake, vinginevyo asingefika.

digo: Mairi mairi Mwatunya wachimbirwa ni gari
literal_en: Two by two Mwatunya missed the bus
idiomatic_en: You cannot do two things at once without missing out on one.
swahili: Mbili mbili Mwatunya alipitwa na gari
commentary_en: The bus will always come when you have gone away for a moment and so you miss it. You can’t do two things at once.
commentary_dg: Gari rinahedza wakati umephiya kpwa muda, nawe unarikosa. Huwezi kuhenda mambo mairi kpwa wakati mmwenga bila kuphoterwa rimwenga.
idiomatic_sw: Ukijaribu kufanya mambo mawili kwa wakati mmoja, utapoteza moja.
commentary_sw: Gari litakuja wakati umeondoka kwa muda na hivyo utakosa. Huwezi kufanya mambo mawili kwa wakati mmoja.

digo: Makalo ga simba, ganagalwa-galwa ni nyani
literal_en: The lion’s mane, the baboons roll around on it
idiomatic_en: Even the fiercest person has a gentle side.
commentary_en: Even a fierce person can be gentle
commentary_dg: Hata mutu mukali anaweza kukala mpole. Simba ana ukali, ela makalo gakpwe nyani anagalwa-galwa — kuna upole hata kpwa achiye na nguvu.
idiomatic_sw: Hata mtu mkali anaweza kuwa mpole.
commentary_sw: Hata mtu mkali anaweza kuwa mpole.

literal_en: Good things are a road/way
idiomatic_en: Good deeds pave the way forward.
commentary_en: advice
commentary_dg: Manono — mambo manono — ndigo ganagokuphukira njdzira ya kunyendeka maishani. Henda manono, undaona njira.
idiomatic_sw: Matendo mazuri ndiyo yanayokuongoza mbele.
commentary_sw: Ushauri.

literal_en: A poor person has no ears
idiomatic_en: Nobody listens to a poor person.
commentary_en: everyone has ears, even a poor person (is this ironic?)
commentary_dg: Mchiya achigomba, takuna ariye ansikira. Atu taaheshimu maneno ga mutu asiye na chitu. Ela chila mutu ana masikiro — ni atu asinao ambao taamuphundza.
idiomatic_sw: Maneno ya maskini hayasikilizwi na watu.
commentary_sw: Kila mtu ana masikio, hata maskini (je, hii ni kejeli?).

literal_en: Sorrows are taken away one by one
idiomatic_en: Problems can be solved one at a time.
commentary_en: said to comfort someone who has many problems. They can be solved individually even if they appear many
commentary_dg: Husemwa kumuhurumisha mutu ariye na matatizo manji. Masono tagaweza kuisha gosi kpwa mara mwenga, ela ganaweza kuzolwa mwenga mwenga.
idiomatic_sw: Matatizo yanatatuliwa moja baada ya jingine.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kumfariji mtu mwenye matatizo mengi. Yanaweza kutatuliwa moja moja hata yakionekana mengi.

literal_en: Eyes have no fence/boundary
idiomatic_en: You cannot limit what the eyes can see.
commentary_en: Acknowledges that one cannot control what others see or notice. The eyes are free to look anywhere, and no wall or boundary can stop them. Said when someone has been caught observing something or when privacy is difficult to maintain.
commentary_dg: Inatambua kukala kuwezekana kudhibiti atu anavyoona. Matso gana uhuru wa kulola phatu phophosi, na taphana lichigo rinagoweza kugadzuiya. Husemwa mutu achigundulika akulola chitu au faragha ichishindikana.
idiomatic_sw: Macho hayazuiliki kutazama.
commentary_sw: Inatambua kuwa huwezi kudhibiti watu wanavyoona. Macho yana uhuru wa kutazama mahali popote, na hakuna ukuta au ua unaoweza kuyazuia. Husemwa mtu anapogundulika akitazama kitu au faragha inaposhindikana.

literal_en: Empty tears do not return a person sent away as a substitutionary payment for murder
idiomatic_en: Hollow tears cannot undo what has already been done.
commentary_en: Refers to the Digo practice of 'kore' — giving a person as substitutionary payment in murder cases. Empty, insincere tears cannot undo a grave act that has already been carried out. Said to caution against hollow regret after irreversible harm.
commentary_dg: Inahusiana na desturi ya Chidigo ya 'kore' — kulavya mutu dza malipo ga badala kpwa kesi za kuolaga. Matsozi mahuphu tagauya chitu chirichokpwisho kuhendwa. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya majuto mahuphu bada ya madhara gasigoudza.
idiomatic_sw: Kulia bure hakurudishi kilichopotea.
commentary_sw: Inahusiana na desturi ya Kidigo ya 'kore' — kutoa mtu kama malipo ya badala katika kesi za mauaji. Machozi matupu na ya uongo hayawezi kurudisha kitendo kizito kilichokwisha kufanywa. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya majuto matupu baada ya madhara yasiyorudishika.

literal_en: Your friend’s mother is your mother too
idiomatic_en: Treat your friend's mother with the same respect as your own.
swahili: Mama wa rafiki yako ni mama yako pia
commentary_en: You should respect her just as you respect your mother
commentary_dg: Ni lazima umuheshimu mayo wa msena wako dza unavyomuheshimu mayoyo mwenye. Heshima kpwa mayo ni heshima kpwa mutu yosi.
idiomatic_sw: Mheshimu mama wa rafiki yako kama mama yako mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Unapaswa kumheshimu kama vile unavyomheshimu mama yako.

literal_en: Funeral contributions do not rot
idiomatic_en: The generosity you show at funerals will be returned to you.
commentary_en: Contributions given as a sign of condolence. When you are bereaved people will bring you gifts just like you did for them
commentary_dg: Michango ya mazikpwa tagaola. Uwe uchimphira mutu mazikiro, wakativyo uwe uchifwirwa, atu nao nandakuphira dza urivyohenda.
idiomatic_sw: Wema unaofanya wakati wa msiba utarudishwa kwako.
commentary_sw: Michango inayotolewa kama ishara ya rambirambi. Utakapofiwa watu watakuletea zawadi kama ulivyowafanyia.

digo: Mbona unanikundza-kundza dza chingo ya mbega?
literal_en: Why do you fold me up like the skin of a colobus?
idiomatic_en: Why do you keep manipulating me?
swahili: Mbona unanipindisha kama ngozi ya mbega?
commentary_en: A complaint from someone who feels they are being manipulated or exploited repeatedly. The colobus monkey's skin was folded and used for various purposes; the speaker likens themselves to that skin, bent and shaped at another's will.
commentary_dg: Ni malalamiko ga mutu anayehisi anadanganywa au kutumirwa mara kpwa mara. Chingo ya mbega kala inakundzwa na kutumirwa kpwa madhumuni ganji; msemadzi anadzilinganisha na chingo iyo, achipindzishwa kpwa matakwa ga munyawe.
idiomatic_sw: Kwa nini unaendelea kunidanganya na kunibadilisha?
commentary_sw: Ni malalamiko ya mtu anayehisi anadanganywa au kutumiwa mara kwa mara. Ngozi ya mbega ilikunjwa na kutumika kwa madhumuni mbalimbali; msemaji anajifananisha na ngozi hiyo, akipindishwa kwa matakwa ya mwingine.

literal_en: Your friend’s goat is meat
idiomatic_en: People care less about what belongs to others.
swahili: Mbuzi ya rafiki yako ni nyama tu
commentary_en: You don’t have any emotional attachment to it so are quite happy to eat it
commentary_dg: Mbuzi ya msena wako ni nyama tu kpwako — kuna uhusiano wa kihisia kpwa mwenye, si kpwako. Mutu taana huruma na mali ya mutu mwinjine dza ya kwakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Mali ya mtu mwingine haijalishi sana machoni mwako.
commentary_sw: Huna uhusiano wa kihisia nayo, kwa hivyo unafurahi kuila.

literal_en: A woman is not seduced by crying
idiomatic_en: A man who cries will not win a woman's heart.
commentary_en: If a man is crying a woman won’t be impressed by him and will think him weak
commentary_dg: Mlume achirira, mchetu taonere heshima na andamona ni dhaifu. Mlume ni lazima akale na nguvu mbere za mchetu.
idiomatic_sw: Mwanamume anayelia haoni heshima kwa mwanamke.
commentary_sw: Mwanamume akilia mwanamke hataona heshima kwake na atadhani ni dhaifu.

digo: Mchira wa ng’ondzi unariwa ni kala 160.Mdide msena, mvyere msena A
literal_en: The sheep’s tail is eaten by a fox young friend, an elderly friend
idiomatic_en: Good things are sometimes wasted on those who don't deserve them.
swahili: Mkia wa kondoo unaliwa na mbweha
commentary_en: The sheep’s tail contains lots of fat. A good thing is often just wasted. Eg. if a beautiful big woman is married to a small ugly man.
commentary_dg: Mchira wa ng'ondzi una mafuta manji — ni chitu chinono. Ela mara nyinji chitu chinono chinaharirika — dza mchetu muzuri achiolerwa ni mlume mdide.
idiomatic_sw: Kitu kizuri mara nyingi kinapotezwa kwa mtu asiyestahili.
commentary_sw: Mkia wa kondoo una mafuta mengi. Kitu kizuri kinapotezwa. Kwa mfano, mwanamke mzuri akiolewa na mwanamume mdogo mbaya.

digo: Mdigo kataki cha myawe chivyale patsa
literal_en: A Digo person doesn’t like his companion to have twins
idiomatic_en: People are jealous when their neighbor is doubly blessed.
commentary_en: A proverb about envy among peers. When one person is doubly blessed — symbolised by twins — their neighbour becomes jealous rather than happy. This is said with self-awareness; the Digo acknowledge this tendency among themselves as a human weakness to be resisted.
commentary_dg: Methali kuhusu wivu kahi za atu. Mutu mmwenga achiphaha baraka mairi — dza ishara ya patsa — jiraniye anaona wivu badala ya furaha. Adigo anaikiri tabiya ihi dza udhaifu wa chibinadamu ufwao kupingwa.
idiomatic_sw: Watu huwa na wivu jiran apatapo baraka zaidi yao.
commentary_sw: Methali kuhusu wivu miongoni mwa watu. Mtu mmoja anapopata baraka maradufu — kama ishara ya mapacha — jirani wake anaona wivu badala ya furaha. Husemwa kwa kujitambua; Wadigo wanakiri tabia hii kama udhaifu wa kibinadamu unaopaswa kupingwa.

digo: Mfupha uchishinda fisi diya kauturya/ Mfupha uchishinda fisi diya undauweza kuphi?
literal_en: If a bone defeats a hyena a dog cannot eat it (either)
idiomatic_en: If the stronger one fails, the weaker one has no chance.
commentary_en: the hyena is stronger than the dog
commentary_dg: Fisi ana nguvu kuriko diya. Mfupha uchishinda fisi, diya kandauweza bii. Achiye na nguvu achishindwa, ariye na udhaifu zaidi kandaweza.
idiomatic_sw: Kama mwenye nguvu ameshindwa, dhaifu hataweza zaidi.
commentary_sw: Fisi ana nguvu kuliko mbwa.

literal_en: You kill Mgala but pay him his dues
idiomatic_en: Even those you despise deserve to be paid their dues.
swahili: Mgala muuwe lakini haki yake mpe
commentary_en: Even if you despise those below you (eg. your employees) you must still give them what is due to them.
commentary_dg: Hata ukimchukiya mutu, ni lazima umphe haki yakpwe. Mgala muolage, ela vyakpwe — haki yakpwe — muphe. Haki ya mutu ni ya Mlungu.
idiomatic_sw: Hata ukimchukia mtu, mpe haki yake.
commentary_sw: Hata ukiwadharau walio chini yako (kama wafanyakazi wako) lazima uwape haki yao.

digo: Mgala-gala na pwa karya wari muhuphu
idiomatic_en: The braggart who boasts endlessly ends up with nothing to show for it.
swahili: Mgala-gala na pwa kala wali muhuphu
commentary_en: Said of a boastful person who talks big but ends up with nothing. The braggart who keeps promising great feasts ends up eating plain rice with no accompaniment. A warning against empty boasting.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kpwa mutu anayedzigamba ela mwishowe anasala bila chitu. Mutu wa kugala-gala na pwa mwishowe anarya wari muhuphu. Ni onyo dhidi ya majivuno mahuphu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayejisifu mwishowe anajionea aibu.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kwa mtu anayejigamba lakini mwishowe anabaki bila kitu. Mjigambi anayeahidi karamu kubwa mwishowe anakula wali tupu. Ni onyo dhidi ya majivuno matupu.

literal_en: A medicine man does not treat himself
idiomatic_en: No one can solve their own problems alone.
commentary_en: Observes that even a healer cannot diagnose or treat their own ailments. Everyone needs help from others, no matter how skilled they are. Said to encourage people to seek help and to remind the proud that self-sufficiency has limits.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala hata mganga kamanya kudzilagula mwenye. Chila mutu anahitaji msaada wa anjina, haidhuru ana ujuzi chiasi gani. Husemwa kumtiya moyo mutu atafute msaada na kumkumbukiza mwenye kiburi kukala kudzitegemea kuna miphaka.
idiomatic_sw: Mganga hawezi kujitibu mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kuwa hata mganga hawezi kujitibu mwenyewe. Kila mtu anahitaji msaada wa wengine, haidhuru ana ujuzi kiasi gani. Husemwa kumtia moyo mtu atafute msaada na kumkumbusha mwenye kiburi kuwa kujitegemea kuna mipaka.

literal_en: The divider is still at the distribution point
idiomatic_en: God is still distributing blessings; your turn will come.
swahili: Mgawanyi bado yuko mahali pa kugawanya
commentary_en: said to comfort people who are in need. They will receive something eventually. Mganyi usually refers to God
commentary_dg: Mganyi — kpwa kawaida ni Mlungu — achere matuni anaganya. Usikate tamaa, mana wakativo nao undaphokera fungu rako.
idiomatic_sw: Mungu bado anagawa; subiri zamu yako.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kumfariji watu wenye uhitaji. Watapata kitu hatimaye. Mganyi kwa kawaida anamaanisha Mungu.

literal_en: Towns do not hate each other
idiomatic_en: Communities should live in peace with one another.
commentary_en: gonerana - old form. If they do hate each other there will be problems. People should love one another
commentary_dg: Midzi ikigonerana kundakala na matatizo. Atu a midzi tofauti-tofauti ni lazima aishi kpwa amani na kupendana.
idiomatic_sw: Watu wanapaswa kupendana na kuishi kwa amani.
commentary_sw: Gonerana - neno la zamani. Wakichukiana kutakuwa na matatizo. Watu wanapaswa kupendana.

literal_en: If a palm tappers wine is praised he usually adds water to it
idiomatic_en: Too much praise can spoil a good thing.
swahili: Mpanda mnazi akisifiwa pombe yake huongeza maji
commentary_en: If you are praised too much you may end up spoiling everything
commentary_dg: Mjema achisifiwa uchi wakpwe, anaweza kuongeza madzi ili apate manji zaidi. Kusifiwa kupita chipimo kunaweza kuharibu chitu chinono.
idiomatic_sw: Sifa nyingi mno zinaweza kuharibu kitu kizuri.
commentary_sw: Ukisifiwa kupita kiasi unaweza kuharibu kila kitu.

digo: Mjeni aphokerwa ndata, ela matso anago mwenye
literal_en: A visitor has his staff taken, but he (still) has his eyes
idiomatic_en: A guest may be welcomed politely, but they still see everything.
swahili: Mgeni anashikwa fimbo, lakini macho anayo mwenyewe
commentary_en: Superficial politeness (taking a visitor’s staff inside) does not mean that other (bad) behaviour will not be noticed
commentary_dg: Mjeni aphokerwa ndata — ni adabu ya dzulu-dzulu. Ela matso anago mwenye, na anaona gosi — tabia mbaya na tabia nono. Adabu ya kpweli ni ya moyoni.
idiomatic_sw: Heshima ya nje haifichi tabia mbaya ndani ya nyumba.
commentary_sw: Adabu ya juu juu (kumchukua mgeni fimbo yake) haimaanishi tabia nyingine (mbaya) haitaonekana.

literal_en: A visitor comes, the inhabitant should be healed
idiomatic_en: A guest's arrival benefits the whole household.
commentary_en: When a visitor comes good food must be prepared, which will benefit all. The visitor may have brought food too
commentary_dg: Mjeni achifika, chakurya chinono chinaandikpwa, na chosi atu a kaya nao anarya. Mjeni anaweza kukala amelera na chakurya pia. Kufika kpwa mjeni ni baraka kpwa osi.
idiomatic_sw: Ujio wa mgeni huleta baraka kwa nyumba nzima.
commentary_sw: Mgeni akifika lazima chakula kizuri kiandaliwe, ambacho kitawanufaisha wote. Mgeni pia anaweza kuwa ameleta chakula.

literal_en: A visitor is rain
idiomatic_en: A guest, like rain, comes and goes.
commentary_en: Rain doesn’t last for ever, the sun comes out again. In the same way a visitor will be with you for a while and will then go.
commentary_dg: Mvula taidumu milele — dzuwa rinatsupa. Vivyo hivyo mjeni andakala nawe kpwa muda, kisha andaphiya. Mukaribishe, ela kumanya andaphiya.
idiomatic_sw: Mgeni, kama mvua, hukaa kwa muda mfupi kisha kuondoka.
commentary_sw: Mvua haidumu milele, jua litaibuka tena. Vivyo hivyo mgeni atakuwa nawe kwa muda kisha ataondoka.

literal_en: A hunter doesn’t get tired, when he gets tired he roasts
idiomatic_en: A true hunter never rests until the hunt is done.
commentary_en: The hunter only roasts when he has caught something. If you want something, don’t get tired and give up. Only rest when you have got what you want.
commentary_dg: Mkala wa kpweli karemwa hata achikala anazola kpwa muda mure. Achiremwa, ni mana akaphata kitu — na ndipho anaocha. Ukitaka chitu, usikate tamaa hadi uchipate.
idiomatic_sw: Usikate tamaa hadi ufikie lengo lako.
commentary_sw: Mwindaji anaoka tu anapopata mnyama. Ukitaka kitu, usichoke na kukata tamaa. Pumzika tu utakapopata unachotaka.

idiomatic_en: The one who leads today will follow tomorrow; fortune is always turning.
commentary_en: Teaches that roles and fortunes are cyclical — the one who was ahead will eventually fall behind, and the one behind will come forward. Said to encourage patience and humility, as no one's position is permanent.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala nafasi na bahati ni za mzunguko — ariye mbere ndakala nyuma, na ariye nyuma ndakala mbere. Husemwa kutiya moyo uvumilivu na unyenyekevu, mana nafasi ya mutu si ya kudumu.
idiomatic_sw: Aliyetangulia ndiye atakayefuata; kila mtu ana zamu yake.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kuwa nafasi na bahati ni za mzunguko — aliye mbele atakuwa nyuma, na aliye nyuma atakuja mbele. Husemwa kutia moyo uvumilivu na unyenyekevu, kwa kuwa nafasi ya mtu si ya kudumu.

idiomatic_en: Even the most undesirable person will find someone who accepts them.
commentary_en: Even the worst or most unappealing person will find someone who accepts them. Just as a bad tree will still attract its monkeys, everyone has their match or their place. Said to comfort the rejected or to caution against despising others.
commentary_dg: Hata mutu mui au asiyevutiya andaphaha mutu wa kumkubali. Dza vira mkpwadzu mui undaphaha tumbiriwe, chila mutu ana myawe au phatu phakpwe. Husemwa kumfariji ariyekataliwa au kuonya dhidi ya kudharau anjina.
idiomatic_sw: Hata mti mbaya utapata tumbiri wake.
commentary_sw: Hata mtu mbaya au asiyevutia atapata mtu wa kumkubali. Kama vile mti mbaya bado utapata tumbiri wake, kila mtu ana mwenzake au mahali pake. Husemwa kumfariji aliyekataliwa au kuonya dhidi ya kuwadharau wengine.

literal_en: A medicine man’s wife does not get thin A sick person has no friend W ar
idiomatic_en: The healer's household never goes hungry; but a sick person bears their pain alone.
swahili: Mke wa mganga hanenepi; mgonjwa hana rafiki
commentary_en: A medicine man always gets lots of money and gifts ho will help you to carry the pain when you e sick? You have to bear it yourself
commentary_dg: Mkpwaza mganga siku zosi anapata pesa na zawadi nyinji, kpwa hivyo kaonda. Na mkpwongo kana msena wa kpweli — maumivu gako ni gako macheye.
idiomatic_sw: Mganga hupata utajiri lakini mgonjwa lazima avumilie peke yake.
commentary_sw: Mganga hupata pesa na zawadi nyingi. Ni nani atakayekusaidia kubeba maumivu unapokuwa mgonjwa? Lazima uyavumilie mwenyewe.

literal_en: A sick person is the granary of the medicine men
idiomatic_en: A sick person is the medicine man's livelihood.
commentary_en: Healers depend on the sick for their livelihood. Without illness, there would be no need for medicine men. Said to observe the irony that one person's misfortune is another's opportunity, particularly in the context of traditional healing.
commentary_dg: Aganga anategemea akpwongo kpwa riziki yao. Bila ukpwongo, taukungekala na haja ya aganga. Husemwa kueleza kukala msiba wa mutu mmwenga ni fursa ya munyawe, hasa kpwa uganga wa jadi.
idiomatic_sw: Mgonjwa ndiye chanzo cha mapato ya mganga.
commentary_sw: Waganga wanategemea wagonjwa kwa riziki yao. Bila ugonjwa, hakungekuwa na haja ya waganga. Husemwa kueleza kinzani kuwa msiba wa mtu mmoja ni fursa ya mwingine, hasa katika muktadha wa uganga wa jadi.

literal_en: An empty hand is not licked
idiomatic_en: No one values a person who has nothing to offer.
commentary_en: A person who has nothing to offer will not attract attention or respect. People are drawn to those who can provide something — generosity earns goodwill. Said to motivate productivity or to explain why the poor are often overlooked.
commentary_dg: Mutu asiye na chochosi cha kulavya taphahi heshima wala umakini. Atu huvutwa ni atu anaoweza kulavya chitu — ukarimu hudzengera heshima. Husemwa kuhimiza uzalishaji au kueleza kpwa utu maskini mara kpwa mara hupuuzwa.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na chochote cha kutoa hapati heshima.
commentary_sw: Mtu asiye na chochote cha kutoa hapati heshima wala umakini. Watu huvutiwa na wale wanaoweza kutoa kitu — ukarimu hujenga heshima. Husemwa kuhimiza uzalishaji au kueleza kwa nini maskini mara nyingi hupuuzwa.

literal_en: A long piece of rafia does not tie firewood
idiomatic_en: Having more than you need does not guarantee results.
commentary_en: Having abundant resources does not guarantee that a task will be accomplished. A long piece of raffia may seem sufficient, but if it is poorly used, it will not tie the firewood. Quality and skill matter more than quantity.
commentary_dg: Kukala na rasilimali nyinji takuhakikishi kazi indahendwa. Mlala mure unaweza kuonekana wa kutosha, ela ukitumirwa vibaya, taufunga kuni. Ubora na ujuzi ni muhimu zaidi ya unji.
idiomatic_sw: Wingi wa rasilimali hauhakikishi mafanikio.
commentary_sw: Kuwa na rasilimali nyingi hakuhakikishi kazi itafanyika. Ukili mrefu unaweza kuonekana wa kutosha, lakini ukitumika vibaya, hautafunga kuni. Ubora na ujuzi ni muhimu zaidi ya wingi.

literal_en: One who sleeps with ‘ngoyo’, when they wake up they do it.
idiomatic_en: One who sleeps with a plan in mind will carry it out at dawn.
commentary_en: If you go to bed with plans in your head you carry them out as soon as you wake up
commentary_dg: Mutu achilala na mipango mumo moyoni, achivyuka asubuhi anaisuna pho hipho. Ngoyo ni nia, na nia ndiyo inayokutsukula kpwa matendo.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye azimio atalitekeleza mara tu anapopata nafasi.
commentary_sw: Ukilala na mipango kichwani mwako utaitekeleza mara tu unapoamka.

literal_en: One who licks honey doesn’t just lick once
idiomatic_en: Once you taste something sweet, you will always want more.
commentary_en: Once a person has tasted something good, they will inevitably return for more. Said to observe human nature's tendency toward desire and repetition — what pleases us draws us back again and again.
commentary_dg: Mutu achilamba asali, kandalamba lumwenga heke. Husemwa kuonyesha tabiya ya chibinadamu ya kutamani na kurudhiya — chinachohufurahisha chinahuvuta tsona na tsona.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu akionja kitu kizuri, atataka zaidi.
commentary_sw: Mtu akionja kitu kizuri, ataendelea kurudi. Husemwa kuonyesha tabia ya kibinadamu ya kutamani na kurudia — kinachotufurahisha kinatuvuta tena na tena.

literal_en: If a ‘bird’ gets burnt it is roasting ‘rambo’
idiomatic_en: When a dark bird gets burnt, it looks like it's roasting something.
swahili: Mnazi mmoja hauna pombe ya kutosha
commentary_en: black type of bird
commentary_dg: Mlamba ni aina ya nyuni mweusi. Achiphya, ni dza anaochera rambo — kpwa sababu rangirakpwe ri dza ra kuchwa kare.
swahili_equivalent: Mnazi mmoja hauna pombe ya kutosha
idiomatic_sw: Hata kitu kibaya kinaweza kuonekana kina thamani katika hali fulani.
commentary_sw: Aina ya ndege mweusi.

literal_en: Blood doesn’t run away from each other or leave each other
idiomatic_en: Blood ties can never be broken.
commentary_en: Blood is thicker than water. You will always be attached to your relatives.
commentary_dg: Mlatso — damu — ni nzito kuriko madzi. Ukoo wako tauweza kuchimbirana wala kurichana. Undahusiana na ndugu zako hata kama kuna migogoro.
idiomatic_sw: Uhusiano wa damu hauvunjiki kamwe.
commentary_sw: Damu ni nzito kuliko maji. Utahusiana na ndugu zako milele.

digo: Mlume wangu waochera muuyu arye vidzogavye
literal_en: My husband has roasted for a baobab (so that) he can eat its mushrooms
idiomatic_en: One does a favor expecting something in return.
swahili: Mume wangu ameokea muuyu ale uyoga wake
commentary_en: The parasitic mushrooms will be destroying the tree so removing them is good for the tree, but they also make good food. Someone does a favour for someone so that they will gain also
commentary_dg: Mutu anahenda wema ili apate faida pia. Mlume waochera muuyu ili arye vidzoga — anaondoa vidzoga ambavyo vinaharibu muhi, ela naye anarya. Kuterya si kpwa bure tu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu hufanya wema kwa matarajio ya kupata faida pia.
commentary_sw: Uyoga wa vimelea utaharibu mti, kwa hivyo kuuondoa ni nzuri kwa mti, lakini pia ni chakula kizuri. Mtu hufanya wema ili apate faida pia.

literal_en: God sleeps alone
idiomatic_en: God's ways are beyond human understanding.
commentary_en: He has his own ways and thoughts which cannot be understood or known
commentary_dg: Mlungu ana njira zakpwe na mawazo gakpwe ambago tagaweza kueleweka wala kumanyikana ni mutu. Alala macheye — mawazo gakpwe ni gakpwe macheye.
idiomatic_sw: Njia za Mungu hazieleweki kwa binadamu.
commentary_sw: Ana njia zake na mawazo yake ambayo hayawezi kueleweka au kujulikana.

literal_en: God does not give to you and then take from you
idiomatic_en: God's gifts are permanent; He does not take back what He gives.
commentary_en: God does not take back his gifts to you
commentary_dg: Mlungu achikupha chitu, takakuhika tsena. Zawadi za Mlungu ni za milele. Usiogophe kukala Mlungu andakuusa urizophewa.
idiomatic_sw: Mungu harudishi zawadi zake.
commentary_sw: Mungu harudishi zawadi alizokupa.

idiomatic_en: God does not harm His own faithful servant.
commentary_en: Affirms that God does not strike or harm His own servant. Used to express trust in divine protection and to comfort someone who fears misfortune, reminding them that God is merciful to those who are faithful.
commentary_dg: Inathibitisha kukala Mlungu katsupha mujawe mwenye. Husemwa kuonyesha imani ya urindzi wa Mlungu na kumfariji mutu anayeogopa msiba, kumkumbukiza kukala Mlungu ni mwenye rehema kpwa aaminifu.
idiomatic_sw: Mungu hamdhuru mja wake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Inathibitisha kuwa Mungu hampigi mja wake mwenyewe. Husemwa kuonyesha imani katika ulinzi wa Mungu na kumfariji mtu anayeogopa msiba, kumkumbusha kuwa Mungu ni mwenye rehema kwa waaminifu.

idiomatic_en: What is already ruined cannot serve as the seed for something new.
commentary_en: A papaya tree that is rotting has seeds that are no good for planting. What is corrupted or ruined cannot be used as the basis for something new. Said to warn against building on a flawed foundation or trusting damaged goods.
commentary_dg: Mnaphaya-phaya unaoharibika makopa gagikpwe tagaikpwa mbeyu. Chitu chirichoharibika tachiweza kukala mudzi wa chitu chiphya. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya kudzenga dzulu ya mudzi wenye kasoro au kutegemea chitu chirichoharibika.
idiomatic_sw: Kitu kilichoharibika hakifai kupandwa tena.
commentary_sw: Mpapai unaoharibika mbegu zake hazifai kupandwa. Kitu kilichoharibika hakiwezi kuwa msingi wa kitu kipya. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya kujenga juu ya msingi wenye kasoro au kutegemea kitu kilichoharibika.

literal_en: What palm wine do you get from one palm tree?
idiomatic_en: One person alone cannot accomplish much; cooperation is needed.
commentary_en: One palm tree does not have much wine. You must work together
commentary_dg: Mnazi mmwenga tauna uchi munji. Kpwa hivyo ni lazima atu ahendekazi phamwenga — mutu mmwenga macheye kaweza kuhenda manji.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mmoja peke yake hawezi kutimiza mengi; ushirikiano unahitajika.
commentary_sw: Mnazi mmoja hauna pombe nyingi. Lazima mfanye kazi pamoja.

digo: Mnazi wa mchetu taujemwa na ngaro/ndaro
literal_en: A woman’s coconut palm isn’t tapped with joy
idiomatic_en: Be careful when using what belongs to a woman; she will demand it back.
swahili: Mnazi wa mwanamke haupandwi kwa furaha
commentary_en: A woman/wife is very jealous of her own possessions. Don’t rejoice when taking/using her things. She will want a refund
commentary_dg: Mchetu ana wivu na mali yakpwe. Mnazi wakpwe taujemwa na ndaro — usifurahi uchitumiya vitu vya mchetu, mana ndakutaka ariphe.
idiomatic_sw: Ukitumia mali ya mwanamke, jitayarishe kulipa.
commentary_sw: Mwanamke/mke ana wivu sana na mali yake. Usifurahi unapochukua/kutumia vitu vyake. Atataka kulipwa.

digo: Mndzala kana masikiri 182b. Mndzala kana masikiro
literal_en: A hungry person has no followers A hungry person has no ears
idiomatic_en: A hungry person cannot focus on anything else.
commentary_en: Either no-one will follow a hingry person, or a hungry person has no intereszt in anything except food
commentary_dg: Mutu ariye na mndzala kana masikiro — taasikira chochosi isiphokala chakurya. Mndzala inafunga masikiro ga mutu na kumuhenda asifikire chitu chinjina.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye njaa hawezi kusikiliza wala kufuata mtu.
commentary_sw: Ama hakuna atakayemfuata mtu mwenye njaa, au mtu mwenye njaa hana hamu ya kitu chochote isipokuwa chakula.

literal_en: A hungry person can not bury his mother
idiomatic_en: Extreme hunger makes a person unable to plan for the future.
swahili: Mwenye njaa hawezi kumzika mama yake
commentary_en: When you are hungry, food is more important to you than anything else, even paying for your mother’s funeral. You won’t save up any money for the future but will eat all you have
commentary_dg: Ukiwa na mndzala, chakurya ni muhimu kuriko chitu chochosi — hata kuzika nine. Mutu wa mndzala tarya chosi arizhonacho na taweka akiba kpwa siku za mbere.
idiomatic_sw: Njaa inazuia mtu kupanga mambo ya baadaye.
commentary_sw: Unapokuwa na njaa, chakula ni muhimu zaidi kwako kuliko kitu kingine chochote, hata kulipa mazishi ya mama yako. Hutaweka akiba bali utala ulivyonavyo vyote.

idiomatic_en: Once bitten by a snake, a person fears even a harmless rope.
commentary_en: Someone who has been bitten by a snake becomes wary of anything that resembles a snake, even a harmless vine. Past trauma makes a person overly cautious. Said to explain fearful or distrustful behaviour rooted in a painful experience.
commentary_dg: Mutu ariyeng'atwa ni nyoka nkudziondoka hata ling'ongo rinagolandana na nyoka. Chiwewe cha kare chinamuhenda mutu kukala mwangalifu kupita kiasi. Husemwa kueleza tabiya ya hofu au kutoamini inayotokana na uzoefu wenye kulumwa.
idiomatic_sw: Aliyeumwa na nyoka huogopa hata kitu kinachofanana na nyoka.
commentary_sw: Mtu aliyeumwa na nyoka huogopa hata kamba inayofanana na nyoka. Kiwewe cha zamani humfanya mtu kuwa mwangalifu kupita kiasi. Husemwa kueleza tabia ya hofu au kutoamini inayotokana na uzoefu wenye maumivu.

digo: Moho tauvyala moho 195.Moyo wa mutu ni tsaka
literal_en: Fire does not give birth to fire The heart of a person is a forest
idiomatic_en: Destruction does not breed creation; the human heart is unfathomable.
swahili: Moto hauzai moto; moyo wa mtu ni msitu
commentary_en: It gives birth to ash, it destroys
commentary_dg: Moho tauvyala moho, unavyala jivu; unabananga. Moyo wa mutu ni tsaka kpwa sababu kaweza kumanya go gosi garigo ndani.
idiomatic_sw: Uharibifu hauzai mema, na moyo wa mtu haueleweki.
commentary_sw: Moto hauzai moto bali majivu; unaharibu.

literal_en: A stupid person does not become straight
idiomatic_en: A stubborn fool never changes their ways.
commentary_en: A stupid person is stubborn and will never change their ways or opinions
commentary_dg: Mpamba ni mutu mukaidi, kandagaluka wala kubadilisha maazo gakpwe. Uchimuonya kandakusikira.
idiomatic_sw: Mpumbavu ni mkaidi na hatabadilika kamwe.
commentary_sw: Mpumbavu ni mkaidi na hatabadilisha njia zake au maoni yake kamwe.

idiomatic_en: Put your own house in order before meddling in the affairs of others.
commentary_en: One who plays the 'tsetse' board game begins with their own pieces before touching others'. Said to teach that one should put their own affairs in order before meddling in the business of others.
commentary_dg: Mphakusa tsetse aandza na yakpwe mwenye kabila ya kugusa ya anjina. Husemwa kufundisha kukala mutu aike mambo gakpwe sawa kpwandza kabila ya kuinjira mambo ga anjina.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu huanza na mambo yake mwenyewe kabla ya kuingilia ya wengine.
commentary_sw: Mcheza tsetse huanza na zake mwenyewe kabla ya kugusa za wengine. Husemwa kufundisha kuwa mtu aweke mambo yake sawa kwanza kabla ya kuingilia mambo ya wengine.

literal_en: He who walks carefully does not stumble
idiomatic_en: Caution prevents mistakes.
commentary_en: Praises the virtue of caution and deliberate action. The person who moves carefully and thoughtfully avoids obstacles that trip up the hasty. Said to encourage patience and care in all undertakings.
commentary_dg: Inasifiya fadhila ya uangalifu na kuhenda kpwa makini. Mutu aphiyaye pore kaepuka vikpwazo vinavyomkpwamisha mutu wa haraka. Husemwa kutiya moyo uvumilivu na uangalifu kpwa mambo gosi.
idiomatic_sw: Utaratibu na uangalifu hukuzuia makosa.
commentary_sw: Inasifiya fadhila ya uangalifu na kutenda kwa makini. Mtu anayetembea kwa uangalifu huepuka vikwazo vinavyomkwamisha mtu wa haraka. Husemwa kutia moyo uvumilivu na uangalifu katika mambo yote.

idiomatic_en: One who habitually betrays others reveals themselves to be a traitor.
commentary_en: One who severs ties with many people is a traitor. A person who betrays or cuts off relationship after relationship reveals their own treacherous character. Said to warn against habitual disloyalty.
commentary_dg: Mutu anayerema uhusiano na atu anji ni mtsai. Mutu anayesaliti au kurema uhusiano mara kpwa mara anadhihirisha tabiya yakpwe ya utsai. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya uhaini wa mazoea.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayewakata watu wengi ni msaliti.
commentary_sw: Mtu anayekata uhusiano na watu wengi ni msaliti. Mtu anayesaliti au kukata uhusiano mara kwa mara anadhihirisha tabia yake ya usaliti. Husemwa kuonya dhidi ya uhaini wa mazoea.

digo: Mrya chakpwe chitsanga kana kondo na mutu
idiomatic_en: One who quietly earns their own living has no quarrel with anyone.
swahili: Mlaji chake chitsanga hana ugomvi na mtu
commentary_en: A person who quietly minds their own business and earns their living by their own effort has no quarrel with anyone. Self-sufficiency and contentment with one's own lot bring peace. Said to praise the quiet, hardworking person.
commentary_dg: Mutu anayerya chakpwe chitsanga na kudziphahira riziki kpwa jitihada yakpwe kana kondo na mutu yeyosi. Kudzitegemea na kuridhika na urichonago huleta amani. Husemwa kumsifu mutu mnyenyekevu na mwenye bidii.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayejitahidi kwa chake mwenyewe hakosei na mtu.
commentary_sw: Mtu anayejishughulisha na mambo yake mwenyewe na kujipatia riziki kwa jitihada yake hana ugomvi na mtu yeyote. Kujitegemea na kuridhika na ulichonacho huleta amani. Husemwa kumsifu mtu mnyenyekevu na mwenye bidii.

literal_en: A person who lives a bad life is not imitated
idiomatic_en: No one follows the example of someone who lives badly.
commentary_en: Said of someone whose bad behaviour serves as a warning rather than an example. People observe those who live poorly and choose a different path.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kpwa mutu ambaye mahendo gakpwe mabaya ganahenderwa dza onyo badala ya mfano. Atu huona mutu asagalaye vii na kuchagula njira tofauti.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayeishi maisha mabaya hafuatwi na mtu.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kuhusu mtu ambaye tabia yake mbaya hutumika kama onyo badala ya mfano. Watu huangalia wanaoishi vibaya na kuchagua njia tofauti.

idiomatic_en: Speaking the truth does not make you guilty of the wrong you name.
commentary_en: Used to defend someone who speaks the truth or points out wrongdoing. Speaking honestly about a matter does not make one guilty of that matter.
commentary_dg: Hutumika kutetea mutu anayeamba ukpweli au kuonyesha makosa. Mutu achisema ngambi, siyo sawa na mutu achiyehenda kosa hiro.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayesema ukweli si lazima awe na hatia.
commentary_sw: Hutumika kutetea mtu anayesema ukweli au kuonyesha makosa. Kusema ukweli kuhusu jambo hakumfanyi mtu kuwa mhalifu.

literal_en: The one whosews the clothes should try his own waist
idiomatic_en: Practice what you preach before judging others.
commentary_en: A reminder that one should test advice or judgement on oneself before applying it to others. The tailor who does not try his own garments has no right to judge the fit on others.
commentary_dg: Ni ukumbusho kpwa mutu kukala ni lazima ajeze ushauri kpwa nafsi yakpwe kpwandza kabila ya kuuhendeza kpwa atu anjina. Mshona nguwo asiyejeza chunuchakpwe kana haki ya kuhukumu anjina.
idiomatic_sw: Kabla ya kumhukumu mwingine, jaribu kwanza wewe mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Ukumbusho kwamba mtu anapaswa kujaribu ushauri au hukumu kwake mwenyewe kwanza kabla ya kuutumia kwa wengine. Mshonaji asiyejaribu nguo zake hana haki ya kuhukumu wengine.

idiomatic_en: Even a bystander can get caught in the crossfire.
commentary_en: A warning that bystanders are not immune from consequences. Even one who merely watches can end up being harmed, so it is better to take an active role or stay well away.
commentary_dg: Onyo kukala mutu anayetsedza tu si salama. Hata mtsedza anaweza kupigbwa dzengoni, kpwa hivyo ni bora kushiriki au kutsama kure kabisa.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayetazama tu bila kujiingiza anaweza kuathirika pia.
commentary_sw: Onyo kwamba watazamaji hawako salama kutokana na matokeo. Hata mtu anayetazama tu anaweza kuumizwa, kwa hivyo ni bora kushiriki au kukaa mbali kabisa.

literal_en: Rejoice at a carcass, don’t rejoice at a track
idiomatic_en: Don't celebrate until the task is truly done.
commentary_en: Applied to humans. Don’t think you’ve killed your enemy until the deed is actually done
commentary_dg: Ndarira ihi inahumirwa kpwa atu. Usifikiri ukamala kazi hadi ichikala ikakamilika kpweli kpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Usisherehekee kabla jambo halijamalizika kweli.
commentary_sw: Inatumika kwa wanadamu. Usidhani umemwangamiza adui yako hadi kitendo kimefanyika kweli.

digo: Mtsimba mbira naye piya aendza atsimbirwe
literal_en: The person who digs a grave also wants to have a grave dug for him (i.e. to be decently buried)
idiomatic_en: Those who serve others also expect to be served in return.
swahili: Mchimba kaburi naye pia anataka achimbiwe
commentary_en: Speaks to the reciprocal nature of communal service, especially around burial customs. The person who digs graves for others also expects that when their time comes, the community will do the same for them.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi ni kuhusu kusaidiana kpwa jamii, hasa mambo ga mazishi. Mutu anayetsimba mbira kpwa atu anjina anatarajia kukala jamii yakpwe nayo indamutsimbira wakati wakpwe ukifika.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayewahudumia wengine naye pia anataka kuhudummiwa.
commentary_sw: Inahusu asili ya usaidizi wa pamoja, hasa kuhusu mila za mazishi. Mtu anayechimba makaburi kwa wengine anatarajia kwamba jamii itamfanyia vivyo hivyo wakati wake utakapofika.

literal_en: The one who goes out to receive a visitor cannot be ignored
idiomatic_en: Do not disrespect the one who comes to welcome you.
commentary_en: You should not ignore your host
commentary_dg: Mutu achiyekutsolokerera kpwako usimberwe. Mwenye nyumba ni mutu wa ishima, naye aifwahiwe.
idiomatic_sw: Usimpuuze mtu anayekupokea kwa ukarimu.
commentary_sw: Usimpuuze mwenyeji wako.

literal_en: The breadwinner doesn’t die, the servant isn’t killed
idiomatic_en: A provider must look after himself first so his family can survive.
commentary_en: He has to look after himself first so that he has energy and resources to provide for his family. If he neglects himself the whole family will suffer.
commentary_dg: Mtsumi ni lazima adzitunze kpwandza ili akale na nguvu za kulisha nyumba yakpwe. Achidzi-dza-haza [?], nyumba yosi indateseka.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayetegemewa na wengine lazima ajilinde kwanza.
commentary_sw: Lazima ajitunze kwanza ili awe na nguvu na rasilimali za kuwapatia familia yake. Akijipuuza familia nzima itateseka.

literal_en: The breadwinner is not killed nor does he die
idiomatic_en: The one everyone depends on must be protected at all costs.
commentary_en: Emphasises the importance of the family provider or breadwinner. The community recognises that the person who feeds others must be protected and cared for, because without them the whole household suffers.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inasisitiza umuhimu wa mtsumi wa kaya. Jamii inatambua kukala mutu anayerisha atu anjina ni lazima arindziwe, mana bila iye kaya yosi indateseka.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayewalisha wengine hapaswi kupotezwa.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza umuhimu wa mlezi wa familia. Jamii inatambua kwamba mtu anayewalisha wengine lazima alindwe na kutunzwa, kwa sababu bila yeye kaya nzima inateseka.

idiomatic_en: The rich and the poor alike each carry their own burdens.
swahili: Mlishi na mali yake, mnyonge naye ana yake
commentary_en: A reminder that every person, whether wealthy or poor, has their own burdens and resources. The rich provider has responsibilities, and the humble person has their own modest means and dignity.
commentary_dg: Ukumbusho kukala chila mutu, mtsumi au mnyonge, ana mzigo wakpwe mwenye. Mtsumi ana mali na wajibu, mnyonge naye piya ana ga kwakpwe na heshima yakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu ana mzigo wake, tajiri na maskini.
commentary_sw: Ukumbusho kwamba kila mtu, tajiri au maskini, ana mzigo wake mwenyewe. Tajiri ana wajibu wake, na mnyonge ana mali yake ndogo na heshima yake.

digo: Mtsuzi wa ndenje unewa/uriwa wa moho
literal_en: The sauce of an (old) he-goat is taken/eaten when hot
idiomatic_en: Deal with important matters right away before they go bad.
swahili: Mchuzi wa beberu uliwa ukali moto
commentary_en: The sauce will smell bad if not eaten straight away. An important matter has to be dealt with immediately and not left until later.
commentary_dg: Mtsuzi wa ndenje uchiricha kunukia vibaya. Dzambo muhimu rinahaza kushughulikiwa phapho kpwa phapho, usirichise.
idiomatic_sw: Jambo muhimu lishughulikiwe mara moja, lisije likaharibika.
commentary_sw: Mchuzi utanuka ukiachwa. Jambo muhimu lazima lishughulikiwe mara moja wala lisiahirishwe.

literal_en: The sweetness of tea is sugar
idiomatic_en: Something good needs the right ingredient to make it truly enjoyable.
commentary_en: Used to highlight what makes something good or valuable. Just as sugar is the key ingredient that makes tea enjoyable, every good thing has an essential element that gives it its quality.
commentary_dg: Hutumika kuonyesha chitu chinachohenda chitu chinjina kuwa kizuri. Dza vira sukari ndiyo ihendayo chai kuwa ntswano, chila chitu chizuri china siri ya ubora wakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila kitu kizuri kina siri ya utamu wake.
commentary_sw: Hutumika kuonyesha kile kinachofanya kitu kuwa kizuri au chenye thamani. Kama vile sukari ndio inayofanya chai kuwa tamu, kila kitu kizuri kina siri ya ubora wake.

literal_en: He who is told (something) in conversation has his own thing to say
idiomatic_en: Everyone you speak to has their own story to tell.
commentary_en: If someone tells you something then you give your opinion, or own story
commentary_dg: Uchimuambira mutu dzambo, naye andakupha maazo gakpwe ama hadithi yakpwe. Chila mutu ana go gamuhusu.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu ana maoni yake na hadithi yake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Ukimwambia mtu jambo, naye atatoa maoni yake au hadithi yake.

literal_en: (if) the trap stands up it is to rest its back b et
idiomatic_en: Losing a burden can be a relief in disguise.
commentary_en: A trap is ‘bent over’ when it is set. Eg. if some of your employees leave you are freed from the duty of paying them. This is a relief if you are urdened by too many workers/wives/children c. to support.
commentary_dg: Muhambo uchifyuka unaphumuzisha mongo. Mfwano, ahendadzi-kazi achiuka unaachiwa mzigo wa malipo gao. Ihi ni faraja kpwa ariye na atu anji a kuatereza.
idiomatic_sw: Kupoteza mzigo wakati mwingine ni faraja.
commentary_sw: Mtego unainama unapowekwa. Kwa mfano, baadhi ya wafanyakazi wakiondoka unaachiwa mzigo wa malipo yao. Hii ni faraja ukiwa na watu wengi wa kutegemea.

literal_en: The one who does good is eaten by bad people
idiomatic_en: Kind people are often taken advantage of by the ungrateful.
commentary_en: A lament about the unfairness of life. The person who does good for others is often the one exploited or betrayed by those with bad intentions. Said to warn the generous to be cautious.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi ni kuhusu udhalimu wa maisha. Mutu ahendaye manono kpwa atu anjina ndiye ariwa ni aphuphi. Husemwa kuonya mutu mwenye moyo wa ukarimu akale makini.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwema mara nyingi hutumiwa vibaya na watu wabaya.
commentary_sw: Kilio kuhusu udhalimu wa maisha. Mtu anayewatendea wengine mema mara nyingi ndiye anayeliwa na watu wabaya. Husemwa kuonya wenye moyo wa ukarimu wawe makini.

literal_en: If a tree defeats a baboon, the monkey cannot (climb it either)
idiomatic_en: If the expert fails, don't expect the amateur to succeed.
commentary_en: the baboon is a better climber than the monkey
commentary_dg: Nyani ni mpandadzi mubomu kuriko tumbiri. Muhi uchimshinda nyani, tumbiri kauweza kpwa vyosi vyosi.
idiomatic_sw: Kama stadi ameshindwa, usitarajie mwanafunzi afanikiwe.
commentary_sw: Nyani ni mpandaji bora kuliko tumbili.

literal_en: A tree falls on those who are near
idiomatic_en: Those closest to a problem suffer the most when it strikes.
commentary_en: When a problem comes, those who are closest suffer most.
commentary_dg: Tatizo richikudza, ariye phephi na ro ndio anaogopha kpwandza. Muhi ugbwerera phatu phakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Watu wa karibu ndio wanaopata madhara zaidi tatizo linapotokea.
commentary_sw: Tatizo linapokuja, walio karibu zaidi ndio wanaoteseka zaidi.

literal_en: A messenger is not killed
idiomatic_en: Don't punish the messenger for the message they carry.
commentary_en: A universal principle protecting messengers and intermediaries. The person who carries a message should not be punished for its content, even if the news is unwelcome.
commentary_dg: Kanuni ya kulinda ahumwa na aphirikira maneno. Mutu ahumirwaye ujumbe kaadhibiwa kpwa maneno garigo mumo, hata gakale ni habari mbii.
idiomatic_sw: Usimwadhibu mjumbe kwa habari anazoleta.
commentary_sw: Kanuni ya ulimwengu inayolinda wajumbe na wapatanishi. Mtu anayebeba ujumbe hapaswi kuadhibiwa kwa yaliyomo, hata kama habari hiyo haipendezi.

literal_en: The thief, catch him with the ear (of corn)
idiomatic_en: Don't accuse someone of wrongdoing without proof.
commentary_en: Don’t accuse someone of being a theif without proof. Catch his red-handed
commentary_dg: Usimshitaki mutu kukala muivi bila ushaidi. Mgbwire na suche mkononi ndipho undamanya ni kpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Usimshtaki mtu wizi bila ushahidi.
commentary_sw: Usimshtaki mtu kuwa mwizi bila ushahidi. Mkamte akiwa na mali mkononi.

literal_en: The one who sees a monitor lizard is the trapper
idiomatic_en: The one who identifies the problem is best suited to solve it.
commentary_en: A monitor lizard is hard to spot, so only a skilled person can catch one. Similarly, the one who sees a solution to a problem is the best person to implement it or explain it to others.
commentary_dg: Mbulu ni nyama mgumu kuonwa, kpwa hivyo mutu stadi macheye ndiye anayeweza kuihumbula. Vivyo hivyo, mutu aonaye suluhisho ra tatizo ndiye mubomu wa kuritimiza.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayeona tatizo ndiye anayefaa kulisuluhisha.
commentary_sw: Kenge ni mgumu kuonekana, kwa hivyo ni mtu stadi peke yake anayeweza kumkamata. Vivyo hivyo, mtu anayeona suluhisho la tatizo ndiye bora zaidi wa kulitekeleza.

literal_en: The one who lets the animal/meat rot is the one who owns the trap
idiomatic_en: Neglecting your responsibilities leads to waste and loss.
commentary_en: If a trapper is lazy and doesn’t check his traps often the animal might decay. A person who doesn’t pull his weight may be dismissed from his job. If you don’t tend your crops you will not have a good harvest.
commentary_dg: Muhumbuladzi mvivu asiyeangalira mihambo yakpwe, nyama inaweza kuoza. Mutu asiyehenda kazi yakpwe vizuri anaweza kufutswa. Usiphotunza mimera yako kundapata mavuno mazuri.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiyetunza mali yake ndiye anayeipoteza.
commentary_sw: Mtegaji mvivu asiyeangalia mitego yake mara kwa mara, mnyama huoza. Mtu asiyefanya kazi yake vizuri anaweza kufukuzwa. Usipotunza mazao yako hutapata mavuno mazuri.

literal_en: A person can be told while he is returning, he cannot be told while he is going
idiomatic_en: People only listen to advice after they have already failed.
commentary_en: Someone determined to do a certain thing will not listen to advice until they have run into difficulties
commentary_dg: Mutu achiyeamua kuhenda dzambo fulani kandasikira ushauri hadi achikpwama na matatizo. Ela achikala anauya, ndipho anamanya kusikira.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu hasikii ushauri hadi ajikwae mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Mtu aliyeamua kufanya jambo fulani hatasikia ushauri hadi apate matatizo.

literal_en: A person is told that, he isn’t told this
idiomatic_en: Some things are better left unsaid in certain company.
commentary_en: A guide to social discretion. There are certain things that may be said to a person and other things that should not be mentioned directly. Wisdom lies in knowing the difference.
commentary_dg: Mwongozo wa busara kpwa maongezi. Kuna mambo ganagoweza kuambwa mutu na ganye gasigofwa kuambwa mwenga kpwa mwenga. Achili ni kumanya tofauti hiyo.
idiomatic_sw: Kuna mambo fulani yanayofaa kusemwa na mengine hayafai.
commentary_sw: Mwongozo wa busara katika maongezi. Kuna mambo fulani yanayoweza kusemwa kwa mtu na mengine yasiyopaswa kusemwa moja kwa moja. Hekima iko katika kujua tofauti.

literal_en: A personn is saved by his own
idiomatic_en: In the end, only your own efforts can save you.
commentary_en: Emphasises self-reliance. Ultimately, a person's salvation comes from their own effort and resources. Others may help, but your own effort is what truly saves you.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inasisitiza kudzitegemea. Kpwa kpweli mutu aokolwa ni chakpwe mwenye. Atu anjina anaweza kusaidiya, ela juhudi zako mwenye ndizo zinazokuokola.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu huokolewa na juhudi zake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza kujitegemea. Hatimaye, mtu huokolewa na juhudi na rasilimali zake mwenyewe. Wengine wanaweza kusaidia, lakini juhudi yako ndiyo inayokuokoa kweli.

literal_en: A mad person is not imitated
idiomatic_en: Never follow a bad example, no matter who sets it.
commentary_en: Don’t follow any sort of bad example
commentary_dg: Usifuate mfwano wowosi mbaya. Mutu wa koma ana tabiya mbaya, nawe uchimuiga undakala dza iye.
idiomatic_sw: Usifuate mfano mbaya wa mtu yeyote.
commentary_sw: Usifuate mfano wowote mbaya.

literal_en: A baobab is covered by undergrowth
idiomatic_en: Even a great leader needs supporters to uphold their status.
commentary_en: A ‘big-man’ needs his entourage to support him, who add to his prestige.
commentary_dg: Muuyu mkpwulu unahaza kutsitsizwa ni mitsatsa midide inayouzunguka. Mutu mkpwulu anahaza atu akpwe a kumzunguka ambao anamphera ishima.
idiomatic_sw: Kiongozi mkubwa anahitaji wafuasi wake ili kudumisha hadhi yake.
commentary_sw: Mtu mkubwa anahitaji wasaidizi wake wanaomzunguka, wanaompa heshima.

literal_en: The rain is waited for with a cleared plot
idiomatic_en: Prepare in advance so you are ready when opportunity comes.
commentary_en: So that the crops can be planted immediately. Plan ahead.
commentary_dg: Panga kazi yako mapema ili uchikpwedza mvula ukale tayari kupanda. Mutu wa achili anadzitayarisha kabla ya wakati.
idiomatic_sw: Jitayarishe mapema ili uwe tayari fursa inapokuja.
commentary_sw: Ili mazao yapandwe mara moja mvua inaponyesha. Panga mambo mapema.

digo: Mvumirizi agodzera rakpwe/ Mvumirizi naagodze gakpwe
literal_en: The one who perseveres waits for his own
idiomatic_en: Be careful how you comfort others, for you may face the same hardship someday.
commentary_en: If someone is in a hard situation we should be careful how we comfort them. If you tell someone not to worry and don’t take their problems seriously, you may react differently when the same thing happens to you.
commentary_dg: Ukale mwangalifu unavyomfariji mutu ariye kpwenye hali ngumu. Uchimuambira asijali na usizingatie matatizo gakpwe kpwa uzito, unaweza kuhenda vivyo kpwako ukale tafwauti.
idiomatic_sw: Uwe makini unavyomfariji mtu, kwani unaweza kupitia hali ile ile siku moja.
commentary_sw: Mtu akiwa katika hali ngumu tuwe waangalifu jinsi tunavyomfariji. Ukimwambia mtu asijali na usizingatie matatizo yake kwa uzito, unaweza kuitikia tofauti jambo kama hilo likitokea kwako.

literal_en: The one who destroys a country is a citizen
idiomatic_en: A nation is destroyed from within by its own people.
commentary_en: If we want development in our country we should take action ourselves and not wait for outsiders (missionaries/NGOs etc.) to come and do it for us. If we don’t do it ourselves we are destroying our country.
commentary_dg: Huchilonda maendeleo kpwenye tsi yehu, lazima huhende wenyewe. Husipohenda wenyewe, naswi ndio hunaoibananga tsi yehu. Usigodzere atu a kunze edze ahuhendere.
idiomatic_sw: Nchi inaharibiwa na watu wake wenyewe.
commentary_sw: Tukitaka maendeleo katika nchi yetu tunapaswa kuchukua hatua wenyewe na tusibaki kusubiri wageni waje watufanyie. Tusipofanya wenyewe tunaiangamiza nchi yetu.

literal_en: You can’t sing songs about a singer
idiomatic_en: You cannot outwit someone at their own game.
commentary_en: Said of someone who tries to outwit an expert in their own field. You cannot best a singer with song, just as you cannot fool a specialist with tricks from their own domain.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kpwa mutu anayejeza kumshinda mtaalamu kpwa fani yakpwe mwenye. Mvwini kavwirwa — huwezi kumshinda mutu kpwa mambo gakpwe mwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kumshinda mtu katika fani yake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kuhusu mtu anayejaribu kumshinda mtaalamu katika fani yake. Huwezi kumshinda mwimbaji kwa wimbo, kama vile huwezi kumdanganya mtaalamu kwa ujanja wa fani yake.

literal_en: A parent does not eat to their satisfaction
idiomatic_en: A good parent always sacrifices personal comfort for the sake of the children.
commentary_en: They will put the needs of the children first.
commentary_dg: Mvyazi anavika mahitaji ga ana mbere ya gakpwe mwenye. Kanarya hadi achishiba kpwa sababu ana akpwe ndio kpwandza.
idiomatic_sw: Mzazi mwema hujitolea kwa ajili ya watoto wake.
commentary_sw: Mzazi huweka mahitaji ya watoto mbele ya yake mwenyewe.

idiomatic_en: Both parents and the childless bear their own hardships; no life is free from struggle.
commentary_en: Both the parent and the childless person face their own struggles. Parenthood brings joy but also heavy burdens; childlessness brings its own kind of grief. Neither life is without hardship.
commentary_dg: Mvyazi na mgumba osi ana changamoto zao. Kuvyala kunareha furaha ela kpwa piya mzigo mzito; kutokukala na mwana kunareha utsungu wakpwe. Maisha ga kpweli ni kondo kpwa chila mutu.
idiomatic_sw: Mzazi na asiye na mtoto wote wana changamoto zao.
commentary_sw: Mzazi na mtu asiye na mtoto wote wana changamoto zao. Uzazi huleta furaha lakini pia mzigo mzito; kutokuwa na mtoto huleta aina yake ya huzuni. Hakuna maisha yasiyo na ugumu.

digo: Mvyere mtsape mairo, ela kumshinda marifwa
literal_en: You can run past an old person, but you won’t beat his wisdom
idiomatic_en: Youth may be faster, but age holds greater wisdom.
swahili: Mzee mshinde mbio, lakini huwezi kumshinda hekima
commentary_en: A proverb honouring the wisdom of elders. Young people may have speed and physical strength, but they cannot surpass the experience and knowledge that comes with age.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inaheshimu achili ya avyere. Adide anaweza kukala na mairo na nguvu za mwiri, ela hawawezi kushinda marifwa ga mutu mvyere.
idiomatic_sw: Kijana anaweza kuwa na nguvu, lakini hekima ya mzee haishindiki.
commentary_sw: Methali inayoheshimu hekima ya wazee. Vijana wanaweza kuwa na kasi na nguvu za mwili, lakini hawawezi kushinda uzoefu na maarifa yanayokuja na umri.

digo: Mwadinda dinda mwenye utsi tauhendera mutu
literal_en: You should work hard yourself, the public won’t do anything for a person
idiomatic_en: Work hard for yourself because nobody else will do it for you.
commentary_en: A call to self-motivation and personal industry. No one from the public will come to do your work for you; success depends on your own hard effort.
commentary_dg: Wito wa kudzidinda mwenye na kuhenda bidii. Utsi tauna mutu ndayekpwedzera kukuhendeza kazi yako; mafanikio ganategemea dinda rako mwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Fanya bidii mwenyewe, kwani watu hawatakufanyia kazi yako.
commentary_sw: Wito wa kujitahidi na bidii ya kibinafsi. Hakuna mtu kutoka kwa umma atakayekuja kukufanyia kazi yako; mafanikio yanategemea juhudi zako mwenyewe.

literal_en: ‘Mwadziko’, the same tomorrow
idiomatic_en: May today's blessings be repeated tomorrow.
commentary_en: Blessing, expression of thanks
commentary_dg: Neno ra baraka na kushukuru. Uchiphaha vitu vizuri rero, unaomba vigale vivyo muhondo.
idiomatic_sw: Baraka za leo na zijirudie kesho.
commentary_sw: Baraka, neno la kushukuru.

literal_en: ‘Mwaligulu’, the matter of the owner
idiomatic_en: The owner of a task must work harder than those helping.
commentary_en: If I have something to do I should work harder than those who are helping him. e.g. if you have asked people to help in your shamba.
commentary_dg: Mwenye dzambo ndiye anapaswa kuhenda bidii kuriko osi anaosaidiya. Mfwano, ukiita atu edze akusaidiye mundani, uwe wa kpwandza kuhenda kazi.
idiomatic_sw: Mwenye kazi lazima afanye bidii zaidi kuliko wasaidizi wake.
commentary_sw: Ukiwa na kazi ya kufanya unapaswa kufanya bidii zaidi kuliko wanaokusaidia, kwa mfano ukiwauliza watu wasaidie shambani kwako.

idiomatic_en: Everyone you share news with already has a story of their own to tell.
commentary_en: Acknowledges that everyone who is told news already has their own perspective or story. You cannot simply tell someone something and expect them to have no response or opinion of their own.
commentary_dg: Chila mutu achiyeambirwa ng'andzi ana zakpwe piya za kuamba. Huwezi kumuambira mutu habari na kutarajia kukala kana jibu au maoni ga kwakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu aliyeambiwa jambo ana la kwake pia la kusema.
commentary_sw: Inatambua kwamba kila mtu anayeambiwa habari tayari ana mtazamo wake au hadithi yake. Huwezi kumwambia mtu jambo na kutarajia hana jibu au maoni yake mwenyewe.

digo: Mwana-mbuzi aphyere-aphyere phakpwe kpwandza, ndipho akakurya-kurya kpwa myawe
literal_en: The goat kid sweeps his own compound first then eats at his frind’s place
idiomatic_en: Take care of your own responsibilities before seeking favors elsewhere.
swahili: Mwana mbuzi husafisha kwake kwanza, kisha hula kwa jirani
commentary_en: Teaches children and adults alike to tend to their own home and duties before going elsewhere. The young goat sweeps its own compound before eating at the neighbour's, symbolising that personal responsibility comes first.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inafundza ana na atu akpwulu kukala ni lazima uhende mambo ga kwako kpwandza kabila ya kuphiya kpwa atu anjina. Mwana-mbuzi aphyere phakpwe kpwandza, ndipho akakurya kpwa myawe.
idiomatic_sw: Timiza wajibu wako kwanza kabla ya kutafuta msaada kwa wengine.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha watoto na watu wazima kutunza nyumba na wajibu wao kwanza kabla ya kwenda mahali pengine. Mwana mbuzi husafisha kwake kwanza kabla ya kula kwa jirani, kuashiria kuwa wajibu wa kibinafsi unakuja kwanza.

literal_en: An abnormal child cannot be hidden
idiomatic_en: Some things cannot be concealed no matter how hard you try.
commentary_en: Some truths or conditions are too conspicuous to hide. A child with a visible difference cannot be concealed, and similarly, certain situations cannot be covered up no matter what effort is made.
commentary_dg: Kuna ukpweli au hali ambazo ni dhahiri sana kufwitsika. Mwana ariye na chijego kaona kufwitsika, dza vira mambo ganjina tagaweza kusetiriwa hata ujeze kpwa nguvu zosi.
idiomatic_sw: Mambo fulani hayawezi kufichwa hata ujaribu kiasi gani.
commentary_sw: Baadhi ya ukweli au hali ni dhahiri sana kufichwa. Mtoto mwenye tofauti inayoonekana hawezi kufichwa, na vivyo hivyo, hali fulani haziwezi kusetiriwa hata kwa juhudi kiasi gani.

literal_en: If a child wets your thigh don’t cut it off
idiomatic_en: Don't overreact to small offenses; learn to forgive.
commentary_en: The problem of a child wetting your thigh does not require drastic action (amputation). Therefore forgive people who wrong you
commentary_dg: Mwana achikukodzerera chiga, usichikahe. Tatizo dide ra mwana arihaze kushughulikiwa na uvumirizi, si kpwa ukali. Asamehe atu anaokukosera.
idiomatic_sw: Usijibu kwa hasira kubwa kosa dogo; jifunze kusamehe.
commentary_sw: Tatizo la mtoto kukukojoa paja halihitaji hatua kali kama kukata mguu. Kwa hivyo wasamehe watu wanaokukosea.

literal_en: A well-cared for child does not lack
idiomatic_en: A child who is properly cared for will not go without.
commentary_en: Encourages attentive child-rearing. A child who is raised with proper care, guidance, and provision will not lack the essentials of life. Good parenting yields good outcomes.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inahimiza malezi manono ga ana. Mwana achiyererwa na utunzaji na mwongozo mzuri kakosa makungu ga maisha. Malezi manono ganareha matokeo manono.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto anayetunzwa vizuri hatakosa chochote muhimu.
commentary_sw: Inahimiza malezi mazuri ya watoto. Mtoto anayelelewa kwa utunzaji, mwongozo, na mahitaji yake hatakosa mambo muhimu ya maisha. Malezi mazuri huleta matokeo mazuri.

literal_en: The child who listens is not told many (times)
idiomatic_en: An obedient child only needs to be told once.
commentary_en: This child obeys and only needs to be told something once
commentary_dg: Mwana msikizi anahii na anahaza kuambirwa dzambo mara mwenga tu. Kaphoserwa mara nyinji.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto anayetii huhitaji kuambiwa mara moja tu.
commentary_sw: Mtoto huyu hutii na anahitaji kuambiwa jambo mara moja tu.

literal_en: The child who is willing to run errands does a lot of poos
idiomatic_en: A willing helper is always well rewarded.
commentary_en: because he will be rewarded by being given lots of food
commentary_dg: Mwana apendzaye kuhumikiwa andaripwa kpwa kuphewa chakurya chinji. Ndiyo mana mavige gakpwe ni manji.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayejitoa kusaidia hulipwa vizuri.
commentary_sw: Kwa sababu atalipwa kwa kupewa chakula kingi.

literal_en: A child is something to borrow for
idiomatic_en: A child can be used to gain sympathy and ask for help.
swahili: Mtoto ni kitu cha kuomba nacho
commentary_en: You use your child as a sympathy-tool when you need to ask for things/beg
commentary_dg: Unaweza kuhumira mwana wako ili uphate huruma unapohaza kuomba vitu kpwa atu. Mwana ni chifwadho cha kuomba nacho.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto anaweza kutumika kupata huruma na msaada kutoka kwa wengine.
commentary_sw: Unaweza kutumia mtoto wako kama chombo cha kupata huruma unapohitaji kuomba vitu.

literal_en: The child at the side has no guardian
idiomatic_en: A child with no family connection will not be looked after as well.
commentary_en: Refers to a child who isn’t related to you and is just around. He won’t be taken care of in the same way as children you know.
commentary_dg: Mwana ambaye kana uhusiano nawe, ariye hiko kanda tu, kandatunzwa dza ana unaomanya. Mrezi wa kpweli ni wa jamaa.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto asiye na uhusiano wa karibu hatatunzwa kwa usawa.
