commentary_sw: Mtoto ambaye hana uhusiano nawe na yupo tu karibu. Hatatunzwa kwa njia ile ile kama watoto unaowajua.

digo: Mwana wa kutseka-tseka walafya ise nyama
literal_en: A laughing child refuses (to give) the father meat
idiomatic_en: A cheerful face can hide stubbornness and disobedience.
swahili: Mtoto wa kutabasamu humnyima baba nyama
commentary_en: The laughter can hide stubbornness and refusal. A clever child is not always helpful to his parents
commentary_dg: Kutseka kunaweza kufwitsa ukaidi na kuremwa. Mwana mjanja si siku zosi anavyosaidiya avyazi akpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Uso wa furaha unaweza kuficha ukaidi na kutotii.
commentary_sw: Tabasamu linaweza kuficha ukaidi na kukataa. Mtoto mjanja si lazima awe msaada kwa wazazi wake.

literal_en: Your companion’s child is cold
idiomatic_en: We tend to care less for what belongs to others than our own.
commentary_en: You do not value another person’s child as much as your own. If you are looking after them you might not take as good care of them as you should.
commentary_dg: Mwana wa myao wako kumthamini ni vigumu dza mwanao mwenye. Uchimuangalira, unaweza usimtunze dza uhendago mwanao.
idiomatic_sw: Binadamu huthamini zaidi vitu vyake kuliko vya wengine.
commentary_sw: Humthamini mtoto wa mtu mwingine kama wako. Ukimlinda unaweza usimtunze vizuri kama unavyopaswa.

literal_en: The young of a snake is a snake
idiomatic_en: A child takes after its parents.
commentary_en: Children inherit the character and nature of their parents, for better or worse. Used to comment when a child displays traits clearly derived from the parents, especially negative ones.
commentary_dg: Ana a mutu hurithi tabia na asili ya avyazi ao, kpwa manono au mabaya. Husemwa mutu achiona mwana anaonyesha tabia za avyaziwe, hasa zisizo nono.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto hufuata tabia za wazazi wake.
commentary_sw: Watoto hurithi tabia na asili ya wazazi wao, kwa mema au mabaya. Hutumika kutoa maoni wakati mtoto anaonyesha tabia zilizotokana na wazazi, hasa mbaya.

digo: Mwananyau ni chidema, mwanadiya ni wa lugbwe
literal_en: A kitten is a rag, a puppy is for a rope
idiomatic_en: People are treated differently based on bias, not merit.
swahili: Mtoto wa paka ni kitambaa, mtoto wa mbwa ni kamba
commentary_en: Kittens have rags tied round their necks when they are born so that they won’t be eaten by the male cats. The puppy is tied up with a rope so that it won’t misbehave. To do with bias. Some people are treated better than others.
commentary_dg: Mwananyau anafungbwa chidema shingoni achivyalwa ili akale salama na anyau alume. Mwanadiya anafungbwa lugbwe ili asihende fujo. Ndarira ihi inagomba kuhusu upendeleo: atu anjina anahendwa vizuri kuriko anjina.
idiomatic_sw: Watu wanatendewa tofauti kwa upendeleo, si kwa haki.
commentary_sw: Watoto wa paka hufungwa kitambaa shingoni wazaliwapo ili wasiliwe na paka dume. Mtoto wa mbwa hufungwa kamba ili asifanye fujo. Kuhusu upendeleo: watu wengine hutendewa vizuri zaidi kuliko wengine.

literal_en: The beginning of a sore is a little itch
idiomatic_en: Big problems often start from something very small.
swahili: Mwanzo wa kidonda ni kuwashwa kidogo
commentary_en: Some big problems start out very small
commentary_dg: Matatizo makpwulu ganaandza na mambo madide sana. Chironda chikulu chinaandza na chitswetswe kidide.
idiomatic_sw: Matatizo makubwa mara nyingi huanzia kwa jambo dogo.
commentary_sw: Matatizo makubwa huanzia mambo madogo sana.

literal_en: Four by four finishes the snake
idiomatic_en: Steady, persistent effort will finish even the toughest task.
commentary_en: Encourages persistence and steady effort. Just as repeated blows, four at a time, can kill even a large snake, consistent small efforts will eventually accomplish great tasks.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inahimiza bidii ya kudumu. Dza vira mapigo mwane mwane ganavyomaliza nyoka, juhudi ndide za kudumu ndizo zinazomaliza kazi kulu.
idiomatic_sw: Bidii ya kudumu humalizia kazi hata ngumu.
commentary_sw: Inahimiza bidii na juhudi za mara kwa mara. Kama vile mapigo ya mara kwa mara, manne kwa manne, yanaweza kuua hata nyoka kubwa, juhudi ndogo za kudumu hatimaye hukamilisha kazi kubwa.

digo: Mwasaga nguluwe kazi uchiyolunga, ndivyo uhendayo
literal_en: The grinder pig, the work we have come for is what we are doing
idiomatic_en: Don't waste time on trivialities; get straight to business.
swahili: Nguruwe akiingia shambani hupiga kazi moja kwa moja
commentary_en: Mwasaga means ‘one who grinds’. When the pig enters the field he doesn’t waste time but goes straight away to what he wants and takes it. Don’t waste time on trivialities, get on with the business in hand
commentary_dg: Nguluwe ichiingira mundani taiphoteza wakati, inashika arichokpwedza nacho mara mwenga. Usiphoteze wakati kpwa mambo madide, shughulikia kazi muhimu.
idiomatic_sw: Usipoteze muda kwa mambo ya bure; fanya kazi iliyoko mbele yako.
commentary_sw: 'Mwasaga' maana yake 'anayesaga'. Nguruwe akiingia shambani hapotezi muda bali hushika alichokuja nacho moja kwa moja. Usipoteze muda kwa mambo madogo, shughulikia kazi muhimu.

idiomatic_en: There is no use gossiping about someone who has already departed this world.
commentary_en: Once someone has departed this world, there is no point in gossiping about them. The dead are beyond the reach of slander, and speaking ill of them serves no purpose.
commentary_dg: Mutu achikala akaphiya kuzimu, takuna sababu ya kumsengenyea. Afwile a kure na masengenyo, na kuasengenya takuna faida yoyosi.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu aliyekwisha kwenda hahemewi tena.
commentary_sw: Mtu akishakwenda kuzimu, hakuna sababu ya kumsengenyea. Wafu wako nje ya kufikia kwa masengenyo, na kusema vibaya juu yao hakuna faida.

idiomatic_en: A person of wealth never lacks for visitors at their door.
commentary_en: Wealth attracts people regardless of one's character. A cattle owner will always find visitors at the door, because people are drawn to those with resources. Speaks to the social power of material wealth.
commentary_dg: Mali inavutiya atu bila kujali tabia ya mutu. Mwenye ng'ombe andaona ajeni kpwa muryango wakpwe chila wakati, mana atu huphiya kpwa mutu ariye na mali. Methali ihi inaonyesha nguvu za mali kpwa jamii.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye mali hupata watu wengi wanaomtaka.
commentary_sw: Mali huvutia watu bila kujali tabia ya mtu. Mwenye ng'ombe ataona wageni milangoni kila wakati, kwa sababu watu huvutiwa na wenye rasilimali. Inahusu nguvu za kijamii za mali.

literal_en: The body does not have a (preferred) side to excrete
idiomatic_en: Illness does not discriminate; it can strike any part of the body.
commentary_en: Illness and misfortune do not choose where they strike. Any part of the body can be affected, just as any person or any family can face hardship. Used to counsel acceptance when trouble comes unexpectedly.
commentary_dg: Ukongo na matatizo tagachagula phatu ganaphotokera. Kanda yoyosi ya mwiri inaweza kuathirika, dza vira mutu yeyosi au kaya yoyosi inaweza kukutana na shida. Husemwa kukubali wakati matatizo ganakpwedza bila kutarajiwa.
idiomatic_sw: Matatizo hayachagui upande; yanaweza kutokea popote.
commentary_sw: Ugonjwa na matatizo hayachagui mahali yanapopiga. Sehemu yoyote ya mwili inaweza kuathirika, kama vile mtu yeyote au familia yoyote inaweza kukabiliwa na shida. Hutumika kushauri kukubali wakati matatizo yanakuja bila kutarajiwa.

idiomatic_en: A thorn you step on yourself hurts less than one pushed by another.
commentary_en: A self-inflicted wound hurts less than one caused by another. When you bring trouble upon yourself knowingly, you bear it more easily because you understand its origin and accept the consequences.
commentary_dg: Mwiya wa kudzidunga taulumiza dza wa kudungwa ni mutu munjina. Ukidzirehera matatizo mwenye, unagavumirira kpwa urahisi mana unamanya garikotokera.
idiomatic_sw: Hasira unayojiletea mwenyewe haikuumizi kama ile ya nje.
commentary_sw: Jeraha ulilojiletea mwenyewe haumizi kama ile ya nje. Unapojiletea matatizo kwa kujua, unayastahimili kwa urahisi kwa sababu unaelewa chanzo chake na kukubali matokeo.

digo: Myao achimwaga mboga nawe sima itiye mtsanga
literal_en: If your friend pours out sauce you put sand in the ugali
idiomatic_en: When friends quarrel, both sides end up losing.
swahili: Mwenzio akimwaga mboga, nawe tia mchanga ugalini
commentary_en: These people are quarelling and will both go without food. They both inflict pain on each other due to their dispute.
commentary_dg: Atu hinya anagombana na osi andakosa chakurya. Uchivoana na myao, mosi munaumizana kpwa sababu ya ugomvi wenu.
idiomatic_sw: Ugomvi kati ya marafiki huwadhuru wote wawili.
commentary_sw: Watu hawa wanagombana na wote watakosa chakula. Wote wanaumizana kwa sababu ya ugomvi wao.

literal_en: If your friend stumbles tell him sorry
idiomatic_en: Show compassion when your friend is in trouble.
commentary_en: A call for empathy and solidarity among friends. When your companion stumbles or faces difficulty, the least you can do is offer words of comfort. True friendship is shown in times of trouble.
commentary_dg: Wito wa huruma na ushirikiano kahi-kahi ya myao. Myao achikpwala au kukutana na ugumu, chitu cha kpwandza ni kumuambira pore. Umyao wa kpweli huonekana wakati wa shida.
idiomatic_sw: Onyesha huruma rafiki yako anapokuwa katika shida.
commentary_sw: Wito wa huruma na mshikamano kati ya marafiki. Mwenzako anapojikwaa au kukutana na ugumu, unachopaswa kufanya ni kutoa maneno ya faraja. Urafiki wa kweli huonekana wakati wa shida.

digo: Mzingirwa kamanya, ela enye midzi, anamanya
literal_en: The one who is surrounded doesn’t know, but those the town inhabitants do
idiomatic_en: The person involved is always the last to know.
commentary_en: The person affected by something is the last one to find out
commentary_dg: Mutu anayeathirika na dzambo ndiye wa mwisho kumanya. Ela enye midzi, ao anamanya kare.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayeathirika na jambo ndiye wa mwisho kulijua.
commentary_sw: Mtu anayeathiriwa na jambo ndiye wa mwisho kujua.

literal_en: One who chases many people is not ignored
idiomatic_en: A persistent person cannot be easily dismissed.
commentary_en: Said of a person who is actively engaged in many pursuits. Someone who chases many things cannot be overlooked or ignored, because their presence and activity are felt everywhere.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kpwa mutu anayedzishughulisha na mambo manji. Mutu anayezola atu anji kanemwa, mana uwepo wakpwe na shughuli zakpwe zinaonekana chila phatu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayeshughulikia mambo mengi hawezi kupuuzwa.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kuhusu mtu anayejishughulisha na mambo mengi. Mtu anayefuatilia mambo mengi hawezi kupuuzwa, kwa sababu uwepo na shughuli zake zinaonekana kila mahali.

digo: Nang’a-nang’a ya kuyu, ndani ina mabulu
literal_en: Ripeness of a fig, inside it has maggots
idiomatic_en: Outward appearances can be deceptive; look beneath the surface.
swahili: Uzuri wa tini, ndani ina wadudu
commentary_en: Don’t judge a book by its cover.
commentary_dg: Usihukumu chintu kpwa kuonekana kwakpwe kundze tu. Nang'a-nang'a ya kunze inaweza kufwitsa ubovu wa ndani.
idiomatic_sw: Usihukumu kitu kwa sura yake ya nje; angalia ndani.
commentary_sw: Usihukumu kitabu kwa jalada lake.

digo: Naona makuzi kuzolana, ela simanya lume ni riphi wala chetu ni riphi
literal_en: I see ‘makuzi’ chasing each other, but I don’t know which is male and which is female
idiomatic_en: Outward appearances don't reveal a person's true status.
swahili: Naona ndege wanafukuzana, lakini sijui dume ni yupi
commentary_en: makuzi – type of bird. In many situations Digos are all alike, regardless of wealth or education. eg. at a wedding all the women will be dressed smartly. A good cook may be rich or poor, educated or not, you can’t tell.
commentary_dg: Makuzi ni aina ya nyama a kuruka. Kpwenye hali nyinji, Adigo osi anafanana, bila kujali utajiri ama masomo. Mfwano, kpwenye harusi achetu osi anavwala vizuri. Mpishi mubomu anaweza kukala tajiri ama maskini.
idiomatic_sw: Sura ya nje haifunui hali ya kweli ya mtu.
commentary_sw: Makuzi ni aina ya ndege. Katika hali nyingi Wadigo wote wanafanana, bila kujali utajiri au elimu. Kwa mfano, katika harusi wanawake wote watavaa vizuri. Mpishi hodari anaweza kuwa tajiri au maskini.

literal_en: Collective oppression (on a person) cannot keep a person r
idiomatic_en: Collective pressure can force anyone to leave or change.
swahili: Shinikizo la watu wengi halimwachi mtu
commentary_en: If a person is not liked then the group puts negative pressure on them until they leave. This can also be applied to a problem you want to get id of.
commentary_dg: Mutu asiyemendzwa ni kundi, atu anamuvika shinikizo hadi auke. Ndarira ihi piya inahumirwa kpwa tatizo urirolonda kuriusa.
idiomatic_sw: Shinikizo la pamoja linaweza kumlazimisha mtu yeyote kuondoka au kubadilika.
commentary_sw: Mtu asiyependwa kikundi humweka shinikizo hasi hadi aondoke. Hii pia inaweza kutumika kwa tatizo unalotaka kuliondoa.

literal_en: Whom shall I eat is not the same as ‘what shall I eat’
idiomatic_en: Having someone to share with is different from having nothing at all.
swahili: 'Nitakula na nani' si sawa na 'nitakula nini'
commentary_en: Distinguishes between loneliness and hunger. Having no one to share a meal with is a different kind of suffering from having nothing to eat at all. Companionship and sustenance are both essential but not the same.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inatofautisha kahi-kahi ya upweke na mndzala. Kuamba 'ndarya nani' ni tofauti na kuamba 'ndaryani.' Kukosa mutu wa kurya naye si sawa na kukosa chakurya kabisa.
idiomatic_sw: Kuwa na mtu wa kushirikiana naye ni tofauti na kutokuwa na chochote.
commentary_sw: Inatofautisha kati ya upweke na njaa. Kutokuwa na mtu wa kushirikiana naye chakula ni aina tofauti ya mateso kuliko kutokuwa na chakula kabisa. Urafiki na chakula vyote ni muhimu lakini si sawa.

literal_en: Jealousy/envy suits a barren person
idiomatic_en: Those who lack what others have are naturally prone to envy.
commentary_en: A barren woman will feel envy whenever she sees a child
commentary_dg: Mchetu mgumba andahisi ndeo chila achimuona mwana wa mutu mwanjine. Kukosa chintu ambacho anjina anacho kunaredza wivu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu asiye na kile wengine wanacho huwa na wivu kwa kawaida.
commentary_sw: Mwanamke tasa atahisi wivu kila anapomwona mtoto.

literal_en: An elephant cannot be killed with a dry maize stalk
idiomatic_en: You cannot defeat a powerful opponent with a weak weapon.
swahili: Tembo hauuliwi kwa bua la mahindi
commentary_en: A warning against using inadequate means to face a great challenge. You cannot defeat an elephant with a dry maize stalk; to overcome a powerful adversary you need the right tools and strength.
commentary_dg: Onyo dhidi ya kutumia njira duni kukabiliana na changamoto kulu. Ndzovu kaolagbwa na buwa — ili ushinde adui mkpwulu unahitaji zana na nguvu zinazofwa.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kumshinda adui mkubwa kwa silaha dhaifu.
commentary_sw: Onyo dhidi ya kutumia njia duni kukabiliana na changamoto kubwa. Huwezi kuua tembo kwa bua la mahindi; ili kumshinda adui mkubwa unahitaji zana na nguvu zinazofaa.

literal_en: An elephant is not defeated by his trunk
idiomatic_en: A leader is not undermined by his own subordinates.
commentary_en: The trunk is weaker than the rest of the elephant, the elephant controls the trunk, not the other way around.
commentary_dg: Muirowe ni dhaifu kuriko ndzovu yosi; ndzovu inautawala muirowe, si kinyume chakpwe. Vivyo hivyo, mutu mkpwulu kashindwa ni atu akpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Kiongozi hashindwi na watu walio chini yake.
commentary_sw: Mkonga ni dhaifu kuliko tembo mzima; tembo huutawala mkonga, si kinyume chake.

literal_en: Taking ‘Ng’andu’ is going
idiomatic_en: Taking without asking is the same as stealing.
commentary_en: Snatching something without asking permission.
commentary_dg: Kuala chintu bila kuomba ruhusa ni sawa na kuphiya nacho. Ng'andu ni kunyakua; usihale chintu bila kuomba.
idiomatic_sw: Kuchukua kitu bila ruhusa ni sawa na kuiba.
commentary_sw: Kunyakua kitu bila kuomba ruhusa.

digo: Ng’ombe iolagbwa ni chala cha mwenye
literal_en: A cow is killed by its owner’s finger
idiomatic_en: The one who holds power decides the fate of those beneath them.
swahili: Ng'ombe huuawa kwa kidole cha mwenyewe
commentary_en: The owner has the power to decide which cow to kill and can just point at it and everyone will know. This applies to anyone with power.
commentary_dg: Mwenye ng'ombe ana uwezo wa kuamua ng'ombe yupi achinjwe kpwa kuuashiria na chala tu. Ndarira ihi inahumirwa kpwa mutu yeyosi mwenye mamlaka.
idiomatic_sw: Mwenye mamlaka ndiye anayeamua hatima ya walio chini yake.
commentary_sw: Mwenye ng'ombe ana uwezo wa kuamua ng'ombe yupi achinjwe kwa kuuashiria tu na kila mtu ataelewa. Hii inatumika kwa mtu yeyote mwenye mamlaka.

literal_en: An angry cow has its own person to milk it
idiomatic_en: Even the most difficult person can be handled by someone who knows them well.
commentary_en: A person who is always angry will have someone who knows how to calm them and get things from them.
commentary_dg: Mutu ariye na siru siku zosi andakala na mutu amanyaye jinsi ya kumtuliza na kuphata vitu kpwa iye. Chila mutu ana mkamiwe.
idiomatic_sw: Hata mtu mgumu ana mtu anayejua jinsi ya kumshughulikia.
commentary_sw: Mtu ambaye ana hasira siku zote atakuwa na mtu anayejua jinsi ya kumtuliza na kupata vitu kutoka kwake.

digo: Ng’ondzi kuuya nyuma, siko kuchimbira kondo, ela ni kuaza mizungu
literal_en: (When) a sheep takes some steps backwards, it isn’t running from a war, but is thinking about attacking
idiomatic_en: Do not be fooled by an enemy's retreat; they may be preparing a stronger attack.
swahili: Kondoo akirudi nyuma, si kukimbia vita, bali ni kupanga mashambulizi
commentary_en: Don’t be fooled if you see your enemy/problem retreating a little, it may come back with greater force
commentary_dg: Usidanganyike uchiona adui wako ama tatizo rinauyira nyuma. Rinaweza kurudi na nguvu nyinji zaidi. Ng'ondzi ikuuya nyuma ni kuaza kpwa nguvu.
idiomatic_sw: Usidanganywe na kurudi nyuma kwa adui; anaweza kurudi kwa nguvu zaidi.
commentary_sw: Usidanganywe ukimwona adui/tatizo linarudi nyuma kidogo, linaweza kurudi kwa nguvu zaidi.

literal_en: A sheep looks behind/backwards
idiomatic_en: A wise person learns from the past and keeps it in mind.
commentary_en: A clever person is aware of the past and takes it into account
commentary_dg: Mutu mwerevu anamanya go garichopita na kugahala moyoni. Kuangalira nyuma ni achili, si woga.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye busara hujifunza kutokana na yaliyopita.
commentary_sw: Mtu mwerevu anajua yaliyopita na kuyazingatia.

literal_en: A judge is a foot
idiomatic_en: Reward comes only to those who make the effort.
commentary_en: Traditional judges try cases and receive a reward. They will only be rewarded if they actually make the effort to go and try the case (using their feet). ie. you have to put something in before you will get something out.
commentary_dg: Ahukumu a jadi anasikira kesi na kuphokera malipo. Ela andalipwa tu achihenda juhudi ya kuphiya kusikiriza kesi. Ni lazima uvike chintu kabla ya kuphata chintu.
idiomatic_sw: Malipo yanakuja kwa wale tu wanaojitahidi.
commentary_sw: Mahakimu wa jadi husikiliza kesi na kupokea malipo. Watalipwa tu wakifanya juhudi kwenda kusikiliza kesi. Lazima uweke kitu kabla ya kupata kitu.

literal_en: The strength of water is stones
idiomatic_en: Even something soft like water becomes powerful when it meets resistance.
commentary_en: Speaks to the power of obstacles in revealing strength. Water shows its force when it meets rocks; similarly, a person's true strength is revealed when they face resistance and hardship.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi ni kuhusu vikpwazo vinavyoonyesha nguvu. Madzi ganaonyesha nguvu gakpwe ganaphokutana na mawe; vivyo hivyo nguvu za kpweli za mutu zinaonekana achikutana na upinzani.
idiomatic_sw: Kitu laini kinaweza kuwa na nguvu kikipatana na upinzani.
commentary_sw: Inahusu nguvu za vikwazo katika kuonyesha uwezo. Maji huonyesha nguvu zake yanapokutana na mawe; vivyo hivyo, nguvu za kweli za mtu huonekana anapokutana na upinzani na ugumu.

literal_en: Two paths/ways defeat a hyena
idiomatic_en: Indecision between two choices leads to failure.
commentary_en: Warns against indecision. The hyena, famously greedy, was defeated when it tried to follow two paths at once and ended up on neither. One must choose decisively and commit.
commentary_dg: Onyo dhidi ya kusitasita. Fisi, maarufu kpwa ulafi, ka shindwa kpwa kuzola njira mbiri kpwa wakati mumwenga na kafika phatu phophosi. Mutu ni lazima achague kpwa ujasiri na kudzitolea.
idiomatic_sw: Kusita kati ya chaguzi mbili husababisha kushindwa.
commentary_sw: Inaonyo dhidi ya kusitasita. Fisi, maarufu kwa ulafi, alishindwa alipojaribu kufuata njia mbili kwa wakati mmoja na hakufika mahali popote. Mtu lazima achague kwa ujasiri na kujitolea.

digo: Nndaryani si sawa na nndarya nani
literal_en: ‘What will I eat?’ is not the same as ‘what will I eat with it?’
idiomatic_en: Having food is one thing, but having someone to share it with is another.
swahili: 'Nitakula nini' si sawa na 'nitakula na nani'
commentary_en: In the first instance you have nothing, in the second you have something but it is not complete
commentary_dg: Kpwenye hali ya kpwandza kuna chochosi, kpwenye ya phiri una chintu ela si kamili. Kurya peke yako si sawa na kurya na mutu.
idiomatic_sw: Kuwa na chakula ni jambo moja, lakini kuwa na mtu wa kushirikiana naye ni jambo jingine.
commentary_sw: Katika hali ya kwanza huna chochote, katika ya pili una kitu lakini si kamili.

digo: Nyani kuhenda ngoti nkukala diya rikure 278.Nyasi zina mimba, gomba kusaza/uchisaza
literal_en: A baboon kneeling usually the dog is far away The grasses are pregnant, don’t say everything
idiomatic_en: When authority is absent, the undisciplined take advantage; and beware of hidden listeners.
swahili: Nyani akipiga magoti, mbwa huwa mbali; Nyasi zina mimba, usiseme siri nje
commentary_en: The dog will attack the baboon if it is kneeling. Children with a strict father will do what they like when he is not around! When you are out in the open you may be overheard if you speak about secret things as there may be people hiding in the grass.
commentary_dg: Diya richikala rikure, nyani anahenda arivyolonda. Ana ariye na ise mkali anahenda wapendzavyo ise achikala kure. Piya, unapokala kundze usigombe mambo ga siri kpwa sababu atu anaweza kudzifwitsa nyasini na kukusikira.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto akikosekana baba mkali hufanya atakavyo; na unapokuwa nje unaweza kusikilizwa kwa sababu watu wanaweza kujificha nyasini.
commentary_sw: Mbwa atamshambulia nyani akiwa amepiga magoti. Watoto wenye baba mkali hufanya wanavyotaka baba anapokuwa mbali. Unapokuwa nje unaweza kusikilizwa ukizungumza mambo ya siri kwa sababu watu wanaweza kujificha nyasini.

literal_en: The grasses are pregnant
idiomatic_en: Walls have ears; be careful what you say in the open.
commentary_en: A warning to watch one's words even in seemingly private places. The grasses are 'pregnant' with hidden listeners. Equivalent to 'walls have ears' — secrets spoken carelessly will eventually come to light.
commentary_dg: Onyo ra kuangaliya maneno gako hata phatu phenye siri. Nyasi zina mimba — kuna mutu anaweza kukala anasikira. Siri zinazoambwa bila uangalifu ndazo zikafitsuka siku mwenga.
idiomatic_sw: Uwe makini unachosema kwa sababu mtu anaweza kuwa akisikiliza.
commentary_sw: Onyo la kuangalia maneno yako hata mahali panayoonekana kuwa faragha. Nyasi zina 'mimba' ya wasikilizaji waliojificha. Sawa na 'kuta zina masikio' — siri zinazosemwa bila uangalifu hatimaye zitafichuliwa.

literal_en: ‘a niggle’ is an illness
idiomatic_en: A small nagging ailment is still a real illness that should not be ignored.
commentary_en: You have an illness (eg. aches and pains) but it doesn’t stop you from doing your everyday business. It may kill you one day though
commentary_dg: Nyerenyere ni ukongo ambao taukuzuwiya kuhenda kazi za chila siku, ela siku mwenga unaweza kukuolaga. Usibere ukongo mudide.
idiomatic_sw: Usipuuze maumivu madogo, kwani yanaweza kuwa hatari.
commentary_sw: Una ugonjwa (kama maumivu) lakini hauzuii shughuli zako za kila siku. Lakini unaweza kukuua siku moja.

literal_en: (when a) snake has died a red-tailed lizard bites people
idiomatic_en: When the powerful are gone, the weak begin to show off.
commentary_en: Furious/strong people have gone, now the weak ones are flexing their muscles
commentary_dg: Atu enye nguvu achikala akauka, atu anyonge nao anaandza kudziona. Nyoka ichifwa, ngate piya inadziona ina uwezo.
idiomatic_sw: Wenye nguvu wakiondoka, wanyonge huanza kujigamba.
commentary_sw: Watu wenye nguvu wameondoka, sasa wanyonge wanajisifu.

idiomatic_en: A real confrontation must be settled face to face, not through go-betweens.
commentary_en: Insists that disputes and confrontations should be handled face to face, not through intermediaries or behind someone's back. True resolution requires direct encounter.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inasisitiza kukala magomvi ni lazima gashughulikiwe uso kpwa uso, si kpwa njira ya atu anjina au nyuma ya mutu. Kutatuwa kpwa kpweli kunahitaji kuonana mwenga kpwa mwenga.
idiomatic_sw: Ubishi ni wa uso kwa uso.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza kwamba migogoro na makabiliano yanapaswa kushughulikiwa uso kwa uso, si kupitia watu wengine au nyuma ya mtu. Suluhisho la kweli linahitaji kukutana moja kwa moja.

literal_en: (in) a house where someone has died you should not listen to its mourners
idiomatic_en: Don't believe everything said by those in a house of mourning.
commentary_en: Don’t believe all the stories people will say when someone has died, ie. that the person did not die naturally
commentary_dg: Usiamini hadithi zosi atu anazogomba mutu achikala akafwa. Mfwano, anaweza kuamba kukala hakafwa chifo cha kawaida. Ela si siku zosi ni kpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Usiamini kila kinachosemwa wakati wa msiba.
commentary_sw: Usiamini hadithi zote watu wanazosema mtu anapokufa, yaani kwamba mtu huyo hakufa kifo cha kawaida.

literal_en: A house is problems, it was made out of many parts
idiomatic_en: Every household has its own unique set of problems.
swahili: Nyumba ni matatizo, imejengwa kwa vipande vingi
commentary_en: Each house has its own problems
commentary_dg: Chila nyumba ina matatizo gakpwe. Nyumba ihendwa na vipande vinji, na chila chipande china changamoto zakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila nyumba ina matatizo yake.
commentary_sw: Kila nyumba ina matatizo yake.

literal_en: A house is four corners
idiomatic_en: A proper household requires structure and balance from all sides.
commentary_en: Digo houses are usually square rather than round
commentary_dg: Nyumba za Adigo kpwa kawaida ni za mraba zenye pembe nne, si za mviringo. Nyumba yenye pembe nne ina msingi imara.
idiomatic_sw: Nyumba nzuri inahitaji msingi imara na usawa pande zote.
commentary_sw: Nyumba za Wadigo kwa kawaida ni za mraba badala ya mviringo.

digo: Nyumba ya gbwalala i lala gbwalala
literal_en: (only) A beast sleeps in a beast’s house
idiomatic_en: Only those of the same kind can tolerate each other's ways.
commentary_en: gbwalala – a kind of animal, but no-one knows what it is, also refers to a person who is a beast.
commentary_dg: Gbwalala ni aina ya nyama ambaye taamanyanwa ni atu, piya ni neno ra mutu mwenye tabiya mbaya. Mutu wa tabiya mbaya ndiye anayeweza kuishi na mutu wa tabiya mbaya.
idiomatic_sw: Watu wa aina moja tu ndio wanaovumiliana.
commentary_sw: Gbwalala ni aina ya mnyama ambaye hakuna mtu anayemjua, pia inamaanisha mtu mwenye tabia mbaya.

literal_en: (in a) House of a bachelor there is no mourning
idiomatic_en: A person who lives alone will have no one to mourn them when they die.
swahili: Nyumba ya mtu peke yake haina maombolezo
commentary_en: He is the only one living there, so if he dies there will be no-one there to mourn him, the funeral will take place elsewhere. A warning that people should not live alone. Having a good funeral is v. important in Digo culture.
commentary_dg: Mndzaka anaishi mwenye, kpwa hivyo achifwa takuna mutu wa kumririzira. Mazishi gandahendwa phatu phanjine. Ndarira ihi ni onyo kukala atu asiishi peke yao. Kukala na mazishi mazuri ni muhimu sana kpwa Adigo.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayeishi peke yake hatakuwa na mtu wa kumwombolezea.
commentary_sw: Yeye ndiye peke yake anayeishi hapo, akifa hakutakuwa na mtu wa kumwomboleza; mazishi yatafanyika mahali pengine. Onyo kwamba watu wasiishi peke yao. Kuwa na mazishi mazuri ni muhimu sana katika utamaduni wa Kidigo.

idiomatic_en: Words and exclamations alone cannot break you; do not overreact to mere noise.
commentary_en: Reassures that mere words or shouts cannot cause physical harm. An exclamation of surprise or alarm does not break a vein. Used to counsel against overreacting to verbal provocations.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inahakikisha kukala maneno au kelele peke yakpwe tagaweza kulumiza mwiri. Kuamba 'oho' taivundza msipha. Husemwa kuonya mutu asidze akajibizana kupita chiasi na uchokozi wa maneno.
idiomatic_sw: Sauti au kelele peke yake haviwezi kukudhuru.
commentary_sw: Inatoa uhakika kwamba maneno au kelele pekee haziwezi kusababisha madhara ya kimwili. Sauti ya mshangao au wasiwasi haivunji mshipa. Hutumika kushauri kutokujibu kupita kiasi kwa uchokozi wa maneno.

literal_en: A weak spirit is danced for at the side
idiomatic_en: No one takes a weak person or idea seriously.
commentary_en: No one takes a weak spirit seriously
commentary_dg: Takuna mutu anayemhala pepho mnyonje kpwa uzito. Vivyo hivyo, mutu asiye na nguvu ama uwezo, atu anamberwa.
idiomatic_sw: Hakuna mtu anayechukulia kwa uzito mtu au wazo dhaifu.
commentary_sw: Hakuna mtu anayemchukulia pepo dhaifu kwa uzito.

literal_en: Money is (like) nakedness, they are not shown/exposed
idiomatic_en: Keep your wealth private; never flaunt your money.
commentary_en: You don’t tell other people how much money you have.
commentary_dg: Usimwambire mutu una pesa ngaphi. Pesa ni dza chitsaha, taionyeshwa atu. Uhifwidhi mambo ga mali yako kpwa siri.
idiomatic_sw: Usiwaambie watu una pesa ngapi; ficha mali yako.
commentary_sw: Usiwaambie watu wengine una pesa ngapi.

literal_en: The place where you harvest is the place where you eat.
idiomatic_en: You reap where you have sown.
commentary_en: Stresses the connection between effort and reward. You eat where you have worked and harvested; blessings come from the place where you have invested your labour.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inasisitiza uhusiano wa juhudi na malipo. Mutu anarya phatu arivunira; baraka zinatoka phatu mutu arivophanda na kuhenda kazi.
idiomatic_sw: Unavuna pale ulipopanda.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza uhusiano kati ya juhudi na malipo. Unakula mahali ulipovuna; baraka hutoka mahali ulipowekeza kazi yako.

literal_en: Where there is smoke there is no lack of ‘birds’ When the bush is burning this bird gathers to eat
idiomatic_en: Where there is activity, there are always those who come to benefit.
commentary_en: the insects driven up by the smoke.
commentary_dg: Wadudu arisouswa ni mosi ndio chakurya cha nyuni hizo. Pharipho na shughuli, kunakala na atu a kpwendza faidha.
idiomatic_sw: Mahali penye shughuli, daima kuna watu wanaokuja kunufaika.
commentary_sw: Wadudu wanaoamshwa na moshi ndio chakula cha ndege hao.

literal_en: Where there is a vulture there is a carcass
idiomatic_en: Where there are signs, there is substance behind them.
commentary_en: Where there are signs, there is a cause. Vultures circling overhead indicate a carcass below. Similarly, visible effects always point to an underlying reality that should not be ignored.
commentary_dg: Pharipho na dalili, phana chanzo. Nderi achizunguka dzulu anaonyesha lufu chini. Vivyo hivyo, mambo ganagoonekana chila wakati ganaonyesha ukpweli wa ndani ambao taufwa kupuuzwa.
idiomatic_sw: Dalili za nje huonyesha ukweli wa ndani.
commentary_sw: Dalili zinapoonyesha, kuna chanzo. Tai wanaozunguka hewani wanaonyesha mzoga chini. Vivyo hivyo, athari zinazoonekana daima zinaonyesha ukweli wa ndani ambao haupaswi kupuuzwa.

literal_en: Where it is slippery do not make a boast
idiomatic_en: Don't boast when you're in a precarious position.
commentary_en: If you boast you may fall at any moment
commentary_dg: Uchidjiigamba phatu phenye wereza, unaweza kugbwa wakati wowosi. Phahi usihende chidzinyo kabila hujamanya ichikala u salama.
idiomatic_sw: Usijisifu unapokuwa katika hali hatarishi.
commentary_sw: Ukijigamba unaweza kuanguka wakati wowote.

literal_en: Where there is no hill do not make a hill (Act
idiomatic_en: Don't exaggerate or create problems where none exist.
commentary_en: You cannot create something out of nothing. ual meaning of chidzinyo?)
commentary_dg: Usihende dzambo kulu kula kpwa chitu ambacho tachikuko. Kukala taphana tatizo, usihende dza kukala phanalo.
idiomatic_sw: Usitengeneze matatizo ambapo hayapo.
commentary_sw: Huwezi kutengeneza kitu kutoka kwa kitu ambacho hakipo.

literal_en: A place with elders is not disturbed by things
idiomatic_en: Where there are elders, no problem is too great to be solved.
commentary_en: Nothing will go wrong while there are elders around. They can sort out any problem
commentary_dg: Avyere anamanya kutatuwa mambo gosi. Phenye avyere, takuna dzambo ambaro ni kulu sana hata risiweze kutatsulwa.
idiomatic_sw: Mahali penye wazee, hakuna tatizo ambalo haliwezi kutatuliwa.
commentary_sw: Hakuna kitakachokwenda vibaya wakati wazee wako. Wanaweza kutatua tatizo lolote.

literal_en: Where there is smoke there doesn’t lack fire
idiomatic_en: Where there is smoke, there is fire.
commentary_en: Where there are rumours or signs there is always some truth behind them. Smoke cannot exist without fire, just as accusations or gossip usually have some basis in fact.
commentary_dg: Phenye mosi taphakosa moho — uvumi au dalili chila wakati zina ukpweli nyuma yakpwe. Mosi tauwezi kukala bila moho, dza vira masengenyo mara nyinji gana msingi wa ukpweli.
idiomatic_sw: Kila dalili ina chanzo chake; hakuna uvumi bila ukweli.
commentary_sw: Mahali penye uvumi au dalili daima kuna ukweli nyuma yake. Moshi hauwezi kuwepo bila moto, kama vile shutuma au masengenyo mara nyingi yana msingi wa ukweli.

digo: Rero ni matso machero ni mabatso
literal_en: Today is eyes, tomorrow is bigger eyes
idiomatic_en: Heed today's warning or face greater consequences tomorrow.
swahili: Leo ni macho, kesho ni makubwa zaidi
commentary_en: You are being warned today and if you don’t heed the warning tomorrow you will face the consequences and many people will be focussing on you
commentary_dg: Rero unaonywa, na usiphosikira, machero undakpwedzwa ni mambo makulu zaidi. Atu anji andakala anakuangalira kpwa mabatso.
idiomatic_sw: Sikiliza onyo la leo, au utakabiliwa na matokeo makubwa zaidi kesho.
commentary_sw: Unaoonywa leo na usipozingatia onyo, kesho utakabiliwa na matokeo na watu wengi watakuangalia.

literal_en: If it (a millepede) dies well it looks like the rain
idiomatic_en: When a matter is properly resolved, there is peace and no need to revisit it.
commentary_en: If a millepede dies without being crushed its body produces rings which look a bit like rain. When an argument has been settled there is no need to worry again about this issue
commentary_dg: Jongoo achifwa bila kupondwa mwiriwe nkuhenda pete dza mvula. Vivyo hivyo, ugomvi uchishamarizwa vinono, taphana haja ya kuhangaika nao tsona.
idiomatic_sw: Jambo likitatuliwa vizuri hakuna haja ya kulirudia tena.
commentary_sw: Jongoo akifa bila kupondwa mwili wake hutengeneza pete zinazofanana na mvua. Ugomvi ukishasuluhishwa hakuna haja ya kuuhangaikia tena.

literal_en: The soul tricked its master
idiomatic_en: Sometimes your own desires lead you astray.
commentary_en: A warning about self-deception. Sometimes a person's own desires or heart misleads them into poor decisions. The soul, or inner longing, can trick its master into ruin.
commentary_dg: Onyo kuhusu kudzidanganya. Wakati munjina tamaa au moyo wa mutu mwenye unamuhimira kpwa maamuzi mabaya. Roho inaweza kuchenga bwanawe hadi uharibifu.
idiomatic_sw: Wakati mwingine tamaa zako mwenyewe zinakupoteza.
commentary_sw: Onyo kuhusu kujidanganya. Wakati mwingine tamaa au moyo wa mtu mwenyewe unampoteza katika maamuzi mabaya. Roho, au hamu ya ndani, inaweza kumdanganya bwana wake hadi uharibifu.

digo: Safari ya muhondo i funganywa rero
literal_en: The journey of tomorrow is packed for today
idiomatic_en: Prepare today for what you must do tomorrow.
commentary_en: Advises preparation and foresight. Whatever journey or task awaits you tomorrow should be planned and packed for today, so that when the time comes you are ready.
commentary_dg: Methali ihi inashauri maandalizi na kuona mbere. Safari au kazi yoyosi ya muhondo ifunganywe rero, ili wakati ukifika ukale tayari.
idiomatic_sw: Maandalizi ya kesho hufanywa leo, usije ukashindwa na wakati.
commentary_sw: Inashauri maandalizi na kuona mbele. Safari au kazi yoyote inayokungoja kesho inapaswa kupangwa na kutayarishwa leo, ili wakati utakapofika uwe tayari.

literal_en: A dead ear cannot feel (lit: hear) medicine
idiomatic_en: Those who refuse to listen cannot be helped.
commentary_en: Said to warn someone who refuses to listen to advice. Just as medicine cannot heal a dead ear, counsel cannot help a person who has closed their mind. Used by elders when a stubborn person ignores repeated warnings.
commentary_dg: Husemwa kumwonya mutu anayekatala kusikiriza ushauri. Dza vira dawa taiweza kuphoza sikiro ririkofwa, ushauri tauweza kumsaidiya mutu ariyefunga achili zakpwe. Hutumika ni avyere mutu mkaidi achiphuza maonyo.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu ambaye hasikilizi ushauri hawezi kusaidiwa.
commentary_sw: Husemwa kumwonya mtu anayekataa kusikiliza ushauri. Kama vile dawa haiwezi kuponya sikio lililokufa, ushauri hauwezi kumsaidia mtu aliyefunga akili yake. Hutumika na wazee mtu mkaidi anapopuuza maonyo yanayorudiwa.

literal_en: The ear does not surpass the head
idiomatic_en: A child should not try to surpass their parent.
commentary_en: The ear is smaller than the head, and refers to a child-father relationship
commentary_dg: Sikiro ni dide kuriko chitswa, na hiri rinaonyesa uhusiano wa mwana na ise. Mwana kandatsupa ise kpwa utu wowosi.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto hana haki ya kujiona mkubwa kuliko mzazi wake.
commentary_sw: Sikio ni dogo kuliko kichwa, na hii inaashiria uhusiano kati ya mtoto na baba yake.

literal_en: The lion who walks quietly is the one who eats the meat.
idiomatic_en: The quiet achiever succeeds where the boastful fail.
swahili: Simba mwenda kimya ndiye mla nyama
commentary_en: Praises the value of patience and stealth over loud boasting. The lion that hunts quietly is the one that eats, while the noisy one alerts its prey. Used to encourage working silently toward one's goals rather than announcing them to everyone.
commentary_dg: Inasifu thamani ya kuvumirira na utulivu badala ya kudzenga. Simba aendaye pore ndiye ariyaye nyama, ela mwenye kelele anahadzisha [?] nyama zakpwe. Hutumika kuhimiza kuhumira kpwa utulivu kuelekea malengo gako.
swahili_equivalent: Simba mwenda kimya ndiye mla nyama
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye kimya ndiye anayefanikiwa zaidi kuliko mwenye kelele.
commentary_sw: Inasifu thamani ya subira na utulivu badala ya kujisifu kwa kelele. Simba anayewinda kimya ndiye anayekula, wakati mwenye kelele anatahadharisha mawindo yake. Hutumika kuhimiza kufanya kazi kimya kuelekea malengo yako.

digo: Singo ya kuku i vundzirwa kanda
idiomatic_en: Even a small force can overcome someone who appears strong but has a hidden weakness.
commentary_en: Observes that even a simple cord can break the neck of a chicken, because the chicken's neck is fragile. Applied to situations where a seemingly small burden or opponent can overcome someone who appears strong but has a hidden weakness.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala hata kanda rahisi inaweza kuvundza singo ya kuku, kpwa sababu singo ya kuku ni nyonje [?]. Hutumika kpwa hali ambapo mutu au chitu chidide chinaweza kumshinda mutu anayeonekana na nguvu ela ana unyonje uriofisirwa.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu dhaifu hushindwa na mzigo wake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kwamba hata kamba rahisi inaweza kuvunja shingo ya kuku, kwa sababu shingo ya kuku ni dhaifu. Hutumika katika hali ambapo mzigo au mpinzani mdogo anaweza kumshinda mtu anayeonekana imara lakini ana udhaifu uliofichwa.

literal_en: There is no medicine for character
idiomatic_en: A person's character cannot be changed by any remedy.
commentary_en: Aleopard cannot change its spots
commentary_dg: Tabiya ya mutu taibadilika na dawa yoyosi. Mutu arivyo ndivyo arizo, dza chui ambaye kabadilika madowa gakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Tabia mbaya haibadilishwi na dawa yoyote.
commentary_sw: Chui hawezi kubadilisha madoa yake.

literal_en: You don’t get a small person who does not belong to a clan.
idiomatic_en: No one is so insignificant that they stand alone without a community.
swahili: Hakuna mdogo asiye na ndugu zake
commentary_en: Everyone belongs to a clan and therefore has a network. No-one stands alone.
commentary_dg: Chila mutu ni wa ukoo fulani, na kpwa hivyo ana atu akpwe. Takuna mutu mdide sana hata akale kadzangbwekala na mwandzawe.
idiomatic_sw: Kila mtu, hata mdogo kiasi gani, ana jamaa na ukoo wake.
commentary_sw: Kila mtu ni wa ukoo fulani na kwa hivyo ana mtandao wake; hakuna mtu anayesimama peke yake.

literal_en: There is no market which has no appointment
idiomatic_en: Nothing worthwhile happens without planning.
commentary_en: Everything has to be planned
commentary_dg: Chila chitu chinahitaji kupangbwa mwandzo. Takuna chete wala shughuli yoyosi isiyokala na mpango.
idiomatic_sw: Kila jambo lazima lipangwe mapema ili lifanikiwe.
commentary_sw: Kila kitu kinahitaji kupangwa.

digo: Takuna kanga wa kare na moho
literal_en: You don’t get an old guinea fowl and fire
idiomatic_en: Even the experienced can fall into danger if they are careless.
commentary_en: Any guinea fowl that wanders too near the fire will end up in the cooking pot – old or young. An older person is just as capable of making mistakes as a younger person.
commentary_dg: Kanga yoyosi achisengerera moho andaishira kpwenye nyungu — mvyere wala mdide. Mutu mvyere anaweza kuhenda makosa dza mutu mdide.
idiomatic_sw: Hata mtu mzee anaweza kufanya makosa kama mtu kijana.
commentary_sw: Kanga yeyote anayekaribia moto ataishia kupikwa—mzee au kijana. Mtu mzee anaweza kufanya makosa kama kijana.

literal_en: There is no joking about the throat
idiomatic_en: When someone threatens your life, take them seriously.
commentary_en: If someone goes for your throat you know they are serious about harming you and not fooling around
commentary_dg: Mutu achikugbwira umiro unamanya kala ana nia ya kukudhuru kpwa kpweli, si machani. Mambo ga umiro tagahendwa machani.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu akikushika koo, ujue hana mchezo.
commentary_sw: Mtu akikushika koo unajua ana nia ya kukudhuru kweli kweli, si mchezo.

literal_en: No one gets old on a journey
idiomatic_en: Providence will sustain you wherever your journey takes you.
commentary_en: God will provide while you are travelling
commentary_dg: Mlungu andakupha mahitaji gako uchere charoni. Takuna mutu atsaparaye kpwa kusakala [?] safarini.
idiomatic_sw: Mungu atakuruzuku popote uendapo.
commentary_sw: Mungu atatoa mahitaji yako wakati unapokuwa safarini.

literal_en: There is no person who is so tall he sleeps in two beds
idiomatic_en: No matter how great you are, your needs are no more than anyone else's.
swahili: Hakuna mtu mrefu aliyepita vitanda viwili
commentary_en: Even a very tall person only has one bed to sleep in.
commentary_dg: Hata mutu mure sana ana tsaga mwenga tu ya kulala. Takuna mutu ariye mkulu sana hata mahitaji gakpwe gakale zaidi ga mutu wanjina.
idiomatic_sw: Hata mtu mkubwa kiasi gani, anahitaji kitanda kimoja tu.
commentary_sw: Hata mtu mrefu sana ana kitanda kimoja tu cha kulala.

literal_en: Things cannot be pounded if the pestles are not upright
idiomatic_en: False accusations can become self-fulfilling prophecies.
commentary_en: When there is no maize to pound the pestles are left lying on the ground. E.g. You slander someone and say untrue things about them to their superiors. If the slandered person then goes on to do the things you have unjustly accused them of, you will feel vindicated and will report them with greater confidence. By your earlier accusations you had ‘lifted the pestle upright.’
commentary_dg: Mitsi isiphosimama taphana cha kupondwa. Uchimsingizia mutu na badaye akahenda gago urigomshitaki, undadziona u mwenye haki. Mashtaka gako ga mwandzo ndigo garigosimamisha mitsi.
idiomatic_sw: Shtaka la uongo linaweza kugeuka kuwa ukweli siku moja.
commentary_sw: Mchi usiposimamishwa hakuna kitu cha kupondwa. Ukimsingizia mtu kisha akafanya unalomshtaki, utajisikia umethibitishwa. Mashtaka yako ya awali yalikuwa 'kusimamisha mchi.'

literal_en: There is nothing inside, there is nothing inside means that they are inside
idiomatic_en: Those who insist nothing is inside are the ones hiding something.
commentary_en: A proverb about deception and denial. When someone insists too strongly that nothing is hidden, it usually means they are the ones concealing something. Used to expose those who protest their innocence too loudly.
commentary_dg: Ndarira kuhusu udanganyifu na kukatala. Mutu achishindilia sana kukala takuna chirichofisirwa, mara nyinji ndiye anayefisira chitu. Hutumika kumfichua mutu anayekatala kpwa nguvu kupishira kiasi.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayesisitiza hakuna kitu ndani mara nyingi ndiye anayeficha kitu.
commentary_sw: Methali kuhusu udanganyifu na kukataa. Mtu anaposhindilia sana kwamba hakuna kilichofichwa, mara nyingi ndiye anayeficha kitu. Hutumika kumfichua mtu anayekataa kwa nguvu kupita kiasi.

literal_en: Abundance cannot be peeped into
idiomatic_en: True wealth does not need to be inspected or verified.
commentary_en: When something is truly abundant, there is no need to peer at it closely or count it. Said to discourage suspicion or scrutiny of someone whose wealth or ability is self-evident. Also used to rebuke envy.
commentary_dg: Chitu chikakala chinji kpwa kpweli, takuna haja ya kuchitsungurira au kukuhesabu. Husemwa kukatisha wasiwasi au uchunguzi wa mutu ambaye utajiri au uwezo wakpwe unaonekana wazi. Piya hutumika kukemea wivu.
idiomatic_sw: Utajiri wa kweli haupaswi kuchunguliwa wala kuthibitishwa.
commentary_sw: Kitu kikiwa kingi kwa kweli, hakuna haja ya kukichungulia au kukihesabu. Husemwa kukatisha wasiwasi au uchunguzi wa mtu ambaye utajiri au uwezo wake unaonekana wazi. Pia hutumika kukemea wivu.

idiomatic_en: Those who do the actual work are the ones who reap the reward.
swahili: Mshika punda ndiye mla nyama yake
commentary_en: The one who holds the donkey is the one who eats its meat. Teaches that reward follows direct effort — those who do the actual work are the ones who deserve and receive the benefit. Said to encourage taking initiative rather than waiting for others.
commentary_dg: Anayetsaha bindoni [?] ndiye anayerya nyama yakpwe. Inafundisha kukala malipo ganafuata juhudi ya moja kpwa moja — anaohumira kpwa kpweli ndiyo anaostahili kuphaha faida. Husemwa kuhimiza kuhala hatua badala ya kugodza atu anjina.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayeshika kazi ndiye anayefaidika nayo.
commentary_sw: Anayeshika punda ndiye anayekula nyama yake. Inafundisha kwamba malipo hufuata juhudi ya moja kwa moja — wanaofanya kazi halisi ndio wanaostahili na kupokea faida. Husemwa kuhimiza kuchukua hatua badala ya kusubiri wengine.

literal_en: The contribution of a Digo person is slander
idiomatic_en: A Digo person's way of contributing is often through gossip.
commentary_en: Many Digos go around slandering each other for their own gain
commentary_dg: Adigo anji anaphiya hiku na hiku kusingizia ayawao ili aphate faidha yao enye. Hiri ni tabiya ya kudzisengenyeza ambayo inaaharibu.
idiomatic_sw: Wadigo wengi wanasengenyeza kwa faida yao wenyewe.
commentary_sw: Wadigo wengi wanakwenda huku na huku wakisengenyeza ili wapate faida yao.

idiomatic_en: Persistent provocation between people will eventually escalate into irreparable harm.
commentary_en: A warning against persistent provocation. If you keep teasing and provoking each other, eventually you will gouge out each other's eyes — the conflict will escalate beyond repair. Used by elders to stop quarrels before they become dangerous.
commentary_dg: Onyo dhidi ya uchokozi wa mara kpwa mara. Muchiendelea kutsokozana, mwisho mndaombolana matso — lungo rindapanda zaidi ya uwezo wa kurisuluhisha. Hutumika ni avyere kusimamisha lungo kabila taridzakala hatari.
idiomatic_sw: Ukiendelea kuchokozana, mtaishia kuumizana vibaya.
commentary_sw: Onyo dhidi ya uchokozi wa mara kwa mara. Ukiendelea kuchokozana, hatimaye mtang'oana macho — ugomvi utapanda zaidi ya uwezo wa kusuluhisha. Hutumika na wazee kusimamisha ugomvi kabla haujawa hatari.

idiomatic_en: If you keep provoking others recklessly, you will eventually stir up a force you cannot control.
commentary_en: A companion warning to 'tsoko-tsoko yenu mndaombolana matso.' The 'garege' is a large, dangerous creature. If you keep provoking carelessly, you will eventually provoke something far more powerful than you can handle.
commentary_dg: Onyo rinaro andamana na 'tsoko-tsoko yenu mndaombolana matso.' Garerego ni chiumbe chikulu na hatari. Uchiendelea kutsokola bila kujali, mwisho undatsokola chitu chenye nguvu zaidi ya uwezo wako.
idiomatic_sw: Ukiendelea kuchokoza, utaamsha hasira zisizoweza kudhibitiwa.
commentary_sw: Onyo linaloandamana na 'choko choko yenu mtaombolezana macho.' Garege ni kiumbe kikubwa na hatari. Ukiendelea kuchokoza bila kujali, hatimaye utachokoza kitu chenye nguvu zaidi ya uwezo wako.

digo: Tsukizi za mchizi ni raha ya mvuvi
literal_en: The cuttle fish's anger is the fisherman's delight
idiomatic_en: One person's misfortune is another's gain.
swahili: Hasira za pweza ni furaha ya mvuvi
commentary_en: A coastal proverb drawn from fishing life. When a cuttlefish releases its ink in anger, it makes itself visible and easier to catch — its defensive reaction becomes the fisherman's advantage. Used to describe situations where someone's anger works against them and benefits their adversary.
commentary_dg: Ndarira ya pwani kula maishani ga uvuvi. Mchizi achihenda tsukizi na kulavya wino wakpwe, anadzihendera aonekane na kukala rahisi kuhalwa — ulinzi wakpwe unakala raha ya mvuvi. Hutumika kuelezea hali ambapo tsukizi za mutu zinamuhumira vibaya na kumfaidisha adui wakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Hasira za mtu mmoja zinaweza kumfaidisha mtu mwingine.
commentary_sw: Methali ya pwani kutoka maishani mwa uvuvi. Pweza anapotoa wino wake kwa hasira, anajifanya aonekane na kuwa rahisi kukamatwa — ulinzi wake unakuwa faida ya mvuvi. Hutumika kuelezea hali ambapo hasira za mtu zinafanya kazi dhidi yake na kumfaidisha adui wake.

literal_en: Bad tobacco begins at the stem
idiomatic_en: A bad outcome can be traced back to bad beginnings.
commentary_en: Observes that bad outcomes can be traced to their origins. Poor tobacco starts at the stem — the defect begins at the root. Used to warn that if the foundation of something is flawed, the result will be bad. Applied to parenting, leadership, and planning.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala matokeo mabaya ganaweza kufuatirwa hadi asili yakpwe. Tumbaku iyi inaandza kolo — kasoro inaandza mzizini. Hutumika kuonya kukala msingi ukakala mubaya, matokeo gandakala mabaya. Inatumika kuhusu malezi, ulongozi, na mipango.
idiomatic_sw: Matokeo mabaya huanzia mwanzo mbaya.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kwamba matokeo mabaya yanaweza kufuatiliwa hadi asili yake. Tumbaku mbaya inaanzia shina — kasoro inaanzia mzizi. Hutumika kuonya kwamba msingi ukiwa mbaya, matokeo yatakuwa mabaya. Hutumika kuhusu malezi, uongozi, na mipango.

idiomatic_en: Every problem has a known origin that cannot be concealed.
commentary_en: References the Mwabajiro clan or place, suggesting that every matter has a known origin or source that cannot be hidden. Used when someone tries to conceal the cause of a problem, reminding them that the truth of origins is always traceable.
commentary_dg: Inahusu ukoo au phatu pha Mwabajiro, ikionyesha kukala chila lungo rina chanzo chinacho manywa ambacho tachiweza kufisirwa. Hutumika mutu achijeza kufisira sababu ya tatizo, kumkumbusha kukala ukpweli wa asili wakati wosi unafuatika.
idiomatic_sw: Kila jambo lina chanzo chake ambacho kinajulikana.
commentary_sw: Inahusu ukoo au mahali pa Mwabajiro, ikipendekeza kwamba kila jambo lina chanzo kinachojulikana ambacho hakiwezi kufichwa. Hutumika mtu anapojaribu kuficha sababu ya tatizo, kumkumbusha kwamba ukweli wa asili daima unafuatika.

literal_en: If you hide an illness you will be spoken of in mourning
idiomatic_en: If you conceal a problem, it will grow until it destroys you.
commentary_en: A hidden problem will get so bad you can no longer hide it, especially if you don’t seek help or take action.
commentary_dg: Tatizo uchirifwitsa rindakala baya hadi usiweze kurifwitsa tsona. Ukongo uchikufwitswa, atu andakusemera kpwa chiriro badala ya kukuterya.
idiomatic_sw: Tatizo ukiilificha litakua hadi lishindwe kufichika tena.
commentary_sw: Tatizo lililofichwa litazidi kuwa baya hadi usiweze kulifcha tena, hasa usipotafuta msaada au kuchukua hatua.

digo: Uchihega kanga na kpwiru undarya manga mhuphu
idiomatic_en: Patient nurturing through hardship eventually yields sweet rewards.
swahili: Ukimhega kanga kwa kiu utakula manga mbivu
commentary_en: If you tend to a guinea fowl patiently through its thirst, you will eventually eat ripe mangoes — meaning patience in nurturing yields sweet rewards. Used to encourage persistence in a long-term endeavour, especially farming or raising children.
commentary_dg: Uchihega kanga kpwa kpwiru, mwisho undarya manga mhuphu — maana yakpwe kuvumirira kpwa kurera kunareha matunda manono. Hutumika kuhimiza kuvumirira kpwa juhudi za muda mure, hasa ulimi au kurera ana.
idiomatic_sw: Subira na bidii huleta matunda mazuri hatimaye.
commentary_sw: Ukimtunza kanga kwa subira kupitia kiu yake, hatimaye utakula manga mbivu — maana yake subira katika kulea huleta matunda matamu. Hutumika kuhimiza uvumilivu katika juhudi za muda mrefu, hasa kilimo au kulea watoto.

digo: Uchihega na mwerevu mriche akaondze iye
idiomatic_en: A clever person does not need your help to solve their own problems — step aside and let them handle it.
swahili: Ukihega na mwerevu mwache akaondoe mwenyewe
commentary_en: When you are dealing with a clever person, let them solve their own problems — they do not need your intervention. Can also imply: do not try to outsmart the clever, for they will find their own way out. Used when someone meddles in the affairs of a capable person.
commentary_dg: Uchishughulika na mutu mwerevu, mriche aondze matatizo gakpwe mwenye — kahitaji msada wako. Inaweza piya kumaanisha: usijeze kumshinda mwerevu, mana iye ndaphaha njira yakpwe mwenye. Hutumika mutu achidzi shughulisha na mambo ga mutu mwenye uwezo.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwerevu hawezi kushindwa na tatizo lake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Unaposhughulika na mtu mwerevu, mwache atatue matatizo yake mwenyewe — hahitaji msaada wako. Inaweza pia kumaanisha: usijaribu kumshinda mwerevu, kwa sababu atapata njia yake mwenyewe. Hutumika mtu anapojishughulisha na mambo ya mtu mwenye uwezo.

literal_en: If you lose out at the boma/cattleshed, wait for it in the pot
idiomatic_en: If you miss an opportunity now, be patient and it may come again in another form.
commentary_en: If you find the cow you want to buy is too expensive, don’t lose hope. It will eventually be slaughtered and you may get to eat some of the meat of that very same cow one way or another.
commentary_dg: Ng'ombe unayemlonda uchimkosa chaani kpwa sababu a ghali, usikate tamaa. Hatimaye andatsinjwa na unaweza kuphaha nyama yakpwe kpwa njia yanjina.
idiomatic_sw: Ukipoteza fursa moja, subiri—fursa nyingine itakuja kwa njia tofauti.
commentary_sw: Ng'ombe unayetaka kununua akiwa ghali sana, usikate tamaa. Hatimaye atachinjwa na unaweza kupata nyama yake kwa njia nyingine.

literal_en: If you follow the dance then dance
idiomatic_en: If you commit to something, give it your full effort.
commentary_en: Do the thing you have come to do and don’t be distracted by other things.
commentary_dg: Henda dzambo urirokpwedza kuriihenda, usisumbuliwe ni mambo ganjina. Uchilunga ngoma, ni ufwihe na siyo uangalire tu.
idiomatic_sw: Kitu ulichokuja kufanya, kifanye kikamilifu, usisumbuliwe na mambo mengine.
commentary_sw: Fanya jambo ulilokuja kulifanya na usisumbuliwe na mambo mengine.

literal_en: If you know this your friend knows that
idiomatic_en: Nobody has a monopoly on knowledge; everyone has something to teach.
commentary_en: A proverb promoting intellectual humility and mutual respect. No one person knows everything; your companion may possess knowledge you lack. Used to settle arguments where someone claims to be the sole authority, reminding them that wisdom is distributed.
commentary_dg: Ndarira inayohimiza unyenyekevu wa kiakili na ishima ya pande zosi. Takuna mutu anayemanya chila chitu; myao anaweza kukala na umanyi ambao uwe kuna. Hutumika kusuluhisha malongo ambapo mutu anadai kukala ndiye mwenye achili peke yakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Hakuna mtu anayejua kila kitu; kila mtu ana ujuzi wake.
commentary_sw: Methali inayohimiza unyenyekevu wa kiakili na heshima ya pande zote. Hakuna mtu anajua kila kitu; mwenzako anaweza kuwa na ujuzi ambao wewe huna. Hutumika kusuluhisha mabishano ambapo mtu anadai kuwa mamlaka pekee, kumkumbusha kwamba hekima imegawanywa.

literal_en: When you go hunting don’t chose an animal
idiomatic_en: Take whatever opportunity comes your way without being too choosy.
commentary_en: You kill whatever comes before you
commentary_dg: Uchiphiya windzani, uwe tayari kuolaga nyama yoyosi indayotsembuka. Usikale uchitsambula mana taumanya utakpwendza ni utu wani.
idiomatic_sw: Usichague sana; chukua fursa inayokuja mbele yako.
commentary_sw: Uwindaji, waua mnyama yeyote anayetokea mbele yako.

digo: Uchirya kuku wa myao marondo galola kpwako
idiomatic_en: If you wrong your neighbour, the evidence will inevitably lead back to your door.
swahili: Ukila kuku wa mwenzako, marondo yataleta kwako
commentary_en: A warning about reciprocity and karma. If you eat your neighbour's chicken, the feathers will find their way to your doorstep — your wrongdoing will be traced back to you. Used to discourage theft or taking advantage of others.
commentary_dg: Onyo kuhusu malipo. Uchirya kuku wa myao, marondo gandaona njira hadi mlangoni kpwako — uibu wako undafuatirwa hadi kpwako. Hutumika kukatisha wivi au kutumia atu anjina vibaya.
idiomatic_sw: Ukifanya ubaya kwa mtu, ubaya utakurudia kwako mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Onyo kuhusu malipo na karma. Ukila kuku wa jirani yako, marondo yatapata njia hadi mlangoni kwako — uovu wako utafuatiliwa hadi kwako. Hutumika kukatisha wizi au kutumia wengine vibaya.

digo: Uchirya na chipofu, usimgbwire mkpwono 332.Uchirya, rya cha tele, cha sinda saza f
literal_en: When you eat with a blind person, do not hold their hand When you eat, eat until you are full, save some P or tomorrow
idiomatic_en: Do not take advantage of someone's vulnerability; eat well but save for tomorrow.
swahili: Ukila na kipofu, usimshike mkono; ukila kula cha tele, cha kushinda weka kwa kesho
commentary_en: lan ahead
commentary_dg: Usimuhendere vibaya mutu ambaye kaona kpwa sababu ya upofu wakpwe. Piya, urye cha kutosha ela ubakize cha machero. Panga mapema.
idiomatic_sw: Usimdhulumu mtu asiyeona, na ukila kula kwa kiasi—weka akiba kwa siku zijazo.
commentary_sw: Panga mapema.

literal_en: If you want something from underneath something you must be prepared to bend down
idiomatic_en: If you want something of value, you must be willing to humble yourself to get it.
commentary_en: Teaches that valuable things require effort and sometimes humility to obtain. You must bend down to reach what is under the bed — you cannot get it while standing upright. Used to encourage persistence and willingness to humble oneself for a worthy goal.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala vitu vyenye thamani vinahitaji juhudi na wakati mwingine unyenyekevu kuphaha. Ni lazima uzame kufikira chiricho kpwa mvunguririni — tauweza kukuphaha ukakala umesimama wima. Hutumika kuhimiza kuvumirira na utayari wa kudzinyenyekeza kpwa lengo rinaro stahili.
idiomatic_sw: Ukitaka kitu chenye thamani, lazima ujinyenyekeze kupata.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kwamba vitu vyenye thamani vinahitaji juhudi na wakati mwingine unyenyekevu kupata. Lazima uiname kufikia kilicho chini ya kitanda — huwezi kukipata ukiwa umesimama wima. Hutumika kuhimiza uvumilivu na utayari wa kujinyenyekeza kwa lengo linalostahili.

literal_en: If you laugh at the first signs of leprosy you will become a leper
idiomatic_en: Take early warning signs seriously or face worse consequences.
commentary_en: Take the early signs of illness seriously
commentary_dg: Dalili za mwandzo za ukongo uchizitsekera, zindakukpwedzera ukongo mkulu zaidi. Henda makini na mambo madide kabila hayadzakala makulu.
idiomatic_sw: Usipuuze dalili za mapema za tatizo, vinginevyo litakuwa baya zaidi.
commentary_sw: Chukua dalili za mapema za ugonjwa kwa uzito.

literal_en: The greatness of the ‘bird’ comes and goes
idiomatic_en: Greatness is fleeting; fame and power come and go.
swahili: Ukubwa wa mlamba ni kuuka na kurudi
commentary_en: mlamba – small black bird
commentary_dg: Mlamba ni nyuni mdide mwiiru ambaye nkutsembuka na kuyoyoma. Vivyo hivyo, ukulu wa mutu nkukpwedza na kuphiya — taukalire kpwa kare.
idiomatic_sw: Ukubwa na umaarufu havidumu; huja na kupita.
commentary_sw: Mlamba ni ndege mdogo mweusi.

literal_en: The bow is tight, the string is tight
idiomatic_en: When everything is well prepared, success will follow.
swahili: Upinde ukiwa imara, kamba ikiwa imara, utapiga vizuri
commentary_en: Therefore you will be able to shoot the arrow well.
commentary_dg: Uha ukikala mzuri na liphohe rikale kali, undaweza kupiga mshale vinono. Vitu vyosi vichitayarishwa vizuri, mafanikio gandakpwedza.
idiomatic_sw: Maandalizi mazuri huleta matokeo mazuri.
commentary_sw: Kwa hivyo utaweza kupiga mshale vizuri.

literal_en: Foolishness gives birth, cunning does not give birth
idiomatic_en: Simple people prosper through humility, while the cunning outsmart themselves.
commentary_en: Observes that simple, unassuming people often prosper and have children, while the overly cunning outsmart themselves into isolation. The 'clever' person overthinks and misses life's natural blessings. Used to caution against excessive scheming.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kukala atu rahisi na asio na kiburi mara nyinji hufanikiwa na kuphaha ana, ela enye ujanja kupishira kiasi anadzihendera upweke. Mutu 'mwerevu' anafikira kupishira kiasi na kukosa baraka za asili za maisha. Hutumika kuonya dhidi ya mipango kupishira kiasi.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mjinga lakini mnyenyekevu hufanikiwa zaidi kuliko mtu mwerevu anayejidanganya.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kwamba watu rahisi na wasio na kiburi mara nyingi hufanikiwa na kupata watoto, wakati wenye ujanja kupita kiasi wanajidanganya hadi upweke. Mtu 'mwerevu' anafikiri kupita kiasi na kukosa baraka za asili za maisha. Hutumika kuonya dhidi ya mipango kupita kiasi.

literal_en: You were deceived by the darkness, you urinated in the compound
idiomatic_en: Darkness cannot hide your misdeeds forever.
commentary_en: You did a stupid thing thinking that no-one would find out, but people saw you, so don’t think you can get away with it!
commentary_dg: Wahenda chitu cha chipumbavu uchiona giza rindakufwitsa, ela atu akuona. Phahi usidhani undaepuka, mana makosa gako gandamanyikana.
idiomatic_sw: Usifikiri giza litaficha makosa yako; watu watayaona.
commentary_sw: Ulifanya jambo la kipumbavu ukidhani hakuna atakayegundua, lakini watu walikuona, kwa hivyo usidhani utaepuka!

literal_en: You had sex in the fire and you burnt your pubic hair
idiomatic_en: Reckless behavior in dangerous situations leads to painful consequences.
commentary_en: A blunt, earthy warning against reckless behaviour in dangerous circumstances. The image is vivid and deliberately crude — engaging in intimacy near fire will burn you. Used to rebuke someone who takes foolish risks and then complains about the painful consequences.
commentary_dg: Onyo kali na ra moja kpwa moja dhidi ya tabiya ya hovyo kpwa mazingira ga hatari. Mfano ni wazi — kuhomba phephi na moho undaphya. Hutumika kumkemea mutu anayehala hatari za chipumbafu ndipho kulalamika kuhusu matokeo genye utsungu.
idiomatic_sw: Tabia ya hovyo katika hali ya hatari huleta matokeo mabaya.
commentary_sw: Onyo kali na la moja kwa moja dhidi ya tabia ya hovyo katika mazingira hatari. Picha ni wazi na makusudi mbichi — kufanya mambo ya karibu karibu na moto utaungua. Hutumika kumkemea mtu anayechukua hatari za kipumbavu kisha kulalamika kuhusu matokeo yenye uchungu.

literal_en: The noisy one does not kill
idiomatic_en: A loud approach yields no results; quiet action is more effective.
commentary_en: If you make a lot of noise while you are hunting the animals will all run away.
commentary_dg: Uchihenda hoyo unji uchiwinda, nyama osi andakutiya. Mutu wa hoyo tawuolaga mana nyama anamanya kabila hujamfikira.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye kelele nyingi hafanikiwi; kimya kinafanya kazi zaidi.
commentary_sw: Ukifanya kelele nyingi ukiwinda, wanyama wote watakimbia.

literal_en: The stubborn one does not kill
idiomatic_en: Stubbornness and refusal to cooperate lead to failure.
commentary_en: If this person doesn’t work with the team they will not succeed in the hunt.
commentary_dg: Mutu wa kani asiphohenda kazi na ayae, katafanikiwa windzani. Kani nkuharibu ushirikiano na ndiyo sababu ya kushindwa.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mkaidi asiyeshirikiana na wengine hatafanikiwa.
commentary_sw: Mtu huyu asipofanya kazi na timu, hatafanikiwa kuwinda.

literal_en: The one with a snake is morning
idiomatic_en: The best time to act is early in the morning.
commentary_en: If you want to kill a snake do it early in the morning before the sun falls on it. Snakes come out early in the morning to catch the sun before it is too hot and they go and hide. If you want to do something, the earlier the better.
commentary_dg: Nyoka nkutsembuka ligundzu mapema kupiga dzuwa kabila haridza kuphisa. Uchilonda kuhenda chitu, mapema ndipho bora mana ndipho unapho nguvu na nafasi.
idiomatic_sw: Ukitaka kufanya jambo, mapema ndio bora zaidi.
commentary_sw: Ukitaka kuua nyoka fanya asubuhi na mapema kabla jua haliajaipiga. Nyoka hutoka asubuhi kupata jua kabla joto haljaongezeka ndipo wanajificha. Ukitaka kufanya kitu, mapema ndio bora.

literal_en: Sickness increases at night
idiomatic_en: Suffering feels worst when you are alone in the dark.
commentary_en: Acknowledges that illness and suffering feel worse at night, when one is alone and the world is quiet. There is no distraction, no help readily available, and fear grows in darkness. Used to express sympathy for the sick, or to describe any hardship that worsens in solitude.
commentary_dg: Inakubali kukala ukongo na mateso ganahisika vibaya zaidi usiku, mutu achikala peke yakpwe na ulimwengu umenyamala. Takuna chitu cha kukusumbua, takuna msada wa haraka, na hofu inakula jizani. Hutumika kuonyesha huruma kpwa mukongo, au kuelezea ugumu wowosi unaozidi kpwa upweke.
idiomatic_sw: Maumivu huzidi zaidi usiku wakati uko peke yako.
commentary_sw: Inakubali kwamba ugonjwa na mateso huhisiwa vibaya zaidi usiku, mtu akiwa peke yake na ulimwengu umenyamaza. Hakuna kitu cha kukusumbua, hakuna msaada wa haraka, na hofu inakua gizani. Hutumika kuonyesha huruma kwa mgonjwa, au kuelezea ugumu wowote unaozidi katika upweke.

literal_en: The truth of a child is irritation
idiomatic_en: What children say is mostly an annoyance, not wisdom.
commentary_en: Children never have anything important to say
commentary_dg: Anache taasikirwa na atu akulu mana kpwao taphana maneno ga muhimu. Maneno gao ni chibako tu kpwa avyere.
idiomatic_sw: Maneno ya watoto mara nyingi ni usumbufu tu, si hekima.
commentary_sw: Watoto hawana lolote la muhimu la kusema kamwe.

idiomatic_en: Know where you come from so you can understand where you are going.
commentary_en: Teaches that self-knowledge is the foundation for navigating life. Know your own people and your own origins, so that you can understand where you are going. Used to advise young people not to forget their roots as they pursue new paths.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala kudzimanya ni msingi wa kupishira maisha. Manya atu ako na asili yako, ili uweze kuelewa uphiyako. Hutumika kushauri avulana na asichana asihosahau mizizi yao anapho fuata njira mphya.
idiomatic_sw: Ujue unatoka wapi ili ujue unakokwenda.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kwamba kujijua ni msingi wa kupitia maisha. Jua watu wako na asili yako, ili uweze kuelewa unakokwenda. Hutumika kushauri vijana wasisahau mizizi yao wanapofuata njia mpya.

idiomatic_en: The person you assume someone to be is often not who they truly are.
commentary_en: Warns against making assumptions about people. The person you think you are speaking to — or about — may not be who you believe them to be. Used to caution against judging by appearances or gossip, reminding that people are more complex than our assumptions.
commentary_dg: Inaonya dhidi ya kuhenda mawazo kuhusu atu. Mutu unaiyemuamba — au kumhusu — anaweza asikale unavyomfikira. Hutumika kuonya dhidi ya kuhukumu kpwa sura au uvumi, ikikumbusha kukala atu ni changamano [?] zaidi ya mawazo gehu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu unayemdhania si yeye unavyomfikiri.
commentary_sw: Inaonya dhidi ya kufanya mawazo kuhusu watu. Mtu unayedhani unamzungumza — au kumhusu — anaweza asiwe unavyomfikiri. Hutumika kuonya dhidi ya kuhukumu kwa sura au uvumi, ikikumbusha kwamba watu ni changamano zaidi ya mawazo yetu.

digo: Undatsapa na phaphi na madiya ga Tiwi?
literal_en: How will you pass the place where the Tiwi dogs are
idiomatic_en: Some obstacles are too great to overcome by yourself.
swahili: Utapitaje mahali penye mbwa wa Tiwi?
commentary_en: The Tiwi dogs are many and fierce. Many interpretations incl. You can’t pass through a place with tight security. There is nothing you can do to please all people.
commentary_dg: Madiya ga Tiwi ni manji na makali sana. Phahi taphana njia ya kutsapa phatu phenye usalama wa hali ya dzulu. Vivyo hivyo, tauwezi kumridhisha kila mutu.
idiomatic_sw: Vikwazo vingine ni vikubwa mno kushindwa peke yako.
commentary_sw: Mbwa wa Tiwi ni wengi na wakali. Tafsiri nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na: Huwezi kupita mahali penye ulinzi mkali. Huwezi kumridhisha kila mtu.

literal_en: Much is a pestle
idiomatic_en: There is strength in numbers; the majority has power.
commentary_en: If you can’t beat them join them
commentary_dg: Atu anji achikala phamwenga, ana nguvu dza mutsi wa kupondwa. Usiphoweza kuashinda, jiunge nao mana unji ndio nguvu.
idiomatic_sw: Umoja ni nguvu; wengi wakiungana, wanashinda.
commentary_sw: Usipoweza kuwashinda, jiunge nao.

digo: Usibere ngorowa, mana ngorowa ndiyo ngoma
literal_en: Don't despise the ngorowa dance because it is (a true) dance
idiomatic_en: Do not despise what is humble, for it is genuinely yours.
swahili: Usidharau ngorowa, maana ngorowa ndiyo ngoma
commentary_en: Don't despise your parents whatever their stature/nature because they are your own.
commentary_dg: Usidharau ngorowa hata ichikala ni ngoma ya kawaida, mana ndiyo ngoma yako ya kpweli. Vivyo hivyo usiadharau avyazio hata hali yao isiphokala bora, mana ni ako.
idiomatic_sw: Usidharau chako hata kikiwa duni, kwa sababu ni chako halisi.
commentary_sw: Usiwadharau wazazi wako hata kama hali yao si bora, kwa sababu ni wako.

literal_en: Don't buy dry cassava and dance
idiomatic_en: Do not celebrate before you are sure the outcome is good.
commentary_en: Buy carefully, then cook the cassava, only then will you know that it is good and you can rejoice.
commentary_dg: Usifurahi kabila hujamanya ichikala makopa ni manono. Gule kpwa uangalifu, kisha uyapike, ndipho utsamanya ni ganono na utafurahi.
idiomatic_sw: Usifurahi mapema kabla haujahakikisha matokeo ni mazuri.
commentary_sw: Nunua kwa uangalifu, kisha pika muhogo; hapo ndipo utajua ni mzuri na utaweza kufurahi.

digo: Usiguze ga kaphuni na kuno/mbavi ga mafungo bado
literal_en: Don’t sell from the basket while you still have your heaps (of vegetables)
idiomatic_en: Finish what you have at hand before starting something new.
swahili: Usiuze kwa kikapu na huku marundo bado yapo
commentary_en: Deal with the matter in hand first of all, then take care of other things
commentary_dg: Marizira [?] mambo urigonago mwandzo, kisha ukashughulikie mambo ganjina. Usiguze kpwa kaphuni kuno mafungo bado — phahi henda ra kpwandzo kpwandzo.
idiomatic_sw: Maliza jambo unaloshughulikia kwanza, kisha shughulikia mambo mengine.
commentary_sw: Shughulikia jambo lililo mbele yako kwanza, kisha shughulikia mambo mengine.

literal_en: Don’t be like the one who asks for a sheep
idiomatic_en: Be direct; hesitation causes you to miss your chance.
commentary_en: … and doesn’t ask for it straight away. In other words go straight to the point or otherwise you might miss the boat!
commentary_dg: Uchilonda chitu, chivoye moja kpwa moja bila kuzungukazunguka. Usikale dza mvoya ng'ondzi ambaye kavoya chipore, mana unaweza kuikosa nafasi.
idiomatic_sw: Eleza unachohitaji moja kwa moja, la sivyo utakosa fursa.
commentary_sw: ...asiyeomba moja kwa moja. Kwa maneno mengine, eleza haja yako moja kwa moja au unaweza kupoteza nafasi!

literal_en: Clothes are not sewn in the dark of the night
idiomatic_en: You cannot do good work without the right conditions.
commentary_en: Emphasises that good work requires proper conditions. You cannot sew in darkness — you need light, clarity, and the right circumstances to produce quality results. Used to counsel patience when conditions are not yet right for action.
commentary_dg: Inasisitiza kukala kazi nzuri inahitaji mazingira ganagofaa. Tauweza kushona jizani — unahitaji mwanga, uwazi, na mazingira ga sawa kutoa matokeo manono. Hutumika kushauri kuvumirira wakati mazingira bado tagana faa kpwa hatua.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kufanya kazi nzuri bila mazingira yanayofaa.
commentary_sw: Inasisitiza kwamba kazi nzuri inahitaji mazingira yanayofaa. Huwezi kushona gizani — unahitaji mwanga, uwazi, na mazingira sahihi kutoa matokeo bora. Hutumika kushauri subira wakati mazingira bado hayajafaa kwa hatua.

literal_en: Don’t despise a bad smell, if you grill it it’s food (stew)
idiomatic_en: Do not judge something by its outward appearance; it may prove valuable.
swahili: Usidharau harufu mbaya; ukikaanga ni kitoweo
commentary_en: The smell is made by the ‘fungo’ (civet cat) which leaves its trace on a tree. However, if you kill the animal you can eat it.
commentary_dg: Harufu iyo ni ya fungo anayerichira alama yakpwe muhini. Hata hivyo, uchiolaga nyama huyu unaweza kumrya. Usihukumu chitu kpwa kuonekana kpwakpwe.
idiomatic_sw: Usihukumu kitu kwa sura yake ya nje; kinaweza kuwa na thamani.
commentary_sw: Harufu hiyo ni ya fungo (paka wa pori) anayeacha alama yake mtini. Hata hivyo, ukimuua mnyama huyu unaweza kumla.

literal_en: Do not play around with skin complaints, when they burst they become leprosy
idiomatic_en: Do not ignore minor ailments, for they can become serious diseases.
commentary_en: A medical proverb warning against ignoring small ailments. Minor skin complaints, if neglected, can burst open and become leprosy — a dreaded, incurable condition. Applied broadly to any small problem that, if ignored, can become catastrophic.
commentary_dg: Ndarira ya udakitari [?] inayoonya dhidi ya kupuuza magonjwa madide. Vyoga vidide, vikipuuzwa, vinaweza kuhumbuka na kukala mahana — ukongo wa kutisha usio na dawa. Hutumika kpwa upana kpwa tatizo rorosi didide ambaro, rikipuuzwa, rinaweza kukala janga.
idiomatic_sw: Usipuuze magonjwa madogo; yanaweza kuwa matatizo makubwa.
commentary_sw: Methali ya kitabibu inayoonya dhidi ya kupuuza magonjwa madogo. Matatizo madogo ya ngozi, yakipuuzwa, yanaweza kupasuka na kuwa ukoma — ugonjwa wa kutisha usio na tiba. Hutumika kwa upana kwa tatizo lolote dogo ambalo, likipuuzwa, linaweza kuwa janga.

digo: Utsapapho pore ndipho wedzapho ukaphatwapa mairo
literal_en: The place you pass slowly is the place where in the future you may be running past
idiomatic_en: Where you once walked slowly seeking favors, you may later run past avoiding debts.
commentary_en: When you want something from a person you approach them in a very friendly manner, but when it comes to paying back you run away from them.
commentary_dg: Unapholonda chitu kpwa mutu, unamsengerera pole-pole na urafiki, ela linapofika wakati wa kuripha, unakutiya. Phatu uphatsapapho pore ndipho undaphatsapa mairo.
idiomatic_sw: Mahali unapopita kwa utulivu ukiomba msaada, siku nyingine utapita mbio ukikimbia deni.
commentary_sw: Unapotaka kitu kwa mtu, unakaribia kwa urafiki sana, lakini linapofika wakati wa kulipa, unakimbia.

literal_en: If you laugh at a skin disease you will get leprosy
idiomatic_en: Do not mock others' misfortunes, lest something worse befall you.
commentary_en: Don’t make fun of other people’s misfortunes as something worse may happen to you
commentary_dg: Usitsekere misiba ya atu anjina mana dzambo baya zaidi rinaweza kukutimukira uwe mwenye. Kuchitsekera vyoga vya mwenzio ni kudziihira mahana.
idiomatic_sw: Usicheke msiba wa mtu mwingine; janga baya zaidi linaweza kukupata wewe.
commentary_sw: Usicheke misiba ya watu wengine kwa sababu jambo baya zaidi linaweza kukutokea wewe.

literal_en: The public is (like) a pestle, it cannot be folded You cannot manipulate the public
idiomatic_en: Public opinion is unyielding; you cannot bend it to your will.
commentary_en: The wind represents the 'big man' or powerful person. For a poor person to succeed, they need the patronage of someone powerful. The wind that fans the fire is outside the fire itself — the helper is external but essential.
commentary_dg: Utsi unawakilisha 'mutu mukulu' au mutu mwenye nguvu. Mchiya achimendza kufanikiwa, anahitaji ulinzi wa mutu mwenye mamlaka. Dza vira mutsi taupindika, vivyo hivyo maoni ga utsi — ga atu anji — tagaweza kupindishwa.
idiomatic_sw: Maoni ya umma ni imara kama mchi; hayawezi kupindishwa.
commentary_sw: Upepo unawakilisha 'mtu mkubwa' au mtu mwenye nguvu. Ili maskini afanikiwe, anahitaji ulinzi wa mtu mwenye mamlaka. Upepo unaochochea moto uko nje ya moto wenyewe — msaidizi ni wa nje lakini muhimu.

digo: Utsungu wa mwana umanywa ni mvyazi
literal_en: The bitterness/pain of the child is known by the parent (one who gave birth)
idiomatic_en: Only a parent truly understands their child's suffering.
commentary_en: Expresses the deep bond between parent and child. Only the one who bore the child can truly feel the child's pain. Used to validate a parent's grief or worry, acknowledging that outsiders cannot fully understand the depth of parental concern.
commentary_dg: Inaelezea uhusiano wa ndani kahi ya mvyazi na mwana. Ni yuya ariyemvyala tu anayeweza kuhisi kpwa kpweli utsungu wa mwana. Hutumika kuthibitisha sonono au wasiwasi wa mvyazi, ikitambua kukala atu a konze taamanya kpwa kpweli kina cha wasiwasi wa mvyazi.
idiomatic_sw: Ni mzazi peke yake anayeelewa kikamilifu maumivu ya mtoto wake.
