commentary_sw: Inaelezea uhusiano wa kina kati ya mzazi na mtoto. Ni yule aliyemzaa tu anayeweza kuhisi kweli uchungu wa mtoto. Hutumika kuthibitisha huzuni au wasiwasi wa mzazi, ikitambua kwamba watu wa nje hawawezi kuelewa kikamilifu kina cha wasiwasi wa mzazi.

idiomatic_en: No one chooses the circumstances of their birth, so do not judge others for theirs.
commentary_en: Reflects on the reality that no one chooses where or how they are born. Your birthplace and circumstances are given, not selected. Used to counsel acceptance and humility, and to discourage looking down on others for their origins.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha ukpweli kukala takuna mutu anayerikula phatu au jinsi anavyovyalwa. Phatu pha kuvyalwa na mazingira ni ga kuhewa, si kurikula. Hutumika kushauri kukubali na unyenyekevu, na kukatisha kudharau atu anjina kpwa asili yao.
idiomatic_sw: Mahali unapozaliwa huna hiari ya kuchagua.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha ukweli kwamba hakuna mtu anayechagua mahali au jinsi anavyozaliwa. Mahali pa kuzaliwa na mazingira ni ya kupewa, si kuchaguliwa. Hutumika kushauri kukubali na unyenyekevu, na kukatisha kudharau wengine kwa asili yao.

literal_en: How you are born is not how you become old
idiomatic_en: Life changes you; who you become is not who you were at birth.
commentary_en: Teaches that life transforms everyone. The helpless infant grows into something entirely different from what they were at birth. Used to encourage those going through difficult times — your current state does not define your future — and to caution the fortunate that their circumstances may change.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala maisha ganabadilisha chila mutu. Mwana mudide asiye na msada anakula kukala chitu tofauti kabisa na arivyokala wakati wa kuvyalwa. Hutumika kutiya moyo atu anapho pishira nyakati ngumu — hali yako ya rero taigaamua kpwako kpwa mbere.
idiomatic_sw: Maisha yanakubadilisha; unavyozaliwa si unavyozeeka.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kwamba maisha yanabadilisha kila mtu. Mtoto mchanga asiye na msaada anakua kuwa kitu tofauti kabisa na alivyokuwa wakati wa kuzaliwa. Hutumika kutia moyo wapitao nyakati ngumu — hali yako ya sasa haiamui mustakabali wako — na kuonya wenye bahati kwamba hali zao zinaweza kubadilika.

literal_en: Old age is a rubbish tip
idiomatic_en: Old age brings both blessings and burdens.
commentary_en: Both good and bad things are dumped on you
commentary_dg: Mutu achikala mvyere, mambo manono na mabaya gosi ganamgbwirira. Uvyere ni dza dzala — vitu vyosi vinatsuphwa dzuluye.
idiomatic_sw: Uzee huleta mambo mazuri na mabaya kwa pamoja.
commentary_sw: Mambo mazuri na mabaya yote yanatupiwa juu yako.

literal_en: Old age is tiring
idiomatic_en: Growing old is a heavy burden to bear.
commentary_en: A simple, poignant observation about the burden of old age. As the body weakens, every task becomes heavier. Used to express empathy for the elderly, and to remind the young to respect and assist their elders, for age spares no one.
commentary_dg: Uchunguzi rahisi na wenye kugusa kuhusu mzigo wa uvyere. Mwiri unaphodhoofika, chila kazi inakala ndzito. Hutumika kuonyesha huruma kpwa avyere, na kukumbusha avulana na asichana kuishimu na kusaidiya avyere ao, mana uvyere tauchagula mutu.
idiomatic_sw: Uzee ni mzigo mzito wa kubeba.
commentary_sw: Uchunguzi rahisi na wenye kugusa kuhusu mzigo wa uzee. Mwili unapodhoofika, kila kazi inakuwa nzito. Hutumika kuonyesha huruma kwa wazee, na kukumbusha vijana kuheshimu na kusaidia wazee wao, kwani uzee hauchagui mtu.

literal_en: Chase away those who are eating the maize
idiomatic_en: Deal with the immediate threat first before worrying about distant ones.
commentary_en: Refers to baboons in your maize. Chase them first before turning your attention to those who are still in the trees. Concentrate on the main matter in hand without being distracted by side issues.
commentary_dg: Fukuza nyani ariyomo mwako mwereni kpwandzo, kabila hujadzishughulisha na ario bado dzulu ya mihi. Shughulikia dzambo kulu uriho naro bila kusumbuliwa ni mambo madide.
idiomatic_sw: Shughulikia tatizo lililo mbele yako kwanza kabla ya kujishughulisha na mengine.
commentary_sw: Inahusu nyani waliomo shambani mwako. Wafukuze kwanza kabla ya kujishughulisha na wale walio bado mitini. Shughulikia jambo kuu bila kusumbuliwa na mambo madogo.

idiomatic_en: The danger you can see will not destroy you; it is the hidden threat that kills.
commentary_en: What you can see coming cannot kill you — it is the unseen danger that destroys. Used to encourage vigilance while also reassuring that known threats are manageable. The hidden enemy is far more dangerous than the visible one.
commentary_dg: Uchionererwago tachiweza kukuolaga — ni hatari isiyoonekana ndiyo inayoangamiza. Hutumika kuhimiza makini hata hivyo kutiya moyo kukala vitisho vinavyo manywa vinaweza kudhibitiwa. Adui ariyefisirwa ana hatari zaidi kuriko anayeonekana.
idiomatic_sw: Hatari unayoiona haiwezi kukudhuru kama ile isiyoonekana.
commentary_sw: Unachokiona kinakuja hakiwezi kukuua — ni hatari isiyoonekana ndiyo inayoangamiza. Hutumika kuhimiza uangalifu huku pia ukitoa faraja kwamba vitisho vinavyojulikana vinaweza kudhibitiwa. Adui aliyefichwa ni hatari zaidi kuliko anayeonekana.

literal_en: Good eats the fruit tree
idiomatic_en: Generosity can lead to one's own ruin.
commentary_en: Mnago is a type of fruit tree. The tree which is bearing fruit is destroyed by someone breaking off the branches to get the fruit. Refers to the good one has done, but I’m still unclear as to the relationship here!
commentary_dg: Mnago ni muhi wa matunda ambao atu anavundza matawige ili aphate matunda. Uzuri wa mutu unaweza kumuharibu mwenye, mana atu andamtumira hadi aishiwe.
idiomatic_sw: Mti wenye matunda mazuri ndio unaovunjwa matawi—wema wako unaweza kukuangamiza.
commentary_sw: Mnago ni aina ya mti wa matunda. Mti unaozaa matunda unaharibiwa na watu wanaovunja matawi kupata matunda. Inahusu wema uliotendeka, lakini uhusiano wake bado haueleweki wazi!

digo: Vinono vinatsupa na rome ra achiya
literal_en: Good things pass through the poor people’s teaching place
idiomatic_en: Good things often pass through the hands of the poor without benefiting them.
swahili: Vitu vizuri hupita kwenye baraza ya maskini
commentary_en: Observes the cruel irony that good things pass through the gathering places of the poor without benefiting them. The teaching place of the poor sees wealth flow past but never stop. Used to describe systemic inequality where the disadvantaged witness prosperity without sharing in it.
commentary_dg: Inaonyesha kejeli kali kukala vinono vinapishira rome ra achiya bila kuanufaisha. Rome ra achiya rinaona utajiri ukupishira ela tausimama kare. Hutumika kuelezea ukosefu wa usawa ambapo achiya anashuhudia ustawi bila kushiriki kpwenye.
idiomatic_sw: Mambo mazuri mara nyingi hupita mikononi mwa maskini bila kuwanufaisha.
commentary_sw: Inaonyesha kejeli kali kwamba vitu vizuri hupita katika maeneo ya kukusanyika ya maskini bila kuwanufaisha. Baraza ya maskini inaona utajiri ukipita lakini haukomi kamwe. Hutumika kuelezea ukosefu wa usawa wa kimfumo ambapo wasiojiweza wanashuhudia ustawi bila kushiriki ndani yake.

literal_en: Pull pull brings a cut
idiomatic_en: Constant pulling and strife leads to harm for all involved.
commentary_en: Two people pulling in opposite directions will break the rope
commentary_dg: Atu airi achivwehana kpwa pande mbiri-mbiri, hira kamba indadoka. Mvwehano wa kpwenderera nkureha madhara kpwa osi.
idiomatic_sw: Ugomvi wa kudumu huleta uharibifu kwa pande zote.
commentary_sw: Watu wawili wakivutana pande tofauti wataikata kamba.

digo: Vwindi kalamusa goyo na goyo kalamusa vwindi
literal_en: The front of the knee does not greet the back of the knee, and the back of the knee does not greet the front of the knee
idiomatic_en: Some things are so close yet can never meet or communicate.
swahili: Mbele ya goti halimusalimii nyuma, na nyuma ya goti halimusalimii mbele
commentary_en: Describes the paradox of proximity without connection. The front and back of the knee are part of the same body yet can never face each other or communicate. Used to describe estranged relatives, neighbours who never speak, or any situation where closeness does not produce understanding.
commentary_dg: Inaelezea chinachoshangaza cha ukaribu bila uhusiano. Vwindi na goyo ni sehemu za mwiri mmwenga ela taziweza kukabiliana au kulamusa kamwe. Hutumika kuelezea ndugu ariotengana, ajirani asio semesha, au hali yoyosi ambapo ukaribu tauzalishi uelewano.
idiomatic_sw: Vitu vingine viko karibu sana lakini haviwezi kukutana kamwe.
commentary_sw: Inaelezea kitendawili cha ukaribu bila uhusiano. Mbele na nyuma ya goti ni sehemu ya mwili mmoja lakini haziwezi kukabiliana au kuwasiliana kamwe. Hutumika kuelezea ndugu waliotengana, majirani wasiosemeshana, au hali yoyote ambapo ukaribu hauzalishi uelewano.

literal_en: the naughty child is your naughty child
idiomatic_en: Even a mischievous child is still your own, and you must accept them.
commentary_en: Of the children you are going to bear, some will not be as you expect. However, even if he is naughty sometimes, at other times he will be good and anyway he is still your child whatever.
commentary_dg: Kahi ya ana undaovyala, anjina taandakala unavyoatarajia. Ela hata mwana akale vyoni, ni mwanao mwenye na unapaswa kumkubali.
idiomatic_sw: Hata mtoto mbaya bado ni mtoto wako; lazima umkubali.
commentary_sw: Kati ya watoto utakaozaa, baadhi hawatakuwa unavyotarajia. Hata hivyo, hata akiwa mbaya wakati mwingine, wakati mwingine atakuwa mzuri, na bado ni mtoto wako.

literal_en: A person with an eye is not shone upon
idiomatic_en: A person with discernment does not need to be shown the way.
commentary_en: A person who is perceptive and wise does not need to have things pointed out or illuminated for them — they can see for themselves. Used to praise someone's intelligence, or to chide those who waste time explaining the obvious to a capable person.
commentary_dg: Mutu mwenye ufahamu na ikima kahitaji kuonyeshwa au kung'arirwa vitu — anaweza kuona mwenye. Hutumika kumsifu mutu kpwa achili zakpwe, au kumkemea anayepoteza wakati kuelezea chitu chiricho wazi kpwa mutu mwenye uwezo.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mwenye ufahamu hahitaji kuonyeshwa njia.
commentary_sw: Mtu mwenye ufahamu na hekima hahitaji kuonyeshwa au kuangaziwa vitu — anaweza kuona mwenyewe. Hutumika kumsifu mtu kwa akili yake, au kumkemea anayepoteza muda kuelezea dhahiri kwa mtu mwenye uwezo.

literal_en: The mad person is not chased naked
idiomatic_en: Do not lower yourself to the level of a fool when dealing with them.
commentary_en: If a madman is running around naked make sure you are fully dressed before you chase him. Don’t do as he does.
commentary_dg: Mutu wa koma achikala anazola chitsaha, usimzole nawe chitsaha. Uwe hakikisha ukale unavwala vinono kabila hujadzimfukuza. Usihende dza mutu wa koma.
idiomatic_sw: Usishuke hadi kiwango cha mpumbavu unaposhughulika naye.
commentary_sw: Mwendawazimu akikimbia uchi, hakikisha umevaa vizuri kabla ya kumfukuza. Usifanye kama yeye anavyofanya.

literal_en: The mad person is yours
idiomatic_en: The mad person in your family is still your responsibility.
commentary_en: Reminds that even a mad or difficult family member is still yours — you cannot disown them. Family bonds persist regardless of a person's condition or behaviour. Used to call families to responsibility for their troubled members rather than abandoning them.
commentary_dg: Inakumbusha kukala hata mutu wa mbari ariye na koma au mgumu bado ni wako — tauweza kumkatala. Uhusiano wa mbari unaendelea bila kujali hali au tabiya ya mutu. Hutumika kuiha mbari kuwajibika kpwa atu ao enye matatizo badala ya kuaricha.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu wa familia yako, hata akiwa na wazimu, bado ni jukumu lako.
commentary_sw: Inakumbusha kwamba hata mtu wa familia aliye na wazimu au mgumu bado ni wako — huwezi kumkataa. Uhusiano wa familia unaendelea bila kujali hali au tabia ya mtu. Hutumika kuwaita familia kuwajibika kwa wanachama wao wenye matatizo badala ya kuwaacha.

literal_en: The one with a great desire does not ripen
idiomatic_en: Impatience ruins the reward; the eager one does not wait for things to ripen.
commentary_en: If you desire fruit so much you will pick it before it is ready, or you won’t cook your meat properly
commentary_dg: Mutu wa kpwiru sana andachuma matunda kabila hayadzaiva, au kandapika nyama yakpwe vizuri. Kpwiru kunakuharibu kpwa kukuhendesa ukale na hima.
idiomatic_sw: Kutokuwa na subira kunaharibu matokeo; mtu mwenye pupa hasubiri kitu kiive.
commentary_sw: Ukitamani sana matunda, utayachuma kabla hayajaiva, au hutapika nyama yako vizuri.

literal_en: A stinking person does not know (it) himself
idiomatic_en: A person is often blind to their own faults.
commentary_en: Applies to many things such as pride, foolishness
commentary_dg: Mutu wa kunuka kadzimanya mwenye kukala ananuka. Vivyo hivyo, atu anji taamanya makosa gao enye dza kiburi na ujinga.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu mara nyingi haoni makosa yake mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Inatumika kwa mambo mengi kama kiburi na ujinga.

literal_en: The lustful one is never told to bend over
idiomatic_en: A willing person needs no persuasion.
commentary_en: ie. they never need to be told!
commentary_dg: Mutu wa likpwale kahitaji kuambwa dong'ola mana kayagodzera kuambwa. Mutu ariye tayari kahitaji kushawishiwa.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu aliye tayari hahitaji kushawishiwa.
commentary_sw: Yaani hawahitaji kuambiwa kamwe!

literal_en: The toothless one cannot not light a fire
idiomatic_en: Without the right tools, even simple tasks become impossible.
commentary_en: He cannot blow enough to light it
commentary_dg: Mutu wa maphengo kaweza kupuliza moho wa kutosha kuuwasha. Usiphokala na vifaa vya kufwaha, hata kazi nyepesi nayo indakushinda.
idiomatic_sw: Bila zana zinazofaa, hata kazi rahisi haiwezekani.
commentary_sw: Hawezi kupuliza vya kutosha kuuwasha moto.

literal_en: He has has one thing cannot be robbed of two things
idiomatic_en: You cannot lose more than you have.
commentary_en: A logical observation: if someone only has one thing, you cannot take two from them. Used to defend the poor against excessive demands, or to remind creditors and authorities that you cannot extract what does not exist. Also used philosophically — accept what you have and protect it.
commentary_dg: Uchunguzi wa kimantiki: mutu achikala na chitu chimwenga tu, tauweza kumuuywa viiri. Hutumika kualinda achiya dhidi ya madai ga kupishira kiasi, au kukumbusha wadai na mamlaka kukala tauweza kutoa chisicho kpwenye. Piya hutumika — kubali urichonacho na ukirinde.
idiomatic_sw: Huwezi kupoteza zaidi ya ulivyo nacho.
commentary_sw: Uchunguzi wa kimantiki: mtu akiwa na kitu kimoja tu, huwezi kumnyanganya viwili. Hutumika kuwalinda maskini dhidi ya madai ya kupita kiasi, au kukumbusha wadai na mamlaka kwamba huwezi kutoa kisichokuwepo. Pia hutumika kifalsafa — kubali ulichonacho na ukilinde.

digo: Wakala kanga wa tsongo achifukurira azima
literal_en: You were (like) a one-eyed guineafowl scratching for the (other) sighted ones
idiomatic_en: The one who works hard without seeing clearly benefits others more than themselves.
swahili: Ulikuwa kanga wa chongo ukiwafukurira wenye macho
commentary_en: The blind guineafowl cannot see all of the things he is scratching up so the other birds will come and take advantage of this.
commentary_dg: Kanga wa tsongo kavyona vyosi anavyovifukurira, phahi nyuni azima nkukpwedza kuchukuwa hivyo. Mutu ahendaye kazi kpwa nguvu ela kaona vinono, faida yakpwe nkuphiya kpwa ayae.
idiomatic_sw: Mtu anayefanya kazi kwa bidii bila kuona vizuri huwanufaisha wengine zaidi kuliko yeye mwenyewe.
commentary_sw: Kanga kipofu hawezi kuona vitu vyote anavyofukua, kwa hivyo ndege wengine watakuja kuvichukua.

literal_en: You ate ‘bombo’, you’ll pay for ‘bombo’
idiomatic_en: If you misuse something, you will eventually have to pay the price.
commentary_en: Meaning of ‘bombo’ uncertain. If you misuse something you will have to pay for it
commentary_dg: Uchitumira chitu vibaya, undaripha. Warya bombo, phahi hakikisha tayari kuripha bombo. Takuna dzambo ra bure duniani.
idiomatic_sw: Ukitumia kitu vibaya, utakulazimishwa kulipa bei yake.
commentary_sw: Maana ya 'bombo' haijulikani vizuri. Ukitumia kitu vibaya utalazimika kulipa.

idiomatic_en: Cleverness alone does not feed you — without honest work, cunning produces nothing.
commentary_en: Warns that cleverness alone does not put food on the table. A person can be shrewd and cunning, but if they do not work with their hands, their intelligence produces nothing. Used to rebuke those who rely on tricks instead of honest labour.
commentary_dg: Inaonya kukala werevu peke yakpwe tauwika chakurya mezani. Mutu anaweza kukala mwerevu na mjanja, ela asiphohumira kpwa mikono yakpwe, achili zakpwe tazindazalisha chitu. Hutumika kumkemea anayetegemea ujanja badala ya kazi ya uaminifu.
idiomatic_sw: Ujanja peke yake haukuletei riziki.
commentary_sw: Inaonya kwamba ujanja peke yake haiweki chakula mezani. Mtu anaweza kuwa mwerevu na mjanja, lakini asipofanya kazi kwa mikono yake, akili yake haitazalisha kitu. Hutumika kumkemea anayetegemea ujanja badala ya kazi ya uaminifu.

literal_en: A bad song is not used to soothe a baby
idiomatic_en: Bad influences should be kept away from the innocent.
commentary_en: Teaches that children must be shielded from harmful influences. A bad song — vulgar, violent, or corrupting — should never be used to soothe an infant. What a child absorbs in innocence shapes them for life. Used to warn against exposing children to adult conflicts, bad language, or immoral examples.
commentary_dg: Inafundisha kukala ana ni lazima arindwe dhidi ya ushawishi mubaya. Wira mui — wa matusi, jeuri, au uharibifu — taustahili kamwe kuhumirwa kumtuliza mwana. Chitu mwana anachophokea kpwa udide chakpwe chinamuumba maisha gosi. Hutumika kuonya dhidi ya kuika ana kpwa malongo ga avyere au mifano mibaya.
idiomatic_sw: Ushawishi mbaya unapaswa kuondolewa mbali na wasio na hatia.
commentary_sw: Inafundisha kwamba watoto lazima walindwe dhidi ya ushawishi mbaya. Wimbo mbaya — wa matusi, jeuri, au uharibifu — haupaswi kamwe kutumika kumtuliza mtoto. Kile mtoto anachokipokea katika ujinga wake kinamfinyanga maisha yote. Hutumika kuonya dhidi ya kuweka watoto katika migogoro ya watu wazima au mifano mibaya.

digo: Wivu n'chironda uchishiriki undaonda 384.Yezira chakpwe ni ndezi Whoeve
literal_en: Jealousy is a sore, if you share in it you will waste away r disowns his own is a wild rat When leg i This thems a sho
idiomatic_en: Jealousy is destructive; if you let it consume you, you will waste away.
commentary_en: the rat is caught in a trap it gnaws off its n order to escape, thus disowning the leg. is the same with someone who cuts elves off from their family (even if just for rt time out of pique)
commentary_dg: Wivu ni chironda ambacho uchishiriki nacho chindakurya. Dza panya achigbwirwa ni muhego nkuuma mguuwe mwenye ili aepuke — hivyo ndivyo mutu anavyodzikahira na ayae.
idiomatic_sw: Wivu ni kitu kibaya; ukiuruhusu kukutawala utadhoofika.
commentary_sw: Panya akikamatwa mtegoni huuma mguu wake mwenyewe ili aepuke, hivyo kuukataa mguu huo. Ni vivyo hivyo na mtu anayejikata kutoka kwa familia yake, hata kwa muda mfupi tu kwa hasira.

digo: Yuya ni mmwenga, hara ni anji
literal_en: That is one (person), these are many
idiomatic_en: One child is not enough; many children provide security for the future.
swahili: Yule ni mmoja, hawa ni wengi
commentary_en: If you just have one child, people will ask questions, because in the future you may need help and this is better supplied by many children rather than just one. Justification for large families.
commentary_dg: Mwana mmwenga tu si wa kutosha, mana siku za mbere undahitaji msada wa ana anji. Ana anji ni usalama wa uvyere, na ndiyo sababu atu analonda nyumba kulu.
idiomatic_sw: Mtoto mmoja haitoshi; watoto wengi huleta usalama wa baadaye.
commentary_sw: Ukiwa na mtoto mmoja tu, watu watauliza maswali, kwa sababu siku zijazo unaweza kuhitaji msaada na huu hutolewa vizuri na watoto wengi kuliko mmoja tu. Huhalalisha familia kubwa.

literal_en: ‘It is burning! It is burning!’ means it is nearly cooked
idiomatic_en: When things seem to be going wrong, it may mean success is near.
commentary_en: An encouraging proverb drawn from cooking. When food starts to burn, it means it is nearly done — the most intense heat comes just before the dish is ready. Used to encourage someone going through the hardest part of a struggle, assuring them that success is imminent.
commentary_dg: Ndarira ya kutiya moyo iriyohalwa kula kupikia. Chakurya chinacho andza kuphya, maana yakpwe chinakaribia kuiva — moho mkali zaidi unakpwedza kabila tu sahani taidzakala tayari. Hutumika kumtiya moyo mutu anayepishira sehemu ngumu zaidi ya mapambano, kumhakikishiya kukala kufanikiwa kuko phephi.
idiomatic_sw: Mambo yanapooneekana kuharibika, inaweza maanisha mafanikio yako karibu.
commentary_sw: Methali ya kutia moyo iliyotolewa kutoka kupikia. Chakula kinapoanza kuungua, maana yake kinakaribia kuiva — joto kali zaidi linakuja kabla tu sahani haijawa tayari. Hutumika kumtia moyo mtu anayepitia sehemu ngumu zaidi ya mapambano, kumhakikishia kwamba mafanikio yako karibu.

idiomatic_en: Your habits and actions take root wherever you go — you cannot outrun their consequences.
commentary_en: Where you wander is where you plant — your actions take root wherever you go. Teaches that one's behaviour and choices follow them and take effect in whatever place they find themselves. Used to warn that you cannot escape the consequences of your habits.
commentary_dg: Phatu uphazulako ndipho uphaphandako — matendo gako ganashika mizizi phophosi uphiyako. Inafundisha kukala tabiya na marikulo ga mutu ganamfuata na kuhenda kpwa phatu phophosi anaphodziora. Hutumika kuonya kukala tauweza kukimbira matokeo ga tabiya zako.
idiomatic_sw: Mahali unapozurura ndipo unapopanda; matendo yako yanafuata nyayo zako.
commentary_sw: Mahali unapozurura ndipo unapopanda — matendo yako yanashika mizizi popote unapokwenda. Inafundisha kwamba tabia na uchaguzi wa mtu unamfuata na kutenda katika mahali popote anapojipatia. Hutumika kuonya kwamba huwezi kukimbia matokeo ya tabia zako.
